This option is used in cases where the person responsible for managing branch accounts changes, or there is a need to appoint a new manager for a specific branch, or to modify the powers associated with the accounts.

The establishment’s account is logged into the business insurance platform, then the “Edit Account Manager” service is selected, and the new data for the new manager is filled in, such as identity information, account number, and new administrator powers. After that, supporting documents such as a copy of the ID or commercial register are attached, and the amendment request is sent for review and approval by the insurance authority. Upon approval, the change is made and an immediate notification is sent confirming that the modification request has been implemented.

The service helps improve account management and facilitate employee and facility management processes in a flexible electronic manner, which contributes to organizing insurance relationships and reports in a more accurate and transparent manner.

The importance of the account manager modification service - business

Facilitating the management of accounts and securing the powers of officials in an effective and up-to-date manner.
Enabling establishments to easily update officials without the need for personal visits.
Protecting the legal and administrative rights of both managers and establishments.
Ensuring smooth operations and achieving efficiency in managing accounts and reports.
Supporting digital transformation and saving facility owners time and effort in modernization processes.

Difficulties and challenges related to the account manager amendment service - business

It is necessary to enter correct and accurate data to avoid rejection or return of the application.
Document requirements such as identity or commercial registration may pose a barrier in some cases.
Possible delay in approval if data is unclear or incomplete.
The need for continuous follow-up on the Insurance Authority platform to ensure that the process is completed quickly and smoothly.
The employer's responsibility is to ensure that the data is updated periodically and accurately to avoid violations or delays in benefiting from the service.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
ID photo.
Letter to the account manager.

services.name: Joint registration
services.shortDescription: This service allows subscribers (Saudis - non-Saudis) and partners to be registered with the employer.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
Joint Registration Service - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia through the My Insurance Aamal platform. It allows employers to register new employees (subscribers) on the establishment’s account in the social insurance system. The service aims to ensure that all employees are registered legally and properly in accordance with the organizing regulations, thus ensuring that employees benefit from insurance coverage and protect their rights in the social security system.

The service steps begin with the employer or the person authorized to enter the establishment’s account on the electronic social insurance platform, then choose the new subscriber registration service, and fill in the new employee’s data, including the name, ID number, nationality, contract type, subscription start date, and basic wage. Supporting documents such as a copy of identity and passport for non-Saudi workers can also be uploaded. After entering the data and ensuring its accuracy, the registration is approved and the subscriber’s status is activated in the system, allowing the follow-up of his subscription and other insurance entitlements.

The service provides an easy-to-use interface that allows employers to manage their employee records, add new subscribers, and perform data updates on an ongoing basis, which is necessary for the establishment to comply with official laws and requirements and to provide insurance for its employees.

The importance of the joint registration service - business

Ensuring that employees are legally registered to activate their insurance coverage.
Supporting workers’ rights by providing comprehensive social insurance.
Helping establishments comply with government regulations and reduce violations.
Enhancing transparency and improving the management of human resources and insurance data.
Facilitating periodic reporting and financial obligations of the facility.

Difficulties and challenges related to the joint registration service - business

It is necessary to enter accurate and correct data to avoid registration refusal or delay.
Handle employee data updates and contract amendments that may impact registration.
There are some complications in registering non-Saudi workers due to residency certificates and sponsorship.
Requirements to upload and verify documents may cause delays when registering.
The employer follows up on the registration status and updates the data to avoid fines and penalties.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
ID photo.
serviceConditions: The participant must be at least 18 years old.
Not to be a government employee.

services.name: Modify facility type - business
services.shortDescription: This service allows you to modify the facility type, as it can be changed between “main” and “subsidiary”.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The facility type modification service - Aamal is an electronic service available through the General Organization for Social Insurance platform in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It allows the facility owner to modify the facility type registered in the system between “main” or “subsidiary.” The type of facility is one of the basic data that determines how the facility is registered with the Authority, and it has a direct impact on managing reports, insurance subscriptions, and regulating the facility’s relationship with its branches.

The modification process is done by logging into the establishment’s account on the Business Insurance platform, choosing the establishment whose type you want to modify, then going to the basic establishment data section and choosing the option to modify the establishment type. An updated copy of the commercial registry or any official document proving the validity of the requested amendment must be uploaded. After submitting the application and reviewing it by the Authority, the change will be approved or additional documents will be requested as the case may be.

This service helps establishments update their official data to reflect the reality of their activity and actual distribution, including what helps in merging or separating branches, and provides greater flexibility in managing employee records and subscriptions to the Authority.

The importance of the facility type modification service

Ensuring that facility data is updated accurately in accordance with regulatory and commercial changes.
Enhancing the accuracy of insurance reports and adjusting subscriptions according to the real structure of the facility.
Enabling establishments to manage their branches and linking them to the parent establishment legally.
Supporting regulatory compliance by updating official records periodically and regularly.
Improving the facility's performance through flexibility in the administrative and organizational distribution of branches.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

The need to provide the required official documents such as an updated copy of the commercial register.
Postponing approval of the application in the event of a lack of data or a discrepancy in records.
The necessity of coordination between different branches and facilities to ensure compatibility of new data.
Monitoring updates and continuous communication with the relevant authorities to speed up processing.
Adherence to the technical instructions of the platform to ensure acceptance of the request.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: The facilities must be owned by one owner and can be transferred between them.
The commercial registry and labor office must have been transferred from subsidiary to main.

services.name: Modifying subscriber data - Business
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the facility owner to modify the facility’s subscriber data

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
Subscriber data modification service - Business is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, which allows business owners to update and modify the data of subscribers (employees) registered on the establishment’s account in the social insurance system. This data includes personal information such as name, nationality, ID or residence number, profession, salary, subscription start date, address, and other necessary data that affects the management of subscriptions and insurance reports.

The application is submitted by logging into the establishment’s account on the Business Insurance platform, selecting the subscriber whose data is requested to be amended, then updating the required information and attaching any supporting documents such as a copy of the identity card or the employment contract, depending on the type of amendment. After entering the data, the application is reviewed by the authority to ensure its accuracy and authenticity before it is approved and included in the system. This amendment allows employers to accurately update their employees’ records, which ensures the validity of the insurance accounts and the establishment’s obligations towards its employees.

This service is necessary to ensure establishments' continued compliance with insurance regulations and legal procedures, as well as to improve the quality of reports and reduce legal and financial errors.

The importance of the shared data modification service - Business

Ensuring that subscriber data is accurate and updated in accordance with actual changes in the business.
Improving the management of social insurance contributions and financial facility reports.
Avoid fines or violations resulting from incorrect or updated data.
Supporting subscribers’ rights by ensuring that correct and updated data is recorded.
Facilitating the facility's administrative operations and reducing time and effort in modifying data.

Difficulties and challenges related to the shared data modification service - Business

It is necessary to enter accurate and correct data to avoid rejection or delay of the application.
Dealing with updating data in cases of expatriate workers who may require additional documents.
Possible complexity in the event of changes to salary, profession, or subscription period.
It is the employer's responsibility to monitor updates and follow up with the Authority to ensure acceptance of the amendments.
Potential technical challenges in the platform that may hinder the speed of updating or servicing data.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.

services.name: Wages Update - Business
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the facility owner to modify the salaries and wages of subscribers

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
Wages Update Service - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia through the “My Insurance Aamal” platform. It allows business owners to update the salaries of their registered subscribers in the social insurance system accurately and quickly. These updates are made to ensure that participants' salaries reflect the actual agreed-upon wages, to facilitate the implementation of wage protection programs and to ensure employee rights.

The employer or delegate logs into the establishment’s account on the insurance platform, and then chooses the wage update service. Salaries can be modified either by amending individual participant data via the subscriber list, or by uploading an Excel file containing the new wage data for all employees, with the need to maintain the file format as specified by the Authority. The new basic salary must be entered and adjusted and the application submitted for review. After validating the data, the update will be approved and applied starting this month.

The service helps establishments comply with labor and social security laws, improve the accuracy of calculating contributions as well as support wage protection policies, ensuring that workers receive their appropriate financial rights.

The importance of the wages update service - Business

Ensure that salaries reflect the actual amount the participant receives, which enhances transparency.
Supporting the wage protection program and improving compliance with regulatory laws.
Facilitating updating insurance payments and monthly declaration reports.
Protecting the rights of subscribers and ensuring that they are properly entitled to social insurance.
Reducing violations and fines resulting from not updating wage conditions.

Challenges and difficulties related to the wage update service - Business

The need for high accuracy when entering payroll data to avoid rejection or delay.
Complications in updating the wages of non-Saudi workers due to residency procedures and associated certificates.
Dealing with requirements for uploading files correctly according to the required format to prevent technical errors.
Responsibility for continuous follow-up of wage adjustments in emergency cases or job transfers.
The necessity of providing technical support to users when they face difficulties in updating data.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
ID photo.

services.name: Change career - business
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the facility owner to change the professions of the facility’s subscribers

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The Career Change Service - Business is an electronic service provided by the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in integration with the General Organization for Social Insurance. It allows the facility owner to modify the professional title of the workers or subscribers registered with him on the platform to match the profession with the actual nature of work in the facility. The service aims to regulate the labor market, improve the compatibility of registered professions with professional reality, and enhance establishments’ compliance with labor regulations in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

Employers or their authorized representatives benefit from the service by logging into the Qiwi or Business Insurance platform, then going to the Occupation Management/Change Profession service and choosing the worker whose profession he wants to change. After that, the new profession is determined from the Unified Saudi Occupational Classification Guide, and the required documents are attached, such as a copy of the national ID or residence permit, and the appropriate academic qualification, if any.

Applications are reviewed electronically by the competent authorities to verify the compatibility of the new profession with the facility’s activity, and if approved, the profession is officially updated in the systems of the Ministry of Human Resources, Social Insurance, and Passports (for non-Saudis).

The service represents an important step to correct the conditions of workers, and supports the Kingdom’s Vision 2030 in raising the efficiency of the labor market and ensuring an organized and fair work environment.

The importance of career change service - business

Standardizing the worker’s job title in accordance with the actual nature of the work.
Correcting workers’ data in the Social Insurance and Ministry of Human Resources systems.
Contributing to achieving legal compliance for establishments and avoiding penalties.
Raising the efficiency of the labor market and ensuring fair employment among different sectors.
Help improve the reliability of government data on employment and employment.

Difficulties and challenges related to career change service - business
The new profession must be compatible with the facility’s officially registered activity.
Requiring the provision of appropriate academic qualifications for some technical and medical professions.
The need to obtain multiple approvals from official authorities such as passports or insurance.
Delay in accepting the application in the event of a lack of documents or the presence of conflicting data between the systems.
The employer has the legal responsibility to ensure the accuracy of the updated information.
requiredDocuments: ID photo.
A copy of the commercial register.

services.name: Initiative for exemption from delay fines and violations (business)
services.shortDescription: This service allows employers to be exempted from late fines and fines for violations in exchange for paying the amount of contributions due to the organization.​

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The Service for Exemption from Delay Fines and Violations - Aamal is an initiative launched by the General Organization for Social Insurance in cooperation with the Zakat, Tax and Customs Authority in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It aims to enable establishments to correct their regulatory conditions and be exempt from insurance or tax fines that resulted from delays in payment or committing violations. This initiative is one of the important steps to support the business sector in the Kingdom and reduce the financial and administrative burdens on establishment owners and taxpayers.

The initiative provides a 100% exemption from late fines and violations for all establishments that take the initiative to pay their due subscriptions or agree on an approved installment plan within the time period specified for the initiative, which extends until June 30, 2025 AD.

Business owners can apply to benefit from the initiative by logging into their account on the Business Insurance platform or through the Zakat, Tax and Customs Authority portal, choosing the “Exemption from Fines and Violations” service, filling out the application form and attaching the required documents. After submitting and reviewing the application, the competent authority issues a notification accepting or rejecting the exemption based on meeting the regulatory conditions and standards.

The initiative includes all insurance fines resulting from late payment, failure to register workers on time, or late submission of monthly statements or insurance reports. It also includes fines for late payment or tax registration and the relevant certificates issued by the Authority.

Objectives and importance of the initiative to exempt from fines and violations

Supporting establishments in correcting their financial and administrative conditions without bearing additional burdens.
Stimulating voluntary compliance with insurance, zakat and tax regulations.
Enhancing labor market stability and improving the economic environment by reducing the burdens on business owners.
Enabling small and medium enterprises to continue their business and improve their financial position.
Increasing transparency in transactions and reducing financial and administrative disputes.

Difficulties and challenges related to the initiative

It is necessary to pay the full subscriptions due or adhere to an approved installment plan to benefit from the exemption.
The application may be rejected if all regulatory conditions are not met or if the application is delayed before the end of the specified period.
The need to update facility and subscriber data before submitting to ensure acceptance of the application.
The limited duration of the initiative, which may require speedy decision-making and submission.
The facility's commitment to continuous follow-up until receiving the final acceptance notification.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: Debt arose before the decision was issued - paying subscriptions to benefit from the exemption.

services.name: Joint transfer between branches of the establishment - business
services.shortDescription: This service allows the subscriber to be transferred between the establishment's branches, which helps him maintain the continuity of his services effectively.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
Shared transfer service between facility branches - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It allows the facility owner or his delegates to transfer a participant (employee) from one branch to another within the same facility or from one branch to the main headquarters, while preserving all his previous insurance data and continuing his participation in the system without interruption.

The service aims to facilitate the management of employee affairs in establishments that have more than one branch under a unified establishment number, ensuring the organization of work and the continuity of workers’ insurance rights.

The process is carried out by logging into the establishment’s account on the Business Insurance platform, selecting the subscriber to be transferred from the list of active subscribers, then selecting the subscriber transfer option, selecting the new branch and entering the receiving establishment’s subscription number and the exact date of the transfer.

The request is then sent for electronic review and verification of the validity of the transfer data, and an immediate notification of success arrives if all data is complete. The transfer does not lead to the cessation of subscription or the loss of previous insurance periods, but rather the transfer creates complete continuity in the subscriber’s insurance file.

The importance of a shared transportation service between the establishment’s branches - Business

Facilitating the transfer of employees between branches without the need to terminate their services or register new.
Maintaining the continuity of the worker’s participation in the social insurance system.
Improving the efficiency of human resources management in multi-branch establishments.
Support regulatory compliance and preserve insurance rights without interruptions.
Reducing time and procedures by providing the service electronically without the need for paper procedures.

Difficulties and challenges related to the subscriber transfer service

It is necessary to enter correct data such as the transfer date and subscription number for the receiving branch.
The transfer may be rejected if the receiving branch's data is not up to date or inactive.
The need to follow up on the completion of the process by the responsible party to ensure that the transfer is officially approved.
Complexities in some special cases that include different activities or administrative distribution of branches.
The facility must bear responsibility for ensuring that the new subscriber’s wage conforms to the insurance system and the prescribed differences.
requiredDocuments: 1- A copy of the ID.
2- A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: 1- The establishments must be owned by one owner.
2- The worker must have been transferred to the labor office to be transferred with insurance.

services.name: Installment of an establishment’s debt - Business
services.shortDescription: This service allows the establishment's debt to be paid in installments if it reaches or exceeds 6,000 riyals, as it can pay the amounts due in regular installments, which contributes to alleviating financial pressure and facilitating repayment.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
Establishment debt installment service - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It allows establishments that have accumulated sums of money owed to social insurance to request installments of the debt in scheduled payments according to an approved plan. The service aims to support facility owners and reduce the financial burdens on them while ensuring continued commitment to paying insurance dues without financial or emergency obstacles.

This service is available if the debt exceeds 6,000 Saudi riyals, as the facility owner can submit an electronic request through the Business Insurance platform, and choose the debt installment request service. The application form is filled out, which includes the establishment’s data, subscription number, total debt, and a proposal for the appropriate installment plan, specifying the down payment amount and the number of payments. The applicant also attaches supporting documents such as bank account statements and a letter of guarantee or promissory note in some cases, to ensure the seriousness of the application and the integrity of the procedures.

After submitting the application, it is reviewed by the competent authority at the General Organization for Social Insurance to ensure that the conditions are met, then a decision is issued to approve or reject it, with an electronic notification stating the result of the application and the approved payment plan if approved.

This service is considered one of the supporting tools in enabling establishments to correct their situations and continue in the market without falling into violations or financial accumulations that affect their regulatory status.

The importance of the facility’s debt installment service - business

Facilitating the process of repaying enterprises’ accumulated debts in flexible installments.
Supporting financially distressed establishments to ensure they continue to fulfill their insurance obligations.
Reducing financial pressure on companies while maintaining their regulatory compliance.
Enabling small and medium economic sectors to correct their financial conditions.
Contributing to enhancing labor market stability and protecting the insurance system from defaults.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

The debt must exceed the specified minimum (6,000 riyals) for the application to be accepted.
The down payment must be paid within the specified period to activate the installment plan.
Possible cancellation of the order in the event of late payment or non-compliance with the terms of the agreement.
The need for careful review of financial details and guarantees before approval.
Holding the facility responsible for regularly following up on monthly payments and updating its data periodically.
requiredDocuments: 1- A certified debt installment letter on the establishment’s publications.
2- A copy of the facility’s debt.
3- A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: 1- To submit a request for debt installments, the amounts must be more than 6,000 Saudi riyals.
2- Pay the advance payment within the period allowed for payment. If the period is exceeded, the installment will be automatically cancelled.
3- Commitment to pay the installments, and in the event of failure to pay more than once, the installments will be cancelled.​​​
4- That there is no previously submitted installment request or that the facility has previously paid installments.

services.name: Change account manager
services.shortDescription: An electronic service provided by Social Insurance, which allows employers to easily change the branch account manager in the establishment’s account, which saves time and effort in managing the business more efficiently without the need to review the entity.​

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The Account Manager Change Service - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It allows business owners to amend the details of the branch account manager or replace him with a new manager through the Aamal Insurance platform.

The service aims to facilitate the management of establishment accounts, organize access and supervision powers, and ensure the continuity of insurance operations management easily and safely.

The facility owner or delegate can use the service by logging into the main account on the Taminaty Business platform, then selecting the desired facility and entering the option to manage facility supervisors. After that, select the current account manager, choose to change the administrator, then enter the new manager’s data such as name, ID number, mobile phone, and email, specifying his powers within the system. After reviewing the data, the application is submitted electronically, to be reviewed by the institution, and the change is approved immediately after verifying the validity of the data.

This service allows business owners the flexibility to manage their accounts without the need to visit the organization's branches, which ensures the smooth continuity of the organization's organizational and administrative services.

The importance of the service of changing an account manager - business

Enabling establishments to update their officials’ data immediately and easily.
Enhancing digital security by formally transferring powers.
Ensuring continuity of services and not disrupting administrative processes when managers change.
Reducing the need for field visits to insurance offices.
Improving the efficiency of insurance operations management within establishments with multiple branches.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service of changing an account manager - business
It is necessary to enter all data accurately and in accordance with government regulations.
There may be a delay in approving the application due to a lack of documents or incorrect data.
The need to periodically review the powers granted to avoid errors or misuse.
The importance of ensuring that the new manager is legally linked to the facility before adopting the change.
Dealing with some technical complications when transferring powers between accounts.

services.name: Modifying the establishment’s contact information - Business
services.shortDescription: This service allows you to modify the establishment's contact information.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The establishment’s contact data modification service – Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia through the My Insurance Business platform, and enables establishment owners or delegates to update and amend the establishment’s contact information such as phone number, email, and national address immediately and easily. This service aims to ensure that the facility's official contact information remains up-to-date and accurate to facilitate administrative and technical communication between it and the organization.

The service is one of the basic procedures that allows establishments to maintain the continuity of electronic services and receive notifications and alerts related to insurance subscriptions or transactions.

The modification process is done by logging into the establishment’s account on the Business Insurance platform, then entering the establishment’s file and choosing the contact information tab. Then you can update the required information: mobile number, email, or national address, and click on the update data option to complete the process.

The service does not require the submission of additional documents or approvals, and updates are reviewed immediately to ensure speed of implementation and accuracy of approval.

The importance of the facility’s contact information modification service – Business

Ensures continuity of effective communication between the facility and the General Organization for Social Insurance.
Protecting the facility from losing official alerts or delays due to outdated data.
Enhancing the accuracy of the facility’s official data in government systems.
Simplifying the management of electronic accounts and updating them independently without the need for field review.
Improving the speed of completion of electronic services based on the correctness of contact information.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

It is necessary to enter new data accurately to avoid communication interruptions.
Activation may be delayed if there are errors in linking with the national address (Saudi Postal Register).
The establishment's responsibility is to update its data as soon as changes occur to avoid fines or disruption of services.
Rare technical challenges in the platform lead to changes not being recorded immediately.
The need to follow up on alerts after modification to ensure that the updated data is activated successfully.
requiredDocuments: National address.
The facility's bank IBAN.
A copy of the commercial register.

services.name: Facility Supervisors Management - Business
services.shortDescription: This service allows the employer to appoint supervisors and manage their powers easily through the establishment’s account in My Business Insurance.​

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
Facility Supervisors Management Service - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance via the My Insurance Business platform. It allows the facility owner to appoint supervisors on the facility’s account and manage their powers directly and easily without the need to visit the organization’s branches. The service aims to enable facilities to organize their administrative and insurance work, while specifying levels of access and tasks for each supervisor in proportion to the size of the facility and its competencies.

The facility owner or delegate can log in to the facility account on the platform, and then choose the facility supervisor management service from the list of services. Then the following actions can be performed:

Adding a new supervisor: The supervisor’s data is entered, such as name, national ID or residence number, mobile number, and email.
Defining powers: This includes the authority of the overall facility supervisor, or sub-authorities such as the “Registration Supervisor” responsible for registering and excluding subscribers, the “Subscriptions Supervisor” for following up on invoices, and the “Occupational Hazards Supervisor” responsible for reporting and following up on work injuries.
Modifying or deleting a supervisor: You can modify data, change permissions, or cancel supervision when needed.

This service contributes to ensuring that facility operations are managed in an organized and safe manner. It also enables facilities with multiple branches to delegate specific supervisors in each branch to manage daily tasks without conflicts of authority or accounts.

The importance of facility supervisor management service – Business

Facilitating the management of powers and the distribution of tasks electronically without the need for paper follow-up.
Strengthening electronic governance in the management of multi-branch establishments.
Raising the level of security in digital transactions by specifying precise permissions for each user.
Supporting business continuity in the absence of the manager or during the change of administrative tasks.
Improving the efficiency of administrative operation and reducing errors resulting from poor coordination.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

It is necessary to review supervisors’ data periodically to ensure that the powers are consistent with their actual tasks.
It is the responsibility of the facility owner to specify precise powers to avoid misuse.
Possible delay in activating or deleting supervisors in the event of a technical glitch.
The need to follow up on alerts to ensure that changes are implemented officially and approved.
Sufficient knowledge of the system to manage powers in a correct and effective manner.
requiredDocuments: ID photo.
A copy of the commercial register.

services.name: Issuing an establishment certificate (commitment)
services.shortDescription: A service through which the employer can print a certificate of commitment for social insurance

serviceDetails.serviceDescription: A service through which the employer can print a certificate of commitment for social insurance.

services.name: Transfer all subscribers - business
services.shortDescription: This service allows the transfer of all registered subscribers between the facility’s branches.​

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The transfer service for all subscribers - Aamal is an integrated electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia through the My Insurance Business platform. It enables establishment owners to transfer all employees participating in social insurance from a specific branch to another branch of the same establishment or to the main headquarters, in a reliable and fast manner without the need to submit individual requests for each subscriber.

The service aims to facilitate the process of updating data in multi-branch establishments and save time and effort for business owners, especially in cases of administrative merger or closure of a branch.

The transfer process takes place through the facility owner or delegate logging into the facility’s account on the Taminaty Business platform, then entering the subscriber management tab, and choosing the transfer service for all subscribers. After specifying the source branch and the receiving branch, the system displays a list of registered subscribers to be approved for transfer in one go.

Before carrying out the process, it is required that the commercial records and business licenses for both branches be valid, and that the establishment does not have any violations or late subscriptions that may hinder the completion of the procedure. After submitting the application, the process is carried out electronically immediately and a notification of success is sent via the email registered on the platform.

The importance of transportation service for all subscribers - Business

Facilitating the management process for owners of multi-branch establishments.
Reduce time by executing transfers collectively instead of individual orders.
Ensuring the continuity of workers’ records without losing their previous insurance subscription periods.
Supporting flexibility in restructuring facilities or merging different branches.
Reducing administrative errors associated with manually entering or modifying employee data.

Difficulties and challenges related to the transfer service for all subscribers

It is necessary to ensure that the data of the two branches concerned with the transfer is updated before implementing the process.
The application may be rejected if there are violations or unpaid subscriptions.
The need for careful verification of the validity of subscriber data to avoid problems after transfer.
The employer's legal responsibility to preserve the rights of participants during transportation.
The importance of reviewing platform notifications to ensure the success of the process and its final approval.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the ID of the subscribers to be transferred.
serviceConditions: The participants must have been transferred to the labor office.

services.name: Adding the subscription period retroactively - Business
services.shortDescription: This service allows the subscriber to add the subscription period retroactively, provided that the period required to register does not exceed twenty-four months from the date of submitting the application.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of adding the subscription period retroactively - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance through the My Insurance Business platform, and it allows employers to register previous work periods for subscribers that were not registered at the specified time, provided that the period required to be added does not exceed 24 months from the date of submitting the application. This service aims to correct the conditions of insurance contributions and ensure that all periods of actual service are counted in the subscribers’ record in the system.

The process is done by the establishment owner logging into his account on the My Insurance Business platform, then going to the subscriptions tab and choosing the option to add a subscription period retroactively. He then fills out the service form, including the subscriber’s data, the work period to be added (start and end dates), and the wage value during that period, attaching supporting documents such as employment contracts, payslips, or any documents proving the work relationship during the aforementioned period.

Acceptance depends on the conformity of the data provided with the rules and regulations, and after review, a decision is issued to accept or reject the application, along with a statement of the reasons. In the event of acceptance, the period will be added directly to the subscriber's record and the subscription fees due will be calculated retroactively.

The importance of the service of adding the subscription period retroactively - Business

Correcting insurance records and ensuring that all actual service periods for subscribers are included.
Preserving workers’ rights to benefit from full pensions and insurance benefits.
Providing establishment owners with the opportunity to address previous errors in registering workers.
Supporting the regulatory compliance of establishments and achieving justice in calculating contributions.
Ensuring continuity in coverage of contributions in accordance with social insurance laws.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service of adding the subscription period retroactively

Commitment to the statutory period (24 months), which may not be exceeded under any circumstances.
Reliable official documents must be submitted to prove the business relationship within the period required to be added.
The application may be rejected if there are discrepancies between the establishment’s data and the subscriber’s records.
It is necessary to pay the due fees retroactively to activate the addition and approval.
The need for careful review of data by the institution to ensure transparency and accuracy.
requiredDocuments: Attach the employment contract.
Attach payroll.
Attach medical insurance.
Attach attendance record.
Attach a document for the end of the work relationship (in the event of leaving work).
A copy of the commercial register.

services.name: Report a work injury - Business
services.shortDescription: This service makes it possible to report a work injury to a subscriber. The report can be directed to the authority responsible for specialized treatment or to the relevant insurance office.​

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The Work Injury Reporting Service - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance through the My Insurance Business platform, which allows establishment owners to report cases of work injuries to which participants are exposed while performing their job duties or due to the nature of the work. The service aims to enable employers to officially register injuries in the insurance system to guarantee the rights of the injured person and maintain the facility’s compliance with social insurance systems.

The regulations of the Occupational Hazards Branch in the insurance system obligate employers to inform the competent institution within three days from the date of learning of the injury, for any injury for which first aid is not sufficient.

The reporting process is done electronically by logging into the establishment’s account on the Business Insurance platform, then choosing “Report Occupational Hazards” and filling out the reporting form by entering the subscriber’s data such as the ID or residence number, date and location of the injury, type of injury, and circumstances of the accident.

The necessary documents are also uploaded, such as a medical report from the treatment authority or a copy of the accident report, if any. After submitting the application, the employer receives a notification number to follow up on the injury case until it is approved.

The service comes as an important step to protect the insurance rights of workers, as it ensures that they receive the necessary medical care and compensation for periods of treatment or disability, with the employer’s commitment to regulatory procedures and reporting within the specified period.

The importance of the work injury reporting service - Business

Ensuring the registration of occupational accidents and preserving the insurance rights of subscribers.
Facilitating legal procedures related to claims and compensation.
Enabling the organization to follow up on cases of occupational injuries and manage compensation efficiently.
Enhancing the safety of the work environment through occupational risk management.
Reducing penalties and fines resulting from late or abstaining from reporting.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

It is the employer's responsibility to report within the specified period, which is only three days from the date of learning of the injury.
The report may be rejected in the event of a lack of medical documents or accident data.
Difficulty determining whether an injury is a “work injury” or a personal health symptom in some cases.
The need for precise coordination between the facility and the treatment body to issue the medical report.
It is necessary to update subscribers’ data periodically to ensure the validity and accuracy of the report.
requiredDocuments: Medical report on the injury.
Identity of the worker who was injured.
A copy of the commercial register.

services.name: Issuing an occupational safety and health certificate - business
services.shortDescription: This service allows the employer to issue an occupational safety and health certificate, which reflects compliance with safety and health standards in the facility. In addition, the certificate provides specific details regarding the facility's records in terms of the number of injuries and the outcome of each injury, whether the result is recovery without disability, recovery with disability, or even occupational death, which reflects the facility's commitment to providing a safe and healthy work environment for workers.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
Occupational safety and health certificate issuance service - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance in cooperation with the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development. It allows establishments to issue an official certificate confirming their commitment to implementing approved occupational safety and health systems and standards in the work environment within the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

This certificate is documented evidence of the facility’s compliance with regulations for protecting workers from occupational hazards and promoting a safe work environment. It is one of the basic requirements for compliance with the work system and regulations associated with the Occupational Hazards Branch.

The process of issuing the certificate takes place electronically via the Taminaty Business platform, where the facility owner or authorized person logs into the facility’s official account and chooses the service of issuing an occupational safety and health certificate.
After verifying the status of the facility and ensuring that there are no violations or late dues, the system issues the certificate digitally immediately, and it includes details such as the name of the facility, subscription number, date of issuance and expiration, and the scope of commitment to occupational safety.

You can also download a digital copy of the certificate in PDF format for use in official transactions, government contracts, or submission to tenders.

The service aims to encourage establishments to implement occupational safety standards and activate accident prevention programs, in line with the objectives of the Kingdom’s Vision 2030 to enhance the quality of life and improve the work environment.

The importance of the service of issuing an occupational safety and health certificate - Business

Proof of the facility’s commitment to implementing approved occupational safety and health regulations and systems.
Enhancing confidence between establishments, government agencies, and investors by demonstrating professional compliance.
Enabling establishments to obtain government contracts and tenders that require the submission of a safety certificate.
Contributing to reducing the rates of work injuries and occupational accidents.
Improving the facility’s image and reputation as a safe workplace compatible with national regulations.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

It is necessary to adhere to safety requirements in accordance with the regulations of the Ministry of Human Resources to qualify for issuance of the certificate.
The application may be rejected if there are safety violations or failure to update facility data.
The need for periodic follow-up to renew the certificate and ensure continued compliance.
The establishment's responsibility is to implement field controls actually and not just issue the certificate electronically.
The importance of having specialized cadres in occupational safety and health to obtain the certificate and activate it internally.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.

services.name: Disabling the facility admin account - Business
services.shortDescription: This service allows the facility supervisor to immediately disable all of his powers at the facility.​

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The facility supervisor account deactivation service - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance via the Business Insurance platform, which allows the facility owner or their authorized delegates to disable the facility supervisor account and cancel all his administrative powers over the account immediately. This service aims to enhance digital security and ensure the protection of facility data in the event of the supervisor’s absence or the end of his association with the facility for any reason, such as moving to another entity, retirement, or the end of the contractual relationship.

The service enables the facility owner to stop the supervisor’s ability to enter the system or make any modification to the facility or subscriber data. The procedure is carried out by logging into the facility’s account on the Business Insurance platform, then choosing the service of disabling the supervisor’s account from the (Manage facility supervisors) list. After identifying the administrator whose account is to be disabled, the system confirms the process and issues an immediate notification deactivating all his administrative powers.

It is also possible to download an official notification from the system proving the deactivation process for use in official correspondence when needed.

The service aims to raise the level of protection against technical and administrative risks and ensure that powers are restricted exclusively to the employer or his official representative, which contributes to strengthening establishments’ confidence in government digital systems.

The importance of the facility admin account deactivation service - Business

Maintaining the confidentiality and security of facility information and protecting its data from unauthorized access.
Terminating the supervisor’s powers immediately upon leaving his job or transferring him to another organization.
Strengthening internal control and preventing abuse of powers within the system.
Ensuring the continuity of the facility’s operation without administrative or security disruption.
Providing a safe and fast electronic system to disable sensitive accounts.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service
It is necessary to implement the disablement immediately when the supervisor connection ends to avoid security problems.
Possible loss of some unsaved data if running tasks are not reviewed before disabling.
The need to appoint an alternative supervisor to follow up on operations within the account to avoid disruption of services.
Verify the identity of the person authorized to carry out the deactivation process before approving the request.
Be aware of the full powers granted to the supervisor before deleting them to ensure organizational compliance
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
ID photo.
serviceConditions: Select the admin whose account you want to disable.

services.name: Request for compensation for treatment costs - Business
services.shortDescription: This service allows the employer to claim compensation for treatment costs for a participant who has suffered a work-related injury

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The treatment costs compensation request service - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia via the Aamal Insurance platform, which allows the facility owner or the subscriber himself (upon authorization) to claim financial compensation for the medical expenses that were paid as a result of a work injury registered and approved in the insurance system. This service aims to ensure the recovery of medical costs borne by employers or subscribers when providing treatment in emergency cases or outside the Foundation’s accredited hospitals.

The service is available after the occupational injury is officially registered and approved within the cases covered by the Occupational Hazards Branch in the insurance system. The procedure can be implemented by logging in to the facility owner or beneficiary’s account on the My Insurance Business platform and choosing the service for requesting compensation for treatment costs from the list of occupational injury services.

Then, the injured participant is searched for by entering the ID or residence number, selecting the previously registered injury, and attaching medical bills and supporting documents such as treatment reports, medical diagnosis, and electronic payment receipts.
After submitting the application and verifying the documents, the institution reviews and approves it, then compensates the amounts due via bank transfer to the account registered with the institution within a specified period.

The importance of the service of requesting compensation for treatment costs - Business

Enabling the worker or facility to recover the amounts spent on treating occupational injuries.
Ensuring that the injured person receives medical care without delay or additional financial burdens.
Supporting employers in adhering to the insurance system and implementing occupational safety procedures.
Achieving justice and enhancing confidence in the social insurance system through fair compensation.
Improving the quality of health care provided to workers in all work environments.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

It is necessary to have a work injury previously entered and approved in the system before submitting a compensation request.
The application may be rejected in the event of a lack of medical documents or unapproved invoices.
The compensation ceiling is determined based on the regulations of the Occupational Hazards Branch and not on the entire medical bill.
The need to verify that invoices are issued by recognized medical authorities within the Kingdom.
Long medical review and reconciliation period in some complex or critical cases.
requiredDocuments: 1- A copy of the ID.
2- A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: The participant has a previously entered and approved work injury.​

services.name: Mass Casualty Reporting - Business
services.shortDescription: This service allows the facility to report mass casualties that occur to subscribers through a single transaction.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The Mass Injury Reporting Service - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance in cooperation with the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development via the Aamal Insurance platform. This service allows the facility owner or his delegates to report mass incidents in the workplace that resulted in the injury of more than one worker at the same time as a result of common occupational conditions or accidents, such as collapses, fires, or major field accidents.

The service aims to facilitate the documentation of these cases in the insurance system very quickly, to ensure that the injured receive immediate treatment and regular compensation in accordance with the regulations of the Occupational Hazards Branch in the Social Insurance System.

The service is used when more than one injury occurs in one facility or its affiliated work site. The employer logs into his account on the Business Insurance platform, then chooses the mass injury reporting service under the “Occupational Hazards” section.

Details of the mass accident are entered, such as the date and location of the accident, the number of injured, and the type of accident, then initial medical reports or reports from civil defense or concerned safety authorities are submitted. The system then links each infected person to his insurance number registered with the organization and issues follow-up notifications for each individual case of infection.
Reporting mass incidents is legally binding, and the facility must do so within three days from the date of learning of the incident, to avoid fines or delay benefits for the injured.

The importance of the mass casualty reporting service - Business

Accelerating the process of documenting occupational injuries involving several employees in the same accident.
Ensuring that injured people receive immediate treatment within the approved medical system.
Supporting transparency in recording occupational accidents and analyzing their causes.
Facilitating follow-up and compensation procedures for all injured parties.
Contributing to the development of occupational safety procedures and improving the work environment.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

It is necessary to prepare official reports from government agencies (such as civil defense and security agencies).
The need for high accuracy in entering injured persons’ data and linking it to their insurance subscription number.
There may be a delay in processing the report in the event of a lack of documents or multiple entities covered by the investigation.
It is mandatory to adhere to the statutory reporting period to avoid penalties.
The facility's responsibility to follow up on the cases of injured people until the end of treatment or the issuance of a compensation decision.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
ID photo.

services.name: Amending the facility’s bank account data - Business
services.shortDescription: This service allows you to modify the facility's bank account information.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The facility’s bank account data modification service - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance via the Business Insurance platform, which allows facility owners or delegates to modify or update the facility’s approved bank account data (IBAN number) used in payment transactions or receiving financial dues. The service aims to ensure the accuracy of banking data and the integrity of the flow of financial transactions between the facility and the institution.

This service is one of the vital services that enables establishments to manage their financial affairs with the institution, such as recovering amounts or settling debts and monthly transfers.

The request to amend the bank account is easily implemented by logging into the establishment’s account on the Business Insurance platform and choosing the bank account data amendment service, then entering the new IBAN number and uploading a copy of the bank letter or the official bank statement issued in the name of the establishment (the account is required to be in the name of the establishment registered with the insurance). After sending the request, the validity of the data and attachments is verified, and the amendment is approved within two working days.

The service aims to achieve flexibility in updating banking information and enhance the accuracy of electronic transactions in accordance with financial security requirements in the organization’s systems.

The importance of the service of amending an organization’s bank account data - Business

Ensuring that the facility’s payment and financial transfer data is updated securely and reliably.
Facilitating the process of paying financial contributions and receiving receivables without delay.
Reduce errors resulting from old or inactive bank accounts.
Supporting digital transformation and enhancing confidence in electronic financial transactions.
Protecting the facility’s financial rights and ensuring that the amounts arrive in the correct account.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

The bank account must be in the name of the facility itself to avoid rejection of the application.
It is mandatory to attach a bank letter or official statement stating the new IBAN number.
Possibility of delaying adoption of the amendment if documents are unclear or unofficial.
The establishment's responsibility is to update the data immediately after changing the account to avoid delaying transfers.
The requirement that the account be free of any disputes or financial violations before implementing the amendment.
requiredDocuments: IBAN of the bank account to be amended.
A copy of the commercial register.

services.name: Modifying the facility’s mailing address data - Business
services.shortDescription: This service allows you to modify the establishment's mailing address data.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The facility’s postal address data modification service - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance via the My Insurance Business platform, which allows employers or their authorized representatives to modify the facility’s approved postal address (national address) data. The service aims to ensure that the facility's location registered in the organization's database is updated, allowing accurate administrative communication and the arrival of official correspondence and notices to the correct address.

The service is characterized by being immediate and free, and is done by logging into the establishment’s account on the business insurance platform, then entering the establishment’s file and choosing the basic establishment data section. When clicking on the pen icon next to the address, the user can update postal data such as building number, zip code, street name, neighborhood name, city, and region.

After entering the new data, you can, if necessary, upload a document proving your national address (as proof from Saudi Post - Wasel). Once you click on approve the request, the data is updated immediately, and the user receives notification of the success of the amendment via text messages and e-mail.

This service is considered an essential service to ensure the compatibility of establishment data with the unified national address system in the Kingdom and to enhance electronic communication between government agencies and private establishments.

The importance of the service of amending the establishment’s mailing address data – Business

Ensuring the accuracy and speed of receipt of official correspondence and decisions issued by insurance.
Enhancing the reliability of facility data with government agencies and Saudi Post.
Facilitating the management of electronic services that rely on the national address for verification.
Supporting effective communication between the organization, business owners and legal representatives.
Achieving integration with facilities management systems and national addresses approved in the Kingdom.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

It is necessary to ensure that the national address data is correct before adopting it on the platform.
The approval process may be delayed if information is entered that is not compatible with the national postal data.
It is the responsibility of the facility owner to update the address data upon any actual change in the work location.
The importance of keeping a proof of address document to avoid future rejection of the application in official transactions.
The need to follow up on the update success notification to ensure that the modification is finally approved in the system.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
The new national address of the facility.

services.name: Modifying facility identification data - Business
services.shortDescription: This service allows you to modify the facility's identification data.​

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The facility identification data modification service - Aamal is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance via the My Insurance Business platform, which enables business owners to update and modify the basic identification data of the facility registered in the system, including the trade name, economic activity, commercial registration number, national address, and type of legal entity. This service aims to ensure the accuracy of establishment data in the organization’s records in accordance with official updates in commercial records or government licenses.

The facility owner or delegate can modify the identification data by logging in to the Business Insurance platform, then entering the facility file and choosing the facility identification data modification service. The user then updates the required fields, such as the establishment name, activity, or commercial registration number, and attaches the necessary documents such as a copy of the updated commercial registration or regulatory license.

The system then reviews the application electronically to ensure that the data matches the relevant authorities such as the Ministry of Commerce and the General Authority for Small and Medium Enterprises, and the update is approved immediately if all requirements are met.

This service is considered one of the basic services that maintains the transparency of insurance records and contributes to improving the quality of data related to facility owners and their workers.

The importance of the facility identification data modification service - Business

Ensuring the accuracy of basic data for establishments in the social insurance system.
Facilitating communication between the facility and the General Insurance Corporation when implementing financial and administrative services.
Contributing to raising the efficiency of electronic connectivity between insurance and government agencies.
Maintaining the facility’s compliance with official regulations and avoiding violations that may result from out-of-date data.
Facilitating verification procedures for establishments in official transactions and government contracts.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

It is necessary to submit updated official documents to prove the changes, such as the commercial register or approved license.
Possible delay in approval if there is a discrepancy in data between different government agencies.
It is the responsibility of the facility owner to update the data immediately after any official change occurs.
It is necessary to ensure that the establishment’s data matches the data at the Ministry of Commerce and SPL to ensure acceptance.
The need to follow up on application approval or rejection notifications to ensure the process is completed successfully.
requiredDocuments: Attach the commercial registration/license.

services.name: Complete the data of a registered non-Saudi subscriber automatically
services.shortDescription: A service through which the employer can automatically complete the data of the registered non-Saudi employee

serviceDetails.serviceDescription: A service through which the employer can automatically complete the data of the registered non-Saudi employee.

services.name: File a complaint/inquiry
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the employer to file a complaint for any problem within the General Insurance Organization.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The Service for Raising a Complaint or Inquiry - Business is an electronic service provided by the General Organization for Social Insurance through the My Insurance Business platform, which allows business owners and their authorized representatives to submit complaints, reports, or official inquiries related to electronic services, subscriptions, or insurance transactions.
The service aims to facilitate communication between facility owners and the organization, raise the level of customer satisfaction by quickly responding to their comments and inquiries, and resolving complaints in an organized and transparent manner in accordance with approved regulations.

The facility owner can access the Business Insurance platform through the national unified access (Absher Business), and from the services list, choose to file a complaint or inquiry.

Then the type of request is selected from the list (complaint - inquiry - suggestion - report) and the service form is filled out by entering the facility data, subscriber number or insurance subscription number, and an accurate description of the request, with the possibility of attaching supporting documents such as notices, invoices, or previous correspondence.

After sending the request, the user receives an automated follow-up number that can be used later to inquire about the status of the request, and will be responded to within a period not exceeding five working days via email or the digital platform.
The service is available to all establishments officially registered with Social Insurance, whether small, medium or large.

The importance of filing a complaint or inquiry service - Business

Providing a unified and secure channel for electronic communication with the General Organization for Social Insurance.
Enabling facility owners to follow up on their issues and inquiries without the need to visit offices.
Raising the level of transparency in government procedures and enhancing confidence in digital services.
Ensuring that complaints are addressed within specific periods of time in accordance with approved quality standards.
Facilitating obtaining technical support or regulatory clarifications directly from specialists.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

It is necessary to enter all application data accurately to speed up the processing process.
Possibility of delayed response if unclear or incomplete documents are attached.
You must use official channels and not file a complaint through unapproved means (mail or phone only).
It is the facility's responsibility to follow up on the application number periodically until it is officially closed.
The importance of adhering to the official language in describing the request and defining the type of problem clearly and professionally.

services.name: Registering a commercial registry for a sole proprietorship
services.shortDescription: This service enables the investor to start practicing commercial activity, and through it the following is done: Issuing a main or subsidiary commercial register for an institution.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
A commercial registry registration service for an individual institution that grants a legal license to practice commercial activity in the name of one person in the Saudi market, so that the institution’s registry becomes the basis for all commercial and administrative transactions before official authorities, partners, and customers. The service is provided entirely electronically through the Ministry of Commerce’s platform, which provides an easy and quick experience in establishing the facility without the need to visit any government agency.

The procedures begin by choosing the business activity, entering the information of the establishment owner, the trade name, whether reserved or new, specifying the capital and filling out the register data, attaching a copy of the national ID and the national address of the establishment. The data is reviewed immediately by the system and the commercial registration certificate is issued online after paying the fees, so that the establishment owner obtains a printable electronic certificate that can be used before official authorities, banks, customs, and any other party.

The commercial registry registration service for an individual institution also allows adding or deleting activities and registering branches in the future, in addition to the possibility of linking the registry to other government agencies (such as the Ministry of Human Resources, Zakat, Tax and Customs).

The importance of registering a commercial registry for an individual institution

Legal recognition of the institution in all contracts, obligations and commercial transactions.
Opening bank accounts in the organization’s name and linking them directly to the commercial activity.
Facilitating the issuance of other government subscriptions such as municipal and tax licenses.
Protecting the trade name and officially reserving it for the holder of record in the market.
The possibility of contracting with government agencies or entering official tenders and competitions.
Benefit from the services of various digital entities such as the Qiwa platform, the municipality, insurance, and others.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing a commercial registry for a sole proprietorship

It is necessary to carefully select and classify commercial activities so that the application is not rejected or suspended.
Sometimes difficulties arise in reserving a trade name, especially with common or irregular names.
All owner and national address data must be updated and consistent with the data of other official authorities.
Some activities require additional approvals or licenses (such as support for defense or communications systems), which may delay release.
The application may be suspended if there are violations or suspensions in the owner’s civil registry.
It is necessary to renew the commercial register annually and constantly update the organization’s data to maintain the validity of the register and its benefits.
requiredDocuments: A photo of the national ID.
National address.
serviceConditions: The age must not be less than 18 years.
Not be a government employee.
The capital must not be less than 5,000 SAR.
The main record must be current if the version is for a sub-record.

Government fees: 1,200 SAR for the commercial registry.
500 SAR publishing fee 
15% value added tax.
