- The establishment has an investment license valid for a period of no less than (90) days.
- The activities added in the application must be compatible with the company’s investment license.

6- Verify the following if it is a joint stock/simplified joint stock company:
- The presence of the assembly minutes.
- Submitting a letter requesting amendment to the company’s publications certified by the Chamber of Commerce.
- The members of the Board of Directors must not be government employees.

services.name: Extending the reservation of a trade name
services.shortDescription: This service enables the investor to extend the reservation of a trade name before the reservation period expires.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of extending the reservation of a trade name is a regular electronic procedure that allows the applicant or investor to extend the period of pre-reservation of a trade name that he had previously reserved, before its expiration, to ensure the continuity of the exclusive right to use it until the steps of establishing the establishment are completed or the commercial register is issued. This service is important for investors and entrepreneurs who need more time to arrange their regulatory procedures or prepare their business before finally registering the name in the commercial registry.

It is required that the original period of reservation has not yet expired, and that the name adhere to all regulatory controls for trade names, such as not being similar to other reserved or registered names, and not containing prohibited phrases or violating public order. After confirming the request and paying the prescribed fees, the reservation will be extended for an additional period, usually up to 60 days, and in some cases the extension can be repeated within the permitted conditions.

The importance of the service of extending a trade name reservation

Ensuring continuity of ownership of the trade name and not losing it due to the expiration of the reservation period.
Giving the investor additional time to complete incorporation procedures or develop a business plan before final registration.
Preventing the use of the name by another party during the preparation period, which preserves the identity of the future business.
Facilitating financial and administrative planning by securing the trade name during the preparation stage.
Enhance reliability when negotiating with partners or investors by having an officially reserved and approved name.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing an extension to reserve a trade name

It is necessary to submit an extension request before the reservation expires to avoid losing the name completely.
Full compliance with the regulatory controls for trade names, as extension will be refused if the name is found to violate those controls.
Possibility of rejection if there is a legal or commercial objection to the trade name from another party.
The prescribed fees must be paid and the electronic payment process must be completed successfully in order to complete the extension.
Potential technical problems on the electronic platform may delay order execution, especially during times of stress or maintenance.
It is not possible to extend after exceeding the permissible time limit or after canceling the reservation for any legal reason.
requiredDocuments: There is an existing trade name reservation.
The applicant must be the beneficiary of the trade name or authorized by him with a valid authorization.
serviceConditions: There is an existing trade name reservation.
The applicant must be the beneficiary of the trade name or authorized by him with a valid authorization.

services.name: Cancellation of a reserved trade name
services.shortDescription: This service enables the investor to cancel a reserved trade name.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of canceling a reserved trade name is a regular electronic procedure that allows the applicant or investor to withdraw from the prior reservation of a trade name before its expiration, and thus release it from the records of the Ministry of Commerce to become available for use or reservation by other parties. This service is necessary if the investor wishes to change his business plan or choose another name that better suits his activity or marketing strategy, or if the reserved name is no longer suitable from a regulatory or marketing standpoint.

The procedure often does not require additional fees, especially if the cancellation takes place before the commercial register is issued and the name is linked to it. After confirmation of cancellation, the status of the name in the system changes to become available for booking again by other users.

The investor or business owner may resort to using this service if he finds that the trade name is very similar to existing trademarks, or if he decides to change the approved activity, or if there is a legal or commercial objection to the name, or simply if he prefers to launch his project with a new name that is more compatible with his marketing plan.

The importance of the service of canceling a reserved trade name

Providing the investor the freedom to change his plan and obtain a new name that is more appropriate for the activity.
Edit the system's trade name to make it available for reservation by other users.
Avoid confusion or legal conflict with similar names or trademarks.
Reducing the investor's obligations if he decides to cancel or postpone the project.
Simplifying future procedures in the event of activity modification or project re-planning.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing the cancellation of a reserved trade name

It is not possible to recover the name after cancellation if it was quickly reserved by another party.
Loss of the remaining reservation period, as it cannot be recovered or the fees paid for the original reservation can be refunded in most cases.
It is necessary to ensure that the reserved name is not linked to an active commercial register. In this case, the register must be deleted first before cancellation.
Possible technical problems on the platform, especially during times of stress or during updates.
Rushing to cancel before the decision is made, which may lead to disappointment if you later want to restore the name and it is no longer available.
requiredDocuments: There is an existing trade name reservation.
The applicant must be the beneficiary of the trade name or authorized by him with a valid authorization.
serviceConditions: There is an existing trade name reservation.
The applicant must be the beneficiary of the trade name or authorized by him with a valid authorization.

services.name: Annual confirmation of a company's main commercial registry data

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The annual confirmation service for a company’s main commercial registry data is a basic regulatory procedure imposed by the Ministry of Commerce to ensure that companies’ main commercial registry data are up-to-date and consistent with their operational and administrative reality. The goal of this service is to enhance transparency and accuracy in Saudi market data.

This service allows company officials to review the company's main commercial registry data, such as: company name, primary business activity, national address, names of partners and managers, capital, and associated membership and license numbers. After reviewing this data, the user either confirms it as is if it has not changed, or updates it before sending confirmation. After completing the step, the company owner will receive proof that his master record data has been confirmed for the new year.

The importance of the annual confirmation service for a company’s main commercial registry data

Ensuring the accuracy of registered company data and linking it to actual reality to support compliance with regulations.
Avoid fines or suspension of the master record when the scheduled grace period expires without confirmation.
Enhancing the confidence of customers, partners and banks by having an updated and officially approved main commercial registry.
Seamless connectivity with other government agencies such as the Zakat, Taxes and Customs Authority, and the Ministry of Human Resources, which facilitates the completion of regulatory procedures.
Enabling the company to enter into government tenders and contracts that require an effective and confirmed master record.
Improving the level of transparency in the commercial and investment environment, which reflects the company’s seriousness and organization.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing the annual confirmation of the main commercial registry data for a company

It is necessary to carefully verify all data before confirming, as any error or inconsistency may lead to the application being rejected or the need to open it again for modification.
Possible conflict with other party data, such as national address, partner data, or activities, which requires updating them in parallel.
The presence of financial obligations or violations on the company may hinder the completion of the confirmation until they are settled.
Stopping some services such as municipal licenses or banking transactions if the record is not confirmed on time.
The need for cooperation of all partners or delegates in some complex companies to ensure correct and uniform data entry.
Technical problems or delays in the electronic services platform during stressful seasons (such as the beginning or end of the year).
requiredDocuments: The commercial register must be active.
The presence of a valid investment license for the foreign company, with a period of no less than (90) days.
Fulfilling the requirements for the service of updating a company’s main commercial registry data in the event of updating.

Government fees:
1600 SAR for a joint stock company, simplified joint stock.
1200 SAR for a limited liability company.
1000 SAR for a joint liability company, simple recommendation.
serviceConditions: The commercial register must be active.
The presence of a valid investment license for the foreign company, with a period of no less than (90) days.
Fulfilling the requirements for the service of updating a company’s main commercial registry data in the event of updating.

Government fees:
1600 SAR for a joint stock company, simplified joint stock.
1200 SAR for a limited liability company.
1000 SAR for a joint liability company, simple recommendation.

services.name: Lifting the suspension of the main commercial register of a company
services.shortDescription: This service enables the investor to lift the suspension of the company's main commercial registry after the expiry of the (90) day period from the annual confirmation due date.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of lifting the suspension of a company’s main commercial registry is an electronic regulatory procedure provided by the Ministry of Commerce to enable companies to reactivate their main commercial registry after it was suspended as a result of regulatory, administrative, or financial violations. This procedure aims to give companies an opportunity to correct their situations and ensure that the regulatory requirements are met, so that the main commercial registry becomes effective and qualified to practice commercial activity officially and in compliance with the regulations.

The commercial registry is usually suspended for several reasons, including: failure to update the company’s data on a regular basis, failure to adhere to annual data confirmation, accumulation of violations or unpaid financial obligations, expiration of one of the licenses related to the activity, or issuance of regulatory decisions requiring the cessation of dealing temporarily.

The importance of the service of raising the suspension of a company’s main commercial registration

Returning the company to the correct regulatory status allowing it to resume its commercial operations without hindrance.
Enabling the company to benefit from all government services, such as issuing and renewing licenses, dealing with banks, or entering into tenders.
Protecting the company's commercial reputation from the negative effects associated with the continued suspension of the register.
Avoid further regulatory or financial penalties if the suspension continues for a long period.
Enhancing confidence with partners, customers and regulatory authorities by having an active main commercial registry that complies with the regulations.
Business continuity in a legal and official manner, which reflects on the company’s success and the stability of its dealings.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing the lifting of the suspension of the main commercial register of a company

It is necessary to pay all financial obligations, such as fines or late fees, before submitting a request to lift the suspension.
The need to complete and update the data associated with the register, such as the national address, data of partners and managers, or commercial activities.
Delayed processing if there are accumulated violations or multiple reasons for suspension, which require a longer time to settle them.
Automatically suspending some services until the suspension is lifted, such as obtaining certificates or licenses, which may hinder business during that period.
The need to review other parties if the reason for the suspension is related to violations outside the Ministry of Commerce, such as taxes, zakat, or the municipality.
Potential technical challenges on the electronic platform during submitting the request or processing attached files.

services.name: Annual confirmation of a company’s subsidiary commercial registry data
services.shortDescription: This service enables the investor to confirm the subsidiary commercial registry information annually for companies.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The annual confirmation service for a company’s subsidiary commercial registry data is an electronic regulatory procedure provided by the Ministry of Commerce through its digital platforms. It aims to ensure that companies’ subsidiary commercial registry data are updated and reviewed on an annual basis, in a way that accurately reflects the reality of their commercial and administrative activity. 

When using the service, the company official or delegate logs into the platform of the Ministry of Commerce or the Saudi Business Center, then reviews the sub-commercial registry data such as: commercial activity, national address of the branch, data of the director or delegate, and associated license numbers. After reviewing this data, either:

Confirm its validity as it is if no changes have occurred to it, or
Update it before confirmation if there is any modification or discrepancy between it and reality.

After completing the process and paying the fees (if any), an annual confirmation statement is issued proving that the company’s subsidiary register has been legally approved for the new year.

The importance of the annual confirmation service for a company’s subsidiary commercial registry data

Ensuring the continued validity of the sub-registry and the ability to use it in all official transactions.
Enhancing accuracy and transparency in branch data, to support the work of regulatory authorities, banks and commercial partners.
Avoid suspension of the registry or imposition of fines if the confirmation period expires and the annual update is not completed.
Enabling the company to obtain or renew the necessary licenses based on correct and up-to-date data.
Raising the level of credibility in front of customers and suppliers by providing complete and realistic data.
Integration with other government systems such as zakat, taxes, social insurance, and municipal authorities.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing the annual confirmation of a company’s subsidiary commercial registry data

The need to carefully review all branch data to avoid conflicts or mismatches with official documents.
The confirmation procedure is disrupted if there are financial obligations or irregularities on the register or on the parent company that have not been settled.
Possible conflict with third party data such as national address or municipal licenses, which requires parallel updating.
The possibility of temporarily suspending the register if the annual confirmation is not made within the period specified by the Ministry of Commerce.
Delayed electronic processing or difficulty accessing the platform during stressful seasons (such as the beginning or end of the fiscal year).
Multiple branches for large companies may make the process more complicated since the data for each branch register must be confirmed separately.
requiredDocuments: The commercial register must be active.
The presence of a valid investment license for the foreign company, with a period of no less than (90) days.
Fulfilling the requirements for the service of updating a company’s subsidiary commercial registry data in the event of updating.

Government fees:
1,600 SAR for a joint stock company, simplified joint stock.
1200 SAR for a limited liability company.
1000 SAR for a joint liability company, simple recommendation.
serviceConditions: The commercial register must be active.
The presence of a valid investment license for the foreign company, with a period of no less than (90) days.
Fulfilling the requirements for the service of updating a company’s subsidiary commercial registry data in the event of updating.

Government fees:
1,600 SAR for a joint stock company, simplified joint stock.
1200 SAR for a limited liability company.
1000 SAR for a joint liability company, simple recommendation.

services.name: Lifting the suspension of the commercial registration of a sole proprietorship
services.shortDescription: This service enables the investor to lift the suspension of the main or subsidiary commercial register of an individual institution, after the expiration of a period of (90) days from the due date for the annual confirmation.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of lifting the suspension of the commercial register for an individual institution is one of the regular electronic services provided by the Ministry of Commerce through its digital platforms. It aims to enable the owner of the institution to reactivate his commercial register after it was suspended as a result of regulatory or financial violations or failure to meet some regulatory requirements. 

The commercial registration of individual enterprises is usually suspended for common reasons such as: non-confirmation of annual data, non-payment of fees or fines owed, the presence of regulatory violations, failure to update the national address or owner data, or the expiration of licenses related to the activity. After the establishment owner settles these reasons, he can log in electronically to the platform of the Ministry of Commerce or the Saudi Business Center and submit a request to lift the suspension. The request often requires attaching proof that the reason for the suspension has been avoided, such as payment receipts or updated documents. After reviewing and approving the application, the suspension will be lifted directly, so that the commercial register will return to being valid and usable.

The importance of the service of lifting the suspension of the commercial registration of an individual institution

Reactivating the commercial register so that the owner of the institution can practice his commercial activity legally.
Restoring government powers and services, such as opening bank accounts, renewing licenses, issuing invoices, and dealing with suppliers.
Protecting the institution from further penalties or fines that may result from continued suspension.
Enhancing the confidence of customers, partners and banks by possessing a valid and active commercial register.
Maintaining the trademark and business activity associated with the record, without the need to cancel or re-establish the organization.
Enable operational continuity without disrupting existing business or contracts.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing the lifting of the suspension of the commercial register of an individual institution

It is necessary to settle all financial obligations and fees before submitting a request to lift the suspension.
The need to update basic data such as national address and owner data to match official systems.
The possibility of delaying the process in the event of multiple reasons for suspension or violations by other parties (such as Zakat or the municipality).
Temporarily suspending some services until the suspension is lifted, which may affect the conduct of business.
Compatibility with the requirements of different parties may increase the complexity of procedures when there are multiple obligations.
Possible technical difficulties in the electronic platform while submitting the application or uploading the required documents.
requiredDocuments: The status of the commercial registration must be suspended.
The applicant must be the owner of the institution or have an electronic authorization on its behalf.
Having a valid license if the institution is an association or endowment.
The existence of a valid investment license for the foreign institution, with a period of no less than (90) days
Fulfilling the requirements for the commercial registry data update service in the event of updating.
serviceConditions: The status of the commercial registration must be suspended.
The applicant must be the owner of the institution or have an electronic authorization on its behalf.
Having a valid license if the institution is an association or endowment.
The existence of a valid investment license for the foreign institution, with a period of no less than (90) days
Fulfilling the requirements for the commercial registry data update service in the event of updating.

services.name: Lifting the suspension of the subsidiary commercial register of a company
services.shortDescription: This service enables the investor to lift the suspension of the company’s subsidiary commercial register after the expiry of the (90) day period from the due date for the annual confirmation.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of lifting the suspension of a company’s subsidiary commercial register is a basic electronic service provided by the Ministry of Commerce through its digital platforms. It aims to reactivate the subsidiary commercial register of companies after it has been subject to suspension as a result of regulatory or financial violations or failure to meet some regulatory requirements. 

Suspension of the subsidiary commercial register occurs for common reasons such as: failure to update or confirm annual data, accumulation of unpaid fees or fines, expiration or cancellation of activity licenses, the presence of unaddressed regulatory violations, or failure of branch data to match the main register or national address. After the company corrects the reason, the official or authorized person can enter the platform of the Ministry of Commerce or the Saudi Business Center and submit a request to lift the suspension. This often requires attaching supporting documentation confirming that the reason for the suspension has been eliminated (such as payment receipts or valid licenses). After the request is reviewed and approved, the hold is immediately lifted and the sub-record is reactivated and usable.

The importance of the service of lifting the suspension of a company’s subsidiary commercial register

Reactivating the branch legally to enable it to practice commercial activity officially.
Resuming official transactions such as issuing and renewing licenses, opening bank accounts for the branch, or contracting with suppliers and customers.
Avoid additional fines and penalties that may result from continued suspension.
Protecting the company’s commercial reputation in front of its clients and partners by owning active legal branches.
Maintaining the interconnectedness of the company and its branches within the commercial registry system linked to the main one.
Ensuring benefit from government electronic services related to the register, such as insurance, taxes, and municipalities.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing the lifting of the suspension of the subsidiary commercial register of a company

Requirement to close all violations or financial obligations before accepting a lift request.
It is necessary to update the branch data (such as the national address or activity) to match reality and the main record.
The possibility of delaying the approval of the application if there are multiple reasons for suspension or the need for approvals from other parties.
Temporarily disabling some regulatory procedures until the suspension is lifted and the review is completed.
Possible technical obstacles on the electronic platform while submitting the application or uploading attachments.
Administrative problems in companies with multiple branches, as lifting the suspension for each branch requires separate procedures.
requiredDocuments: The status of the commercial registration must be suspended.
The presence of a valid investment license for the foreign company, with a period of no less than (90) days.
Fulfilling the requirements for the service of updating a company’s subsidiary commercial registry data in the event of updating.
serviceConditions: The status of the commercial registration must be suspended.
The presence of a valid investment license for the foreign company, with a period of no less than (90) days.
Fulfilling the requirements for the service of updating a company’s subsidiary commercial registry data in the event of updating.

services.name: Annual confirmation of commercial registry data for a branch of a foreign/Gulf company
services.shortDescription: This service enables the investor to confirm the data of the main or subsidiary commercial registry annually for a branch of a foreign/Gulf company.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The annual confirmation service of commercial registry data for a branch of a foreign or Gulf company is a periodic regulatory procedure imposed by the Ministry of Commerce in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia to ensure that all data of branches of foreign or Gulf companies registered in the Kingdom is up-to-date and consistent with the actual reality of activity. This service aims to enhance transparency, raise the level of regulatory reliability, and ensure that the branch carries out its activities in a manner compatible with all Saudi regulations.

Completing this service on its annual schedule ensures that the branch’s commercial register remains active and avoids regular suspension or suspension. It also demonstrates the parent company’s (foreign or Gulf) commitment to the laws of the Kingdom and its credibility before local partners and government agencies.

The importance of the annual confirmation service of commercial registry data for a branch of a foreign or Gulf company

Ensuring that the branch’s commercial register remains active, allowing it to carry out its commercial activities.
Raising the level of transparency in the data of foreign and Gulf companies and their conformity with operational reality.
Enhancing confidence among investors, customers and local banks with accurate and updated data.
Automatically linking data with other government agencies such as zakat, taxes, customs, and the Investment Authority.
Avoid penalties or suspension of the commercial register as a result of failure to update or confirm data.
Enabling the branch to enter into contracts and tenders or obtain the necessary licenses to expand activity.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing the annual confirmation of commercial registry data for a branch of a foreign or Gulf company

The data must be matched with the parent company’s documents, which sometimes requires translation or legalization of documents.
The need to update data on investment or ministerial licenses before completing confirmation.
The possibility of suspending or rejecting the application if there are financial obligations or fines on the branch that have not been paid.
The complexity of procedures in the event of multiple branches of one company in the Kingdom, as each branch record must be confirmed separately.
Potential technical problems in the electronic platform, especially during stressful periods at the end of the financial year.
Possible delay in final approvals when there is data linked to external parties (such as the Ministry of Investment or competent ministries depending on the activity).
requiredDocuments: The commercial register must be active.
The presence of a valid investment license for the foreign company, with a period of no less than (90) days.
Fulfilling the requirements for the service of updating commercial registry data for a branch of a foreign/Gulf company in the event of updating.
serviceConditions: The commercial register must be active.
The presence of a valid investment license for the foreign company, with a period of no less than (90) days.
Fulfilling the requirements for the service of updating commercial registry data for a branch of a foreign/Gulf company in the event of updating.

services.name: Lifting the suspension of the commercial register of a branch of a foreign/Gulf company
services.shortDescription: This service enables the investor to lift the suspension of the main or subsidiary commercial register of a branch of a foreign/Gulf company after the expiration of a period of (90) days from the due date for the annual confirmation.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of lifting the suspension of the commercial register of a branch of a foreign or Gulf company is an official electronic service provided by the Ministry of Commerce in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It aims to reactivate the commercial register of the foreign or Gulf branch after it was suspended as a result of regulatory or financial violations or due to failure to meet some legal requirements. This service gives the parent company (whether foreign or Gulf) the opportunity to correct the conditions of its branches in the Kingdom and ensure the continuity of its commercial activities in a legal and transparent manner.

The request requires entering the data associated with the record and attaching proof that the reason for the suspension has been removed, such as:

Receipts for payment of fines or fees.
Renewing investment or sector licenses.
Updated documents for director data or national address.

Once the application is reviewed and approved by the Ministry, the suspension will be lifted immediately and the branch’s commercial register will be restored to its active legal status.

The importance of the service of lifting the suspension of the commercial register of a branch of a foreign or Gulf company

Legally reactivating the branch’s commercial activity after the suspension period.
Enabling the branch to carry out its official transactions, such as concluding contracts, opening bank accounts, and renewing licenses.
Prevent the accumulation of additional fines and penalties resulting from continued suspension.
Enhancing the reputation of the parent company before partners and customers in the Saudi and Gulf markets.
Ensuring continued legal integration between the parent company’s main record and its branch record in the Kingdom.
Increasing reliability and commitment to the Saudi system, which enhances opportunities for expansion and investment.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing the lifting of the suspension of the commercial register of a branch of a foreign or Gulf company

It is necessary to settle all financial obligations and fines before filing the application.
The need for coordination with the Ministry of Investment or sectoral authorities if the reason for the suspension is related to an investment license or a restricted activity.
It is mandatory to submit documents translated and certified from the country of the parent company if there are changes related to foreign partners or directors.
The possibility of delaying the approval of the application if the violations are multiple or require a joint review by more than one government agency.
Temporarily suspending some regular services (such as renewals or obtaining statements) until the suspension is lifted.
Possible technical difficulties on the electronic platform while uploading documents or following up on the application status
requiredDocuments: The status of the commercial registration must be suspended.
The presence of a valid investment license for the foreign company, with a period of no less than (90) days.
Fulfilling the requirements for the service of updating commercial registry data for a branch of a foreign/Gulf company in the event of updating.
serviceConditions: The status of the commercial registration must be suspended.
The presence of a valid investment license for the foreign company, with a period of no less than (90) days.
Fulfilling the requirements for the service of updating commercial registry data for a branch of a foreign/Gulf company in the event of updating.

services.name: Cancellation of the subsidiary commercial register of a branch of a foreign/Gulf company
services.shortDescription: This service enables the investor to cancel the subsidiary commercial register of a branch of a foreign/Gulf company upon completion of the commercial activity.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription: This service enables the investor to cancel the subsidiary commercial register of a branch of a foreign/Gulf company upon completion of the commercial activity.
requiredDocuments: Submit a statement that there are no licenses, or cancel licenses for activities that require a license before issuance.

Submitting a certified letter from the company director or agency, and if the agency or authorization is from abroad; It must be authenticated by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and the Saudi Embassy, ​​with a translation from an accredited office.
serviceConditions: Submit a statement that there are no licenses, or cancel licenses for activities that require a license before issuance.

Submitting a certified letter from the company director or agency, and if the agency or authorization is from abroad; It must be authenticated by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and the Saudi Embassy, ​​with a translation from an accredited office.

services.name: Amending the Memorandum of Association/Basic Law (Reducing Capital)
services.shortDescription: This service enables the investor to amend the capital in the company’s articles of incorporation by changing any article of the contract or amending it in accordance with the new corporate legislative system.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription: This service enables the investor to amend the capital in the company’s articles of incorporation by changing any article of the contract or amending it in accordance with the new corporate legislative system.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
Identities of partners and managers (Saudis and residents).
A valid investment license, if the applicant is an investor.
A copy of the articles of incorporation.
serviceConditions: Having a valid commercial register.
If the company is foreign or mixed, there is an investment license that is valid for at least 3 months.
The presence of an extraordinary assembly resolution or a shareholders’ resolution if the company is (joint stock, or simplified joint stock).
The company shall not be suspended by the following authorities: The Ministry of Commerce. Zakat, Tax and Customs Authority for the second and third levels.

The following must be verified if the company is (limited liability, simple partnership, or joint liability company): Obtaining prior approval from the Central Bank of Saudi Arabia if the activity requires this. Amending the investment license if required. The directors must not be government employees.

services.name: Update the data of the commercial registry owner
services.shortDescription: This service enables the investor to update the data of the owner/manager of the commercial register.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The commercial registration owner data update service is one of the electronic services provided through the Ministry of Commerce and the Saudi Business Center, and aims to enable commercial registration owners - whether individual institutions or companies - to modify or complete the owner data registered in the system so that it is up to date and consistent with reality. This service is one of the most important regulatory tools that help raise the level of transparency, ensure accuracy of information, and adherence to Saudi regulations that require that commercial registry data be matched with official identity data, the civil registry, or the competent authorities.

The data updating process usually includes: amending the national identity or residency data for non-Saudis, updating contact information (email, phone), modifying the owner’s national address, or adding recent data related to the owner when needed.

After completing the update process and paying any due fees (if any), the changes are approved immediately and reflected directly in the government system and become available to the rest of the related official bodies (such as zakat and taxes, social insurance, and municipalities).

The importance of the service of updating commercial registration owner data

Ensuring the accuracy and validity of owner data in accordance with official records in the Ministry of Interior and other government agencies.
Avoid suspensions or fines resulting from outdated or incorrect data.
Enhancing reliability for commercial partners and banks by having a commercial register with up-to-date and approved data.
Supporting integration with other government systems such as taxes and insurance through data standardization.
Enabling the continuation of other electronic services related to the commercial registry, such as visas, licenses, and renewals.
Facilitating digital transformation and transparency by relying on accurate and documentable data.

Difficulties related to completing or updating the data of the owner of the commercial register

The necessity of complete conformity with civil status or passport data, and any error may lead to the application being rejected.
The need to attach updated documents such as the national address or translated and certified documents for non-Saudis.
Possible disruption or delay in the event of financial obligations or violations pending in the commercial register.
Pause some services such as printing or registry renewal until the update is approved.
The possibility of encountering technical difficulties when accessing the platform or uploading the required documents.
Approval of changes is delayed if the modifications are related to external parties or require additional review.
requiredDocuments: 1- The commercial register must be active.
2- The age must not be less than 18 years.
3- He must not be a government employee.
4- Add at least one manager to the commercial register.
5- Enter the approval of the appointed manager, if he is not the owner of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: 1- The commercial register must be active.
2- The age must not be less than 18 years.
3- He must not be a government employee.
4- Add at least one manager to the commercial register.
5- Enter the approval of the appointed manager, if he is not the owner of the commercial register.

services.name: Updating the commercial registry data for an individual institution
services.shortDescription: This service enables the investor to update the data of the main or subsidiary commercial registry for an individual institution

serviceDetails.serviceDescription: This service enables the investor to update the data of the main or subsidiary commercial registry for an individual institution.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
A copy of the establishment’s national address.
serviceConditions: 1- The commercial register must be active.
2- The branch manager must not be a government employee, and must not be less than 18 years old.
3- The presence of a valid license in the event of adding an activity that requires a license before issuance.
4- Submitting a statement of cancellation of licenses in the event of removing an activity that requires a license before issuance.
5- The presence of a valid investment license for the foreign institution, with a period of no less than (90) days.

6- Verify the following if it is a cooperative or charitable association: 
- The association has a certificate of registration with the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development.
- The presence of an agency or authorization from the association’s council for the applicant.

7- Verify the following if it is an endowment institution:
- The presence of a waqf deed.
- The presence of a legal power of attorney from the donor, the superintendent of the endowment, or the supervisory board.

categoryName: Saudi Authority for Auditors and Accountants

services.name: Non-practicing primary member
services.shortDescription: The basic non-practicing member is anyone who has obtained a fellowship certificate from the Saudi Organization for Auditors and Accountants and applied for membership in the organization as a basic non-practicing member and obtained it.

serviceDetails.ministryInfo: Saudi Organization for Chartered and Professional Accountants (SOCPA)
serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The non-practicing basic member service is a service provided by the Saudi Organization for Accountants and Auditors and is granted to everyone who has obtained the Authority’s fellowship certificate and wishes to join the Authority’s membership without actually practicing the accounting and auditing profession. This service aims to give members the advantage of professional affiliation to the Authority and benefit from its many advantages without the requirement to practice actual accounting or auditing work in establishments, which enhances professional and personal value while preserving the right to participate in the decisions of the Authority’s General Assembly and to vote and run for membership in the Board of Directors.

To be accepted for this service, it is required to obtain a fellowship certificate from the Saudi Authority for Auditors and Accountants, and to apply electronically through the Authority’s services portal. The application is reviewed and a membership certificate is issued once the conditions are met and the annual fees are paid, which amount to 1,000 Saudi riyals annually. Membership grants its holder the right to attend public meetings and participate in decision-making within the Authority, with the possibility of obtaining a reduction of up to 20% on fees for specialized training courses, and the right to obtain all scientific and professional publications and electronic journals issued by the Authority.

The importance of serving the non-practicing basic member

It gives the Authority's fellowship holders official recognition and a supportive professional platform, even though they do not actually practice the profession.
Supports effective communication with the professional community and the exchange of intellectual experiences and standard practices.
It allows members to benefit from the scientific body’s publications and specialized training programs.
It grants the right to vote, run for the presidency of the Board of Directors, and participate in professional decision-making.
It provides non-practicing members with a recognized professional identity that enhances opportunities for continuous learning and development.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

It is necessary to expedite the payment of annual membership fees to ensure continuity of validity and avoid cancellation.
The Authority faces challenges in updating the database periodically to ensure the accuracy of non-practicing members’ data.
Possible suspension or cancellation of membership in the event of late payment or failure to meet regulatory requirements.
The need for the member to constantly follow up on professional developments and not rely solely on his membership
requiredDocuments: Certificate of Fellowship from the Saudi Authority for Auditors and Accountants.
ID photo.
serviceConditions: Obtaining a fellowship from the Saudi Authority for Auditors and Accountants.

ID photo.
serviceConditions: Obtaining a fellowship from the Saudi Authority for Auditors and Accountants.

services.name: Professional membership
services.shortDescription: Through this service, anyone who has obtained a license to provide accounting services or zakat and tax accounting services can request to obtain a professional membership.

serviceDetails.ministryInfo: Saudi Organization for Chartered and Professional Accountants (SOCPA)
serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The professional membership service provided by the Saudi Organization for Auditors and Accountants is one of the important central services targeting certified public accountants and professionals who hold accredited professional licenses and enable them to practice the profession legally and officially. This membership contributes to strengthening the position of the Authority’s members within the accounting and auditing sector, and confirms their commitment to the laws and regulations regulating the profession, in addition to enabling them to participate effectively in developing the financial and accounting sector in the Kingdom.

Professional membership includes fulfilling several conditions for registration, including obtaining a valid professional license, passing the requirements for continuing professional education, and adhering to the professional quality control programs imposed by the Authority. It also includes procedures for paying the annual membership subscription, which amounts to 7,000 Saudi riyals. Professionals can register and renew electronically through the Authority’s portal, follow the application status directly, and receive membership certificates electronically or through the Authority’s service centers.

Professional membership is considered a symbol of credibility and professionalism in the market, as it contributes to raising the quality of financial services provided, and provides the opportunity to participate in the Authority’s committees and events. It also allows members to benefit from special offers and approved training programs, which supports the continuous development of their professional skills and capabilities.

The importance of professional membership service

Confirming the legal legitimacy of practicing the accounting and auditing profession in Saudi Arabia.
Enabling members to participate in making professional and structural decisions.
Enhancing confidence in financial services provided to institutions and individuals through certified professionals.
Providing training and development opportunities that help improve members’ skills.
Supporting the professional oversight system and adhering to international standards.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

Fulfilling the requirements of continuing professional education and mandatory training programs.
Challenges in updating ongoing data and members’ commitment to renewing membership.
Possibility of facing administrative delays due to lack of documents or errors in registration.
The need to develop the electronic system to be more flexible and quick in dealing with requests.
requiredDocuments: A copy of a license to provide accounting services or zakat and tax accounting services.
ID photo.
serviceConditions: Having a license to provide accounting services or zakat and tax accounting services.

Professional members' subscriptions are calculated on the basis that membership began from the first day of the Authority's fiscal year, with the exception of the first registration or modification of membership, in which case the subscription is calculated over the parts of the period remaining to the end of the fiscal year.

services.name: Request to take the professional certification exam in government accounting and auditing
services.shortDescription: The applicant can register for the test through electronic services

serviceDetails.ministryInfo: Saudi Organization for Chartered and Professional Accountants (SOCPA)
serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The application service for the professional certification test in government accounting and auditing is an electronic service provided by the Saudi Authority for Auditors and Accountants, and aims to enable accountants and those interested in the government field to apply to take professional tests that qualify them to obtain accredited professional certificates that enhance their professional level and prove their scientific and practical competence in the profession of accounting and auditing in government agencies. This certificate is one of the basic requirements for accountants wishing to work in the government sector, as it confirms adherence to national and international accounting and regulatory standards.

The registration process for the professional certificate test takes place through the Authority’s electronic platform, where the applicant creates an account, fills out his personal data, then uploads the required documents, which include a colored personal photo, a copy of the university certificate, diploma, or high school certificate, and a copy of the passport for non-Saudis. After completing the data and documents, the applicant can choose the city in which he wishes to take the test and the available payment method, whether electronic or through other approved means.

The application will be reviewed electronically within a period not exceeding ten working days, and the Authority will issue a final acceptance to the applicant, authorizing him to attend the test. After acceptance is issued, the applicant must choose the test location through the National Center for Measurement website 21 days before the start of the course, otherwise he will be considered absent and not entitled to attend the test in the current session. The test is held in major cities such as Riyadh, Jeddah, and Dammam, and includes multiple choice and essay questions aimed at measuring theoretical and practical knowledge of professional certification.

The importance of the professional certification test application service

Enabling applicants to take an official test proving their competence in government accounting and auditing.
Providing a simplified and fast electronic channel for registration, following up on applications, and communicating with the Authority.
Enhancing the technical and professional qualification of accountants wishing to work in government agencies.
Supporting the achievement of international standards for accounting and financial control in the government sector.
Increase the chances of applicants obtaining recognized government jobs and clear professional requirements.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

It is necessary to complete all required documents and conditions accurately to avoid rejection of applications.
Commitment to registration dates and choosing test locations within the specified time periods.
Facing registration pressure during peak periods that may affect the speed of processing requests.
The challenge is in preparing applicants for the various theoretical and practical aspects of the test.
requiredDocuments: A colored personal photo.
A copy of a bachelor’s degree, diploma, or high school diploma.
A copy of the passport for non-Saudis.
A declaration and authorization form, a certificate equivalency decision, or a certificate verification report.
ID photo.
serviceConditions: Bachelor’s degree, diploma, high school diploma, or equivalent, provided that he has completed the training track program in government accounting or government auditing.

services.name: Request to take the professional certification exam in International Financial Reporting Standards
services.shortDescription: The applicant can register for the test through electronic services

serviceDetails.ministryInfo: Saudi Organization for Chartered and Professional Accountants (SOCPA)
serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The application service for applying for the professional certification test in the International Financial Reporting Standards is a specialized electronic service provided by the Saudi Organization for Auditors and Accountants, and aims to enable professionals to apply to take an approved test that measures the extent of their familiarity with the international standards for preparing and presenting financial reports (IFRS). This service provides accountants and those holding academic degrees in accounting or its equivalent the opportunity to develop their competence and obtain a recognized professional certificate that supports their career path and opens up career advancement prospects for them in the financial and accounting labor market, whether within the Kingdom or in global labor markets.

Electronic registration for the test takes place through the Authority’s platform, where the applicant uploads his personal data and copies of his academic documents, and chooses the appropriate city and date for the test from among the announced schedules that usually include the main cities (Riyadh, Jeddah, Dammam). Application requirements include having a bachelor’s degree or diploma in accounting or any acceptable specialty according to the unified Saudi classification of specializations. The fees for applying for the test are 1,200 Saudi riyals for the first time and 500 riyals for the repeat, and are reduced to 500 riyals for those who have completed the first and second level qualification courses with the Authority or one of its approved partners.

The test itself consists of 60 multiple-choice questions and lasts for 120 minutes. The applicant is required to pass a 60% pass rate, with the possibility of repeating it twice if he does not achieve success on the first attempt, after which he undergoes additional rehabilitation courses. Applications are processed within ten working days of submitting them, and applicants must finally choose the seat for the test through the National Measurement Center twenty-one days before the committee’s appointment to confirm the reservation. It is possible to postpone for one session only, provided that at least thirty days in advance, the Authority grants support programs to compensate fees through the Human Resources Development Fund within the incentives for developing Saudi financial cadres.

The importance of the application service for applying for the professional certification exam in International Financial Reporting Standards

It gives individuals the opportunity to obtain a recognized professional certificate that enhances efficiency and comprehensiveness in applying international financial reporting standards.
It contributes to raising the quality of financial reports and increasing transparency and oversight in Saudi companies and institutions in line with the best international standards.
It supports promotion and employment opportunities for accountants in the government and private sectors, locally and internationally.
It enhances the commitment of Saudi establishments to the requirements of international reports and standards within the Kingdom’s Vision 2030.
It provides a flexible electronic channel for registration and follow-up and integrated technical support services.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

The need for extensive study and a broad understanding of international standards before taking the test.
It is necessary to strictly adhere to the specified dates for registration and choosing locations to avoid losing the opportunity.
Possibility of postponement and difficulty reserving seats during peak periods due to high demand.
Challenging the psychological and procedural readiness to complete an intensive test in a short time.
requiredDocuments: A colored personal photo.
A copy of your bachelor’s degree or diploma.
A copy of the passport for non-Saudis.
Attach a declaration and authorization form, certificate equivalency decision, or certificate verification report.
ID photo.
serviceConditions: The fee for taking the test is 1,200 riyals for the first time and 500 riyals for repeating the test. The fee for taking the test is 500 riyals for those who attended the first and second level courses in full.
Bachelor's or Diploma in Accounting or equivalent.

services.name: License registration form to provide zakat and tax services
services.shortDescription: This form allows you to apply for a license to provide zakat and tax services

serviceDetails.ministryInfo: Saudi Organization for Chartered and Professional Accountants (SOCPA)
serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The license registration form service to provide zakat and tax services is one of the basic professional services provided through the Saudi Organization for Auditors and Accountants in cooperation with the Ministry of Commerce in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. This service aims to regulate the practice of providing zakat, income tax, and value-added tax services through official licensing for individuals or specialized professional offices, and sets clear standards to ensure professionalism and transparency in consulting processes and the preparation of zakat and tax returns for all establishments in the commercial and investment sector.

Practicing the profession is limited to those holding an official license, with the exception of the certified public accountant previously licensed to practice accounting and auditing, which contributes to preventing unprofessional practices, protecting the market for zakat and tax services, and achieving legal and ethical compliance. The license includes preparing zakat and tax returns, providing relevant technical consultations, and prohibits any financial auditing of the facility, in order to preserve the independence of auditing and legal accounting operations.

Licensing conditions include that the applicant must be of Saudi nationality and fully qualified, pass the professional tests approved by the Authority, and be dedicated to practicing the profession fully during the first years, with temporary partial-time work permitted in accordance with the Ministry’s controls. The applicant must fill out the registration form on the Authority’s platform and complete all the required data and attachments, such as academic qualifications, a professional membership certificate, a recent CV, and proof of good conduct. After the application is completed, the Authority conducts an official verification and submits its recommendation to the Ministry, which issues an approval or rejection decision within a period not exceeding five working days.

The applicant has the right to appeal the rejection decision before the Minister within one month, and a successful license is granted a validity period extending to three years, with the possibility of renewal based on an application submitted ninety days before its expiry. All data of the licensee and his partners are recorded in a legal register of the Authority to ensure continuous control and transparency in practice. The licensee must adhere to professional conduct and ethics and specific professional standards, and not link his fees to the results of the services provided.

This service provides great facilitation for specialists in the zakat and tax sector to provide their services legally, and raises the level of quality and professionalism in the Saudi financial field, in a way that supports the strategic directions within the framework of the Kingdom’s Vision 2030 and contributes to consolidating the culture of tax compliance and transparency in the business sector. 

The importance of the service of licensing registration form to provide zakat and tax services

Regulating the licensing of providing zakat and tax services in accordance with precise professional and procedural rules.
Protecting the Saudi market from irregular practices in the field of financial and tax consulting.
Providing a clear legal channel to register and document service providers with the relevant authorities.
Supporting zakat and tax consultations for companies and commercial establishments in order to raise the quality of the returns submitted.
Ensuring that professionals adhere to Saudi and international standards of conduct, ethics, and accounting standards.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

The need to prepare accurate and documented documents to accept the licensing form with the Authority and the Ministry.
Challenges of passing professional exams and the availability of specialized competencies in the field.
Delay or suspension in issuing the license as a result of lack of data or incompatibility with the Ministry’s controls.
The necessity of continuous commitment to professional oversight, transparency and development programs.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the national ID.
A copy of the employer’s release or approval.
A copy of the social insurance certificate.
A copy of the certificate of passing the professional examination held by the Authority.
serviceConditions: To be of Saudi nationality.
To be fully qualified.
Passing the Authority’s professional examination.

Full-time to practice the profession, provided that he has the right to work part-time during the first three years of obtaining the license in accordance with the regulations prepared by the Authority and issued by a decision of the Minister.
It may take some time to view and execute the request.

services.name: Affiliate Membership (Professional Registration)
services.shortDescription: The Authority provides members of the profession in all economic sectors in the Kingdom and in their various categories the opportunity to belong to an inclusive professional entity through its membership.

serviceDetails.ministryInfo: Saudi Organization for Chartered and Professional Accountants (SOCPA)
serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The Affiliate Membership Service (Professional Registration) is one of the important electronic services provided by the Saudi Organization for Accountants and Auditors, and aims to open the door to professional affiliation for all people wishing to associate professionally with regulated foundations and rules, without requiring the applicant to be a certified public accountant. This membership includes accountants who hold academic or professional qualifications in the disciplines of accounting and finance, and who wish to document their professional presence within an officially recognized entity that allows them to benefit from the authority’s services and approved programs to enhance their scientific and practical skills.

Through this service, the Authority provides subscribers with the opportunity to register professionally through an advanced electronic platform, where the membership registration form is filled out and the required documents are attached, such as a copy of the academic or professional certificate, a copy of the national ID or residence permit, and a personal photo, in addition to an acknowledgment and authorization form. The application includes choosing the membership type “Transfer Membership” and filling out all the necessary fields to complete the registration successfully.

Membership requirements include accepting applicants with a bachelor's degree or diploma in accounting, with admission to those holding lower degrees provided they complete specific training requirements and qualification programs. Registration requires paying an annual membership fee of 500 Saudi riyals, in addition to a one-time verification fee of 500 riyals for those holding certificates issued abroad. The application is studied and reviewed by the Authority, then issued after ensuring that all conditions and standards are met within a period not exceeding five working days.

This membership provides multiple opportunities for members to benefit from vocational training, attend educational seminars, and obtain technical support and ongoing guidance to enhance members’ competence and improve their professional opportunities in the local and international labor market. It also maintains an electronic record surrounding their professional identity, ensuring their legal and professional recognition and accreditation of their performance.

The importance of the affiliate membership service

Enabling individuals to document and authenticate their professional presence in the accounting labor market.
Enhancing training and continuing professional development opportunities for members.
Providing an official channel to communicate with the Authority and obtain its electronic services.
Supporting accounting and finance roles in various institutions and companies through certified professionals.
Offering opportunities for later installation within a full professional membership with additional rights.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

It is necessary to confirm the accuracy and validity of the documents and data submitted to ensure acceptance.
Challenges in updating and following up on the legal and professional status of various memberships.
Handling requests at peak times and providing the necessary support to speed up processing.
The need to educate applicants about the importance of adhering to membership conditions and renewal requirements.
requiredDocuments: Attach a copy of the academic or professional certificate.
Attach a copy of your ID (valid).
Attach a copy of the passport (for non-Saudis).
Attach a personal photo (not mandatory for women).
Acknowledgment and authorization, certificate equivalency decision, or certificate verification report.
serviceConditions: 500 riyals membership fee (annual), in addition to 500 riyals fees for verifying the authenticity of the certificate if it was issued outside the Kingdom (paid one time only).
Bachelor's degree or higher qualification in Accounting.

Holders of an intermediate diploma in accounting (provided that the duration of the diploma is not less than two academic years) from recognized universities or colleges. A holder of an associate diploma in accounting (more than one year and less than two years) may apply for membership on the condition of attending the qualifying training program for the accounting technician exam.

He holds a bachelor’s degree from one of the colleges of administrative sciences and has passed at least 15 academic hours in accounting. * Anyone who does not complete the quorum of the aforementioned 15 academic hours can complete the hours in accordance with the approved controls for completing accounting hours.
Holds a professional certificate.

services.name: Approved accounting standards
services.shortDescription: This service allows you to view updates based on accounting standards

serviceDetails.ministryInfo: Saudi Organization for Chartered and Professional Accountants (SOCPA)
serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The Certified Accounting Standards Service represents an important regulatory and professional framework that is applied in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia to regulate the processes of preparing financial statements and classifying them in accordance with the International Financial Reporting Standards known as IFRS﻿. This service is one of the basic pillars that supports the transparency and credibility of the financial statements of economic entities, whether private or public companies, and effectively contributes to their alignment with global best practices.

Various standards, such as IAS 1﻿, IAS 37﻿, IFRS 15﻿, and others, include a set of rules and guidelines that explain how to define, measure, and display financial elements in an organized and unified manner, which facilitates the comparison process between companies and enhances the sustainability of the national and international financial market. The Saudi Organization for Certified Public Accountants relies on issuing periodically updated versions of these standards, to ensure their compatibility with economic and legislative developments, in addition to updates to the application of the standards in line with developments in technology and the economic environment.

The standards are applied to all general purpose financial statements, and are subject to review and audit by official authorities to ensure full compliance with international rules and standards. The stages of applying the standards vary between planning, implementation, and review, with an emphasis on the importance of commitment to transparency and full disclosure of financial information.

Such a service contributes to raising the level of confidence in financial information, facilitating the process of obtaining financing and attracting foreign investments, and also enhances the reputation of the Saudi financial sector at the international level.

The importance of accredited accounting standards service

Aligning financial statements with international standards to increase compatibility and transparency.
Supporting economic and administrative decision-making based on reliable financial reports.
Achieving legal and regulatory compliance in preparing financial statements.
Facilitating the acceptance of financial statements by financing agencies and investors.
Enhancing economic competition and attracting foreign investments.

Challenges and difficulties

Continuously updating standards and implementing them accurately.
Training accounting cadres to understand and apply international standards correctly.
Facing resistance to change from some traditional institutions or companies.
Ensure full compliance with standards and that financial statements comply with the highest quality standards.
requiredDocuments: ID photo.
serviceConditions: According to the agreement to use international standards signed between the Authority and the International Financial Reporting Standards Foundation, only persons residing in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia are entitled to register and view the contents of this page.

services.name: Primary practicing member
services.shortDescription: Through this service, the non-practicing basic member can request to apply for a license to practice the accounting and auditing profession. After obtaining the license, his membership will be upgraded to a basic practicing member.

serviceDetails.ministryInfo: Saudi Organization for Chartered and Professional Accountants (SOCPA)
serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The basic practicing member service is an important and prestigious service provided by the Saudi Organization for Accountants and Auditors. This membership is granted to certified public accountants who hold a license to practice the accounting and auditing profession within the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. This membership is considered the highest level of membership in the Authority, as it reflects the level of professionalism and excellence of the chartered accountant, and entitles the member to actively participate in professional decision-making within the Authority, and to contribute to the development of the accounting and auditing sector in accordance with local and international standards.

To obtain basic practitioner membership, the applicant must be a Saudi citizen, fully qualified, hold a university degree or higher qualification in accounting or its equivalent, and possess a valid practicing license issued by the Authority. Applicants are required to pay an annual subscription fee of ten thousand Saudi riyals, and must adhere to the Code of Professional Conduct and ethical standards that govern the profession. Membership includes broad rights such as attending General Assembly meetings, running for membership in the Board of Directors, and participating in specialized committees, in addition to obtaining exclusive discounts in training courses and conferences organized by the Authority.

Application procedures include filling out the electronic form available on the authority’s platform, attaching the required official documents such as a copy of the identity card, qualification certificate, and license to practice the profession, with the ability to follow the status of the application electronically until approval is issued. Membership is granted with an official, recognized certificate that enhances the member’s professional confidence and facilitates job opportunities for him in the government and private sectors within the Kingdom.

The importance of practicing core member service

It enhances the position of the chartered accountant and gives him high professional practice flexibility and greater responsibilities.
Provides opportunities to influence and participate in the development of professional policy and accounting and auditing laws.
It increases the level of confidence in the accounting and auditing services provided to establishments.
It gives the member benefits and incentives such as discounts, training and ongoing professional support.
Ensures strict professional oversight that enhances adherence to ethical and professional standards.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

The necessity of meeting academic and professional requirements and adhering to ethical standards strictly.
Challenges in renewing membership and paying annual subscriptions on a regular basis to avoid cancellation.
Continuous development of the member’s skills to keep pace with changes in legislation and the accounting environment.
The Authority needs to provide excellent technical support to ensure smooth submission of applications and access to services.
requiredDocuments: A copy of a license to practice the accounting profession.
Proof of obtaining the Fellowship of the Saudi Authority for Auditors and Accountants.
ID photo.
serviceConditions: He holds a fellowship from the Saudi Organization for Auditors and Accountants.
He holds a valid license to practice the profession of accounting and auditing.

1- The person dedicated to practicing the profession in accordance with the following regulations: A- He must be of Saudi nationality. B- He must be fully qualified. C- He must have a university degree or any higher qualification in the field of accounting or any other specialty related to the profession in accordance with the standards specified by the regulations. D- To be a core member of the Authority, and to have experience in the nature of work of no less than one year. E- That he be of good conduct and conduct, and not convicted of a legal punishment or a crime against dishonesty or honor unless he has been rehabilitated, and that a disciplinary decision of dismissal from government service has not been issued against him unless (three) years have passed since the issuance of the disciplinary decision.

2- Who is not dedicated to practicing the profession in accordance with the following controls: A- The conditions mentioned in Paragraph (1) of this Article. B- Determine a maximum period for practicing the profession full-time. C- Raising the percentage of supervision hours required of the licensee for each client, while setting a maximum limit for the total hours during the year. D- Not providing audit services to high-risk companies.

services.name: Annual data program
services.shortDescription: The offices provide the necessary data and information that enable the Authority to follow up and monitor the quality of the professional’s performance.

serviceDetails.ministryInfo: Saudi Organization for Chartered and Professional Accountants (SOCPA)
serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The Annual Data Program service is one of the basic programs provided by the Saudi Authority for Auditors and Accountants, and aims to ensure the accuracy and transparency of the financial and professional information provided by accounting and auditing offices to the Authority. This program comes within the framework of the Authority’s ongoing efforts to develop the quality of professional performance and monitor the extent of accounting offices’ compliance with the approved system and regulations, in order to achieve a high level of professionalism in providing financial and accounting services in the Kingdom.

The mechanism of the program is to oblige offices to submit annual reports and specific financial and administrative data according to forms approved by the Authority, which include general information about the office, data on working members of the Authority, data of professionals and technicians, in addition to work summaries that include the number of clients, working hours, and services provided during the fiscal year. These data are uploaded electronically through the Authority’s automated deposit program within a period not exceeding four months from the end of the financial period covered by the data.

The program aims to provide the Authority with accurate and organized information that helps monitor the quality of accounting offices’ performance, and enables it to take the necessary measures in the event of violations or deficiencies, by examining and analyzing the data provided, and following up with the offices to ensure their compliance with professional and ethical standards. Through this program, the Authority also provides a comprehensive database that supports research studies and strategic planning for the development of the professional sector.

The service includes periodic follow-up and continuous evaluation of the quality of performance through annual and special examinations, and the provision of various forms and reports that comply with the Authority’s requirements to ensure transparency of information and evaluate professional work in accordance with international best practices.

The importance of the annual data program service

Enhances transparency and accountability in the accounting and auditing sector.
It contributes to raising the quality of professional services through continuous performance monitoring.
It helps the Authority make administrative and regulatory decisions based on accurate data.
It supports strategic development and research that benefits the profession and the sector.
Providing financial security for entities benefiting from accounting services.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

It is necessary to strictly adhere to the deadlines for submitting data to avoid violations.
Challenges of ensuring the accuracy and validity of data provided by different offices.
Difficulties in coordinating with offices to ensure the correct handling of inspection notes.
The need for continuous development of the electronic platform to ensure improved experience and speed of verification
requiredDocuments: The start and end dates of the annual data year if the office is new, or the date of joining the company if the chartered accountant joins as a partner.
ID photo.
License copy.
Required data: (name - email - mobile number).
serviceConditions: To benefit from the service, the beneficiary must be an owner, partner, or employee in the accounting office.

services.name: Continuing professional education programme
services.shortDescription: This service allows you to add continuing education points

serviceDetails.ministryInfo: Saudi Organization for Chartered and Professional Accountants (SOCPA)
serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
