services.name: Transfer of ownership of a trademark
services.shortDescription: A service that allows the new owner to submit a request to transfer trademark ownership.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The trademark ownership transfer service is an official procedure supervised by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property, and allows the current owner to transfer all rights associated with the trademark to another individual or company under a documented sale, gift, or assignment contract, or automatically by inheritance, in accordance with Saudi regulations and ensuring the protection of commercial rights. 

Steps to transfer ownership of a trademark

Concluding a contract transferring ownership: The transaction must be in writing (sale contract/donation/assignment/will), stating the two parties and the basic data of the mark, while avoiding any harmful purpose or misleading the public.

Submit an electronic application through the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property portal:

Enter the platform and choose the “Trademark Ownership Transfer” service.
Fill out the ownership transfer form, and attach the legal transfer document, the commercial registry of the new party, the trademark certificate, and the articles of incorporation (if any).
If the request is made through an agent, a legal power of attorney or electronic authorization must be attached.
Payment of transfer fees: The fees for transferring trademark ownership are 1,000 Saudi riyals, and the publishing fees are 500 Saudi riyals.
Officially publishing the transfer incident: The publication invoice is issued, the transfer announcement is published, and the trademark becomes in the name of the new owner after publication is complete.
Updating the trademark data: The right to dispose of and amend the trademark data is transferred to the new owner, provided that no fundamental modifications are made that change the essence of the mark.
Important conditions and questions about transferring ownership
The trademark must be owned by the selling party and the protection period must be valid. Ownership of an expired trademark must not be transferred unless it is renewed first.
The transfer does not gain its legal effect until the change is noted in the authority’s register and the transfer incident is published in accordance with the executive regulations of the trademark system.
The assignment or sale contract states the mark number, commercial category, commercial data of both parties, and the official approval of both parties, with proof of payment of the financial consideration/discharge of liability.

The importance of service

Providing the free disposal of intangible assets for companies and individuals, and the legal transfer of trademark rights.
Protecting both parties from any future claims or disputes and ensuring the right of each party to use the mark within Saudi law.
Enhancing brand value and flexibility in investment or business expansion.

Service difficulties and challenges

It is necessary to complete all legal documents and contracts correctly to avoid disruption or rejection of the application.
The necessity of official publication to ensure the completion of legal procedures and the effective transfer of ownership.
Administrative fees may be relatively high, especially when the trademark has multiple categories or contracts are complex
requiredDocuments: The commercial register of the new owner and the old owner.
Brand image.
The national ID of the new owner and the old owner.
A statement of waiver certified by the Chamber of Commerce.
The national address of the new owner and the old owner.
serviceConditions: 1-The trademark must be owned by the person who wishes to assign it, and the trademark protection period must be valid.

2- Ownership of a trademark may be transferred to others by any event or act transferring ownership. The act must be in writing and not be intended to mislead the public, especially regarding the nature or source of products or services.
3- Assignment of trademark ownership is not considered to have any legal effects for others, except after that assignment has been announced and noted in the commercial registry.

services.name: Certificate of filing for a trademark
services.shortDescription: A service available to the customer only during the trademark publication period. It states that the trademark has been accepted and certifies that the trademark whose data is indicated has been accepted and is still under procedure.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The trademark filing certificate service is an official service issued by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property to the client during the period of publishing the trademark and before the final decision on its registration. The filing certificate states that the mark has been initially accepted by the Authority and states all the trademark data. It is considered a legal document used to prove the priority of filing and the applicant’s entitlement to the trademark during the publication period, especially when applying in other countries to benefit from the international priority right in accordance with intellectual property agreements.

The trademark filing certificate is used when needed to prove the initiation of legal procedures before official authorities, or in the event of applying for international protection, or to confront any infringement of the mark during the procedures stage.

The importance of the trademark filing certificate service

Official proof of the start of trademark registration procedures to ensure the right of priority locally and internationally in accordance with the Madrid Agreements and others.
Facilitating the submission of applications in other countries within the six-month period by attaching the approved filing certificate, which supports the protection of the mark globally.
Protecting the mark during the publication period from infringement or illegal exploitation, considering the certificate as legal evidence of initial ownership.
Supporting the owner’s position in any legal or administrative claims that may arise before the final registration decision is issued.
The speed in issuing the certificate electronically and the possibility of submitting it to government and private agencies with ease and reliability.

Difficulties related to obtaining or issuing a trademark filing certificate

The certificate is only issued during the publication period, that is, after the application is accepted and before the final registration decision. It cannot be obtained if the publication period has expired or the application is not initially accepted.
Some customers may face technical difficulties in accessing the platform or downloading the digital version of the certificate, especially during times of pressure or electronic updates.
The certificate does not constitute a final registration of the mark, and therefore its value may be forfeited if a legal objection is issued or the authority refuses to register the mark for a legal reason.
Procedural requirements such as entering accurate data and matching between the application and the certificate, and the importance of attaching a certified translation when used internationally.
It is necessary to submit the certificate to international bodies within the regulatory period to guarantee the right of priority, otherwise the applicant loses some rights outside the Kingdom.
requiredDocuments: Image of the brand.
A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: Valid trademark.

services.name: Brand renewal
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the applicant to renew trademark protection for ten years.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
A trademark renewal service that allows the owner of a registered trademark in Saudi Arabia to maintain the right to legal protection and the continuity of his ownership of his trademark after the expiration of the original protection period (ten years from the date of registration). The service is implemented electronically through the website of the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property, where the applicant - an individual or establishment - is granted the right to extend protection of the mark for a similar additional period without re-examining the mark or submitting new documents, with speedy implementation once the documents are completed and fees are paid. The process begins by entering the electronic portal, choosing the service, filling out the mark data and submitting the application, then paying the renewal fees and publishing the renewed mark data in the official mark bulletin. There is a grace period to renew the mark extending up to six months after completion for an additional fee.

The importance of trademark renewal service

Protecting the trademark from being dropped or exploited by others while ensuring that the right remains exclusive to the owner.
Ease of renewing protection electronically without the need for new procedures, which maintains business continuity and legal confidence in products or services.
Supporting business expansion plans locally and internationally, where the renewed brand is required when awarding franchises or supply contracts.
Maintaining the balance and value of the mark, and avoiding legal risks associated with not renewing on time.
Enabling the owner to invoke his legal rights before the judiciary and protection authorities when a dispute or imitation arises.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing a trademark renewal

Permanent loss of legal protection if the renewal is not completed within the grace period, allowing it to be registered by others or loss of rights.
It is mandatory to pay significant fees, especially in the event of delay (regular fees are 5,500 riyals and late fees are 6,500 riyals, in addition to a publishing fee of 1,000 riyals).
Some owners face technical or organizational difficulties in preparing digital data or accessing the electronic platform.
Requiring that the renewal and amendment process for any licenses derived from the mark be completed at the end of the mark’s protection period so that the license remains legally valid.
If a mark exceeds the grace period without renewal, the mark is struck from the register and can only be restored after a completely new registration procedure.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
A copy of the trademark registration certificate.
If the applicant is an agent, he needs a mark renewal agency.
ID photo.
serviceConditions: Service fees Renewal fees (if the renewal is within the last year of protection) 5500 Renewal fees (if the renewal is within 6 months from the date of expiration of protection) 6500, Publication fees 1000.

services.name: Record an audio tag
services.shortDescription: A service that allows the user to record an audio tag

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The sound mark registration service represents a specialized service within the services of the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property, and aims to protect non-traditional marks that consist of distinctive sounds used to distinguish goods or services from others in the Saudi market. These signs include musical notes, logo sounds, or any unique sound signature used to promote and identify products or companies. Registration gives the owner the exclusive right to use this audio mark and legally prosecute anyone who infringes its rights. 

The process of registering the sound mark takes place electronically through the platform of the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property, where the user begins by submitting an application to register the sound mark attached to the audio sample (in the form of a musical note, an MP3 audio file, or a detailed description). The application includes the owner's information and the type of goods or services to which the sound tag will be associated. The application is then subjected to initial and specialized examination by the Authority, and the mark is published in the Official Gazette for a period determined by the regulations to allow objection. If no objections are received, a certificate of registration for the sound mark is granted, which protects it for a period of 10 years, subject to renewal.

The importance of audio mark registration service

Protecting the intellectual rights of amazing and new audio brands, which enhances the intellectual property of establishments and individuals.
Differentiating products or services with a unique audio style increases the strength of the brand and its marketing value.
Enabling the owner to have legal defense against exploitation or imitation of the sound mark locally and internationally.
Supporting innovation in marketing and advertising methods using modern audio signs.
Registration of a sound mark facilitates its international registration through international agreements, once available, and the expansion of rights therein.

Difficulties related to achieving or issuing a sound mark registration

It is necessary to provide the audio sample in specific and clear formats to evaluate recordability.
The recording may be rejected if the sound tag is not distinctive or similar to previously recorded sounds.
The period of studying the application and publishing may take time and requires continuous follow-up.
Legal complications in the event of objections or disputes related to the distinctiveness of the sound mark or the limits of its use.
Limited awareness and experience of some users regarding recording and managing audio tags.
requiredDocuments: Attach a sheet music audio clip.
Attach a written description of the sheet music.
Required data: (email - Absher mobile number - national ID - national address).
serviceConditions: A statement of all the musical elements to be recorded, and the precise musical writing of the elements that make up the audio score (melody, rhythm, speed, harmony, musical instruments,.....).

services.name: Register a collective mark
services.shortDescription: Registration of collective marks that are used to distinguish between goods or services of establishments belonging to members belonging to a specific entity that has legal personality.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The collective trademark registration service is a specialized service granted by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property to legal persons or groups that include members affiliated with a specific entity that has a legal personality, with the aim of distinguishing the products or services of these members from others within the Saudi market. The collective mark is used to indicate the source and quality of goods or services produced or provided by this group, and the group has the right to monitor the use of the mark, ensuring the safety of the products and compliance with legal and regulatory standards. 

The registration process begins by logging into the authority’s portal, where the applicant (who is often a legal entity, association, union, or professional federation) fills out a form that includes data for the mark, the entity, and group members. The process is completely electronic for the convenience of customers, along with attaching the statute or regulation that specifies the conditions for using the mark, and how to monitor the quality system and other controls. The collective mark is subject to examination and audit by the Authority, and its data is officially published to allow objection from others. After passing the publication stage without objections, an official registration certificate is issued, valid for ten years and subject to renewal.

The importance of the collective trademark registration service

Enabling associations, unions and federations to protect the reputation and quality of their members’ products under a unified legal umbrella.
Enhancing confidence in collectively branded products and services among consumers within the Saudi market.
Organizing quality control of products and services within the relevant entity in a way that protects the rights of consumers and group members alike.
Supporting commercial and investment cooperation of collective entities through standardization and legal differentiation of the mark.
Promoting economic development by eliminating counterfeit products and pooling the efforts of members within a unified legal framework.

Difficulties related to achieving or issuing a collective mark registration

Complexities in preparing regulatory documents that explain the controls for using the mark within the group.
The need for careful comparison with other signs to avoid similarity and rejection.
The possibility of objection by other parties during publication, leading to delay or modification.
Managing and following up on the quality control requirements and system imposed by the Authority during the trademark registration period.
Restrictions on transferring ownership of a collective mark or amending its use system require the approval of the group members and the authority.
requiredDocuments: Attach an exact copy of the bylaws of the union, organization, or public institution requesting registration, along with the amendments that may have been made to it, provided that it includes the following: A statement of the category of persons entitled to use the mark and their relationship to the applicant. A copy of the requirements for the use of the collective mark for goods and services. An acknowledgment that the applicant is or will conduct precise control over the use of the collective mark by its affiliated members. How members qualify for membership in that body.
Required data: (email - Absher mobile number - national ID - national address).
serviceConditions: Indicate in the registration application that it is a collective mark.
All documents must be notarized and a duly certified power of attorney must be submitted or certified by an apostille and translated into Arabic.

services.name: Registration of inspection or monitoring mark
services.shortDescription: The control or inspection mark is intended to indicate the procedure of monitoring or inspection. Legal persons who undertake monitoring or inspection of certain goods or services in terms of their source, components of their composition, method of manufacture, quality, nature, or any other characteristic can register the mark.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The examination or monitoring mark registration service is a specialized service provided by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property, and aims to protect the marks used by legal persons to monitor or examine the quality or characteristics of some goods or services that they provide. This mark is used to indicate that the products or services have undergone special conditions or quality inspection and technical standards specified by a certain body, which enhances confidence among consumers and ensures the safety and quality of what is classified or sold within this category.

Registration begins via the Authority’s electronic platform by submitting a registration application that includes the applicant’s data, and the sample or design of the examination mark, explaining the nature and scope of the examination or control covered by the mark. The mark is subject to legal and technical examination, then published in the Official Gazette to allow objection. If no objection occurs, a registration license is issued along with an official certificate suitable for legal use. The trademark protection period is ten years, renewable.

The inspection or monitoring mark registration service represents a guarantee of quality and reliability in the Saudi market, and acts as an important barrier to protecting the rights of producers and consumers alike in an organized and developed commercial environment.

The importance of the inspection or monitoring mark registration service

Providing legal protection for quality inspection marks that distinguish products with approved specifications.
Supporting customer and consumer confidence in the quality of products or services bearing the mark.
Allowing the competent authorities to control the use of the mark and ensure that the products comply with the control conditions.
Enhancing the reputation of establishments that adhere to quality and reliability standards in local and international markets.
Regulating the market and reducing fraud by providing official means to distinguish products according to documented standards.

Difficulties related to achieving or issuing the registration of an inspection or control mark

The accuracy and distinctiveness of the submitted mark is necessary to ensure its acceptance by the Authority and its lack of similarity to other marks.
Applicants must provide clear and proven technical data on the inspection processes covered by the mark.
Approval may be delayed due to objections or the need for additional reviews.
Annual or periodic update requirements to demonstrate continuing conditions of monitoring or inspection.
Technical difficulties users may encounter in dealing with the electronic platform sometimes due to system pressure or updates.
requiredDocuments: Attach an exact copy of the bylaws of the union, organization, or public institution requesting registration, with the amendments that may have been made to it, provided that it includes: A - A declaration that the control or inspection mark will be used by anyone other than the applicant and that the applicant will not engage in marketing or production of any goods or services on which this mark is used. It will not engage in discrimination policies that would prevent the use of the mark by third parties that meet the specifications set by the owner.
Attach a declaration that the control or inspection mark certifies the quality, types or advantages of the goods or services and that it does not indicate the source of the goods or services.

Attach documents specifying the specifications and standards applied by the application filing entity in order to monitor the use of the mark and proving that the application entity is practicing an approved quality assessment program.
A certificate showing the merits of the goods or services certified or required to be certified.
Required data: (email - Absher mobile number - national ID - national address).
serviceConditions: All documents must be duly authenticated, certified, and translated into Arabic.

services.name: Registering the trademark of public benefit bodies and professional institutions
services.shortDescription: It is a mark registered for non-commercial purposes, such as logos used by professional organizations to distinguish their correspondence or to serve as badges for their members.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The trademark registration service for public benefit bodies and professional institutions is a specialized service provided by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property. It aims to protect marks used by non-commercial bodies and institutions, such as charities, public benefit associations, professional institutions, and unions, to distinguish their official correspondence or as insignia expressing their membership. These marks are treated as collective trademarks, giving the entity the exclusive right to use them within the scope of its official activity in accordance with the regulations and laws of the Kingdom. 

The registration process takes place through the authority’s electronic platform, where the entity (body or institution) submits an application to register the mark, carrying legal documents such as the statute or regulation that regulates the use of the mark, and the purposes of the authority or institution, with an accurate description of the required mark. The mark is subject to legal and technical examination and then published in the official newspaper to allow objections to be submitted. After passing the publication stage, an official registration certificate is issued, which is valid for 10 years and is renewable.

The trademark registration service for public benefit bodies and professional institutions provides legal protection for marks that express their official identity, and contributes to regulating the relationship of those bodies with members and the public within a clear and documented legal environment.

The importance of the trademark registration service for public benefit bodies and professional institutions

Official and legalization of logos and symbols that distinguish professional bodies and institutions.
Enhancing confidence and credibility in the correspondence and activities that these bodies represent before members and the public.
Protecting the rights to use the mark by the entity and not using it illegally.
Supporting the corporate identity of non-profit and professional organizations by officially documenting their brand.
Facilitating official identification of public benefit institutions and bodies in the Saudi market.

Difficulties related to achieving or issuing the registration of a mark for public benefit bodies and professional institutions

Complexities in preparing the necessary regulatory documents that clarify the use of the mark and its conditions.
The mark must undergo a careful examination to ensure that it is distinctive and not similar to other marks.
Possibility of delaying procedures due to objections or the need for amendments to the application.
Bodies and institutions must adhere to the terms of use of the mark and monitor it in order to preserve legal rights.
Limited use of these marks for non-commercial purposes and within legally defined frameworks.
requiredDocuments: Beneficiary body system if the applicant is a public benefit body.
Attach the public institution’s bylaws if the applicant is a public institution.
Attach the professional institution system to professional institutions.
Required data: (email - Absher mobile number - national ID - national address).
serviceConditions: If a trademark is requested to be registered for non-commercial purposes, this must be indicated in the registration application.

services.name: Modify the address of the trademark owner
services.shortDescription: A service that allows the trademark owner to modify the title of the trademark.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of amending the address of the trademark owner is a service provided by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property. It enables the trademark owner to update or modify his address officially registered with the authority, which ensures the accuracy and validity of the information related to the trademark, helps protect the legal rights of the owner, and ensures official communication with him in accordance with Saudi systems. The service is implemented entirely electronically through the authority’s platform, and provides a quick and reliable update of all owner data related to the address.

The process is completed by entering the trademark portal, choosing the trademark data modification service, then submitting an application that includes the new data and attaching supporting documents such as a copy of the national ID or passport and proof of the new address. An invoice is then issued for the payment, and the data is officially amended after the payment is confirmed and passes the initial inspection. Notification of completion is sent and the mark registry is updated centrally.

The service of amending the address of the trademark owner usually takes a relatively short time compared to services related to registering or transferring ownership of a trademark or issuing new licenses, because it is an amendment to existing data and not a new establishment or complex procedures.

Duration of the procedure: The address modification request is often reviewed and approved within a period of 1 to 3 business days via the electronic platform, provided that the correct application is completed and the required fees are paid without technical problems or lack of documents.

The importance of the service of amending the address of the trademark owner

Ensuring the accuracy of the owner’s official data and constantly updating it to reflect reality.
Facilitating legal and administrative communication with the Authority, government agencies and third parties.
Avoid losing rights or privileges associated with the mark due to incorrect or outdated information.
Supporting trademark protection procedures and carefully following up on legal and technical entitlements.
Providing an easy digital service that speeds up application completion and reduces the need for paper reviews.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing an amendment to the title of the owner of the mark

The necessity of providing official and approved proof of the new address may require external documentation.
The application may be rejected or delayed if there are errors in the attached documents or if they do not match the official data.
Technical difficulties with the electronic platform at peak times or during system updates.
The importance of coordinating with updates to national records and data compatibility with other government agencies.
It is necessary to alert owners to update their data within the legal deadlines to avoid problems.
requiredDocuments: Image of the brand.
A copy of the commercial register.
ID photo.
serviceConditions: Valid trademark.

services.name: Modify the name of the trademark owner
services.shortDescription: A service that allows the trademark owner to modify the name of the trademark owner.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The trademark owner name modification service is an electronic service provided by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property, which enables the trademark owner to update or change the name of the official owner registered in the trademark data. This amendment allows the legal correction of information to ensure compatibility with the owner’s current personal or institutional data and to verify the validity of the registration and its official transfer within the frameworks of Saudi regulations.

The process of amending the name of the trademark owner is done by logging into the authority’s electronic portal, choosing the trademark information amendment service, filling out the new name amendment form and attaching supporting documents such as the entity’s commercial register or the individual’s national ID, then submitting the application and paying the prescribed fees. After the application is examined and approved, the owner's details are officially updated in the Trademark Register, and processing notices are issued to the owner.

The processing time for a request to amend the name of the trademark owner is usually relatively short compared to services for registering or transferring trademark ownership, because it is an amendment to existing data and not a new registration or complete transfer of ownership.

Duration of the procedure: The amendment takes from 1 to 3 working days if the documents are completed and the fees are paid, with a quick electronic review from the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property to ensure the validity and accuracy of the data.

The importance of the service of amending the name of the trademark owner

Ensure that official owner information is up to date and that legal records associated with the mark are correct.
Facilitating legal and administrative communication with the Authority and government agencies when needed.
Supporting the sustainability of the legal protection of the mark and avoiding any problems in ownership or use of the mark.
Facilitating the implementation of other procedures such as transferring ownership or licensing the use of the mark in the future.
Providing an easy digital service that reduces the owner’s time and effort in amending his official data.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing an amendment to the name of the trademark owner

It is necessary to attach certified and valid official documents to prove the new name.
The application may be rejected if the data provided is different or there is a discrepancy in the records.
Technical requirements that may hinder uploading documents or completing the payment process through the platform.
It is necessary to coordinate with other government agencies to ensure complete updating of relevant data.
Sometimes the amendment may require updating contracts or agreements associated with the mark in accordance with the new data.
requiredDocuments: Data of the mark to be amended and the requirements attached.
Image of the brand.
A copy of the commercial register.
ID photo.
serviceConditions: Valid trademark.

services.name: Request a license to use the trademark
services.shortDescription: A service that allows the trademark owner to license a person/organization to use his trademark for all or part of one category.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of requesting a license to use a trademark is a service that allows the owner of a registered trademark to grant the right to use the trademark to another person, entity or institution according to an official licensing agreement, whether this is for the entire trademark or for part of the registered categories. Licensing is one of the most important means of protecting and expanding the brand in the market, and opens the way for commercial franchising, distribution and agency opportunities in Saudi Arabia and abroad, while ensuring the legal regulation of this relationship in compliance with intellectual property systems. 

The process is completed by submitting an electronic application through the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property platform and choosing the licensing service to use the mark. The applicant shall attach a copy of the licensing or disposition agreement, a copy of the commercial registry of the second party, the trademark certificate, and any supporting documents depending on the type of relationship. After examining the application, payment of the regulatory fees is required and approval is issued via an official email. The mark is then transformed into a record containing the data of the licensed party and its use of the specified categories, with the possibility of canceling or terminating the license later by the same mechanism.

The importance of the trademark license application service

It allows the trademark owner to benefit from his trademark through franchising and expansion without giving up ownership.
Protecting the legal grant of a license prevents the exploitation of the mark without the consent of the original party and facilitates the establishment of the rights of both parties.
Supporting the exchange of experiences, product development, and expanding the geographical and service coverage of the brand.
Facilitating entry into government tenders and contracts, as an official license for the mark is sometimes required.
Organizing the financial and legal relationship between the trademark owner and the licensee according to a detailed, documented contract.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing a license application to use the trademark

The necessity of having a clear and correct legal agreement that includes all details of use, including the duration and scope of the license.
The procedure requires approval and scrutiny by the Authority and may be delayed in the event of a lack of documents or a conflict over conditions.
Service fees may be relatively high (3,000 riyals government fees + additional legal fees).
The possibility of a dispute arising later regarding the terms of use or termination of the license unless it is fully clarified in the agreement.
The need to update the data of both parties and the mark’s records continuously during the license period
requiredDocuments: Data of the mark to be licensed.
Image of the brand.
A copy of the commercial register.
ID photo.
serviceConditions: Valid trademark.

services.name: Cancellation of a trademark
services.shortDescription: A service that allows the trademark owner to cancel various types of trademarks in response to the desire of the trademark owner or in implementation of a ruling to cancel a trademark. (Delisting means completely deleting the trademark from the specified category, and it is of two types (voluntary / court).

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The trademark cancellation service is a regulatory service provided by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property, which allows the owner or the competent authority to cancel and liquidate the trademark registration from the trademark registry permanently, whether at the request of the trademark owner himself (voluntary cancellation), or based on a judicial or regulatory ruling or decision (forced cancellation). Cancellation of a trademark is an important measure to protect the legal system and to get rid of abandoned, infringing, or ineffective marks in the Saudi market. It means that the trademark is no longer subject to legal protection after cancellation.
 
Service procedures begin by submitting an electronic application to the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property, which includes the reasons for delisting and supporting records such as a trademark certificate, a statement of non-use, or court ruling documents. Any interested party has the right to request deletion in cases such as:

Failure to use the mark for five consecutive years without a legitimate excuse.
Registering the mark according to fraud, false information, or contrary to public order and morals.
The expiration of the trademark’s protection period without renewal, or the issuance of a decision prohibiting dealing with its owner.
Requests go through examination and regular procedures, and in judicial cases, a lawsuit is required to be submitted to the competent court, and the decision is reviewed before the Board of Grievances if there is a dispute.

The importance of the trademark cancellation service

Cleaning the trademark registry of abandoned or unused marks to support innovation and commercial transparency.
Protecting the rights of third parties from conflicts and infringement resulting from the retention of ineffective marks.
Achieving compliance with trademark regulations in Saudi Arabia and enhancing confidence in the intellectual property market.
Enabling bodies to issue rulings and implement decisions to cancel trademark protection in the event of violations or the expiration of the legal period.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing a trademark cancellation

It is necessary to prove the reason for deletion in a legal and acceptable manner before the authority or court, such as providing evidence of non-use or the existence of a legal violation.
The application is sometimes subject to dispute or objection from other parties who have an interest in the continuation of the mark.
The need to resolve any outstanding legal issues or existing uses before implementing the final delisting.
Legal impacts on the owner’s customers and business partners after delisting, which requires informing them and formally documenting the procedures.
Permanent loss of legal protection for the mark, and the possibility of it being registered by another party in the future.
requiredDocuments: Data of the mark to be deleted.
Image of the brand.
A copy of the commercial register.
ID photo.
serviceConditions: Valid trademark.

services.name: Brand mortgage
services.shortDescription: A service provided to the trademark owner to mortgage his trademark to a person/institution by agreement between the two parties, in accordance with a legal contract in which the first party grants ownership of the trademark to the second party, with the first party retaining its ownership under certain conditions.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The trademark mortgage service is an official service provided by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property, which allows the trademark owner to place his mark as a legal guarantee (financial or legal guarantee) for the benefit of another person or institution under a legally recognized mortgage agreement. With this procedure, the mortgagee has a legal right to the trademark in accordance with the terms of the mortgage contract, in exchange for obtaining financing, settling obligations, or as a guarantee in partnership contracts. The mortgagor retains ownership of the mark but grants the mortgagee the rights of disposition specified in the agreement until the obligations are paid or the mortgage is officially released. 

The mortgage process is carried out by submitting an electronic application from the trademark owner to the Authority, accompanied by:

A certified copy of the mortgage contract stating the name of the trademark, its number, and the categories included;
Data of both parties and reasons for the mortgage.
Brand certificate.
And any supporting documents such as commercial registry or national ID.

After examination and payment of fees, the Authority registers the mortgage incident, and this appears officially in the trademark registry. Ownership of the mark may not be transferred or disposed of without the approval of the notary public or the mortgaged party until the mortgage is officially released.

The importance of brand mortgage service

Enabling companies and individuals to obtain financing or financial guarantees through a protected trademark, which supports the flow of business without compromising ownership.
Protecting both parties legally by documenting rights and responsibilities, and ensuring that the conditions of the mortgage contract are met.
Raising the investment value of brands as assets that can be financially channeled into partnership contracts, financing, or merger deals.
Enhancing confidence in commercial transactions, as the mortgage is approved as official proof in lawsuits or before banks and regulatory authorities.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing a trademark mortgage

It is necessary to draft an accurate legal mortgage contract that includes all terms, conditions and regulatory requirements.
The Authority carefully examines and audits all documents, which may delay the procedure if deficiencies or discrepancies are found.
It is not permissible to dispose of the mark or transfer its ownership throughout the mortgage period except after the mortgagee’s approval or the mortgage is officially released in a documented form.
Service fees may be relatively high compared to other modification services, and depend on the type and value of the mortgage.
In cases of dispute or financial difficulty, the mark may be used to pay a debt or obligations in favor of the mortgaged party, which means losing some rights to the original owner until payment is met.
requiredDocuments: If the applicant is an individual, the following must be submitted: 1- A mortgage contract from the owner of the mark (which must stipulate the name of the mark, the registration certificate number, its date, and its category) 2- A power of attorney from the buyer to complete the mortgage procedures for the mark 3- Attach a notarized power of attorney or authorization for the person who has the capacity to apply “a duly certified power of attorney or certified by an apostille certificate.” -If any-.

If the applicant is an institution, the following must be submitted: 1- A mortgage contract from the trademark owner on his publications, authenticated by the Chamber of Commerce (it must stipulate the name of the trademark, registration certificate number, date, and category). 2- Submitting a letter from the mortgagee to the buyer (the buyer) on his printed documents to the administration requesting the transfer of ownership, mentioning the name of the mark, registration certificate number, date and category. 3- Attach a notarized power of attorney or authorization for the person who has the capacity to apply, “a duly certified power of attorney or certified by an apostille certificate.” -If any-.

If the applicant is a company, the following must be submitted: 1- A mortgage contract from the trademark owner on his publications, authenticated by the Chamber of Commerce (it must stipulate the name of the trademark, registration certificate number, date, and category). 2- The contract of incorporation of the mortgaged company, to ensure the validity of signing the attached mortgage contract. 3- Submitting a letter from the mortgagee to him (the buyer) on his printed documents to the administration requesting the transfer of ownership, mentioning the name of the trademark, registration certificate number, date and category. 4- Attach a certified power of attorney or authorization for those who have the capacity to apply, “a duly certified power of attorney or certified by an apostille certificate.” -If any-.
serviceConditions: Availability of required documents according to the type of applicant (individual - institution - company).

services.name: Cancellation of the license to use the trademark
services.shortDescription: A service that allows the trademark owner or licensee to cancel the license to use the trademark after submitting the necessary documents

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The trademark cancellation service is a regulatory procedure provided by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property, and allows the trademark owner or the licensed party to terminate the legal permission that allows the use of the trademark within the previous licensing agreement. Cancellation may be implemented by agreement of both parties, or based on the expiration of the contract term or revocation of the license, and will result in the licensed party’s right to officially use the trademark being removed from the Authority’s register, with notification of all relevant parties thereof. 

The process is done electronically through the Authority’s portal:

The trademark owner or licensee enters and selects the “Cancel License to Use the Trademark” service.
Attach supporting documents such as a license termination agreement, termination document, or contract expiration statement.
Pays the regular fees.
The application is examined by the Authority, and if it meets the requirements, the trademark registry is updated to cancel the second party’s right and a notification is sent to the other party.
The trademark cancellation service ensures protection of the trademark, renews legal control over it, and supports transparency and compliance in commercial transactions between establishments and individuals in the Kingdom.

The importance of the trademark cancellation service

Protecting the rights of the original owner of the mark and restoring control over the use of the mark after the license expires or is revoked.
Ensuring compliance with legal and contractual systems when wishing to terminate the licensing relationship with another party.
Preventing the continued use of the mark illegally or in violation of the contract after the end of the relationship between the two parties.
Regulating the trademark registry and protecting the interests of all parties and regulatory authorities.
Supporting the resolution of commercial and legal disputes related to the expiration of licenses.

Difficulties related to achieving or issuing a cancellation of the license to use the trademark

It is necessary to attach an official document explaining the reason for cancellation (severance agreement, expiration notice, court ruling...).
The possibility of disputes or reservations arising from the second party regarding the cancellation procedure.
Slowness of the procedure in the event of incomplete documents or the need for a financial and legal settlement between the two parties.
It is necessary to update all contracts and legal files to ensure the proper termination of the license in all relevant authorities.
Sometimes the costs and fees of the procedure change depending on the type and duration of the agreement, and may require additional legal scrutiny.
requiredDocuments: Data of the mark whose license is to be cancelled.
Image of the brand.
A copy of the commercial register.
ID photo.
serviceConditions: Valid trademark.

services.name: Release a trademark mortgage
services.shortDescription: A service provided to the owner of the trademark or to a second party to release the mortgage on the trademark and return its ownership to its primary owner.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The trademark release service is a regulatory procedure provided by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property, which allows the trademark owner or the mortgage party (the beneficiary of the mortgage) to terminate the official mortgage agreement on the trademark and return its ownership and powers to dispose of it to its original owner. The mortgage is released when the debt or mortgage obligation is paid in full according to the terms of the contract, or by agreement between the two parties, or by settling related disputes, which frees the trademark from the restrictions that were imposed on it and restores its full legal status. 

The process of releasing the mortgage is carried out by entering the Authority’s portal and submitting an electronic application in the name of the owner or mortgagee stating the tag number, information of the parties, and the reason for releasing the mortgage, along with attaching supporting documents (such as an acknowledgment of payment of the debt, the agreement of the two parties, or a court ruling to release the mortgage). The authority reviews the documents, and after approval is issued, the mark registry is updated and the mortgage incident is officially cancelled. Full rights return to the owner and he can transfer ownership of the mark or use it for any purpose without the need for the mortgagee’s approval, as the rights are liquidated in an orderly and legal manner.

The importance of the trademark release service

The original owner regains full legal rights and free disposal of his trademark.
Ending the legal burdens and restrictions on the mark and settling the financial obligations associated with it.
Enabling the owner to transfer ownership of the mark or invest it again without additional requirements.
Officially document the trademark status with all governmental and banking authorities.
Resolving potential disputes between the two parties after the end of the mortgage period and ensuring everyone’s rights.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing a trademark mortgage release service

It is necessary to attach complete documents proving the termination of the mortgage (mortgage release contract, payment acknowledgment, court ruling...).
In the event of disputes, the procedure may require legal or judicial proceedings to adjudicate between the parties.
Delay or refusal of the procedure in case of lack of documents or mismatch of official data.
Updating the transactions resulting from the mark with all relevant parties, such as banks or commercial partners, after releasing the mortgage.
Sometimes administrative costs or fees are high or vary depending on the size of the contract and the type of mark.
requiredDocuments:
If the applicant is an individual, the following must be submitted: 

1- The mortgage release contract from the trademark owner (it must stipulate the name of the trademark, the registration certificate number, its date, and its category).
2- Authorizing the buyer to complete the procedures for releasing the mortgage on the mark. 
3- Attach a notarized power of attorney or authorization for the person who has the capacity to apply, “a duly certified power of attorney or certified by an apostille certificate.” -If any-.

If the applicant is an institution, the following must be submitted:

 1- A contract to release the mortgage from the trademark owner on his printed materials, authenticated by the Chamber of Commerce (it must stipulate the name of the trademark, the registration certificate number, its date, and its category). 
2- Submitting a letter from the person whose mortgage has been released (the buyer) on his prints to the administration requesting the transfer of ownership, mentioning the name of the trademark, registration certificate number, date, and category. 
3- Attach a notarized power of attorney or authorization for the person who has the capacity to apply, “a duly certified power of attorney or certified by an apostille certificate.” -If any-.

If the applicant is a company, the following must be submitted: 

1- A contract to release the mortgage from the trademark owner on his printed materials, authenticated by the Chamber of Commerce (it must stipulate the name of the trademark, the registration certificate number, its date, and its category). 
2- The incorporation contract of the mortgaged company, to ensure the validity of signing the attached mortgage release contract. 
3- Submitting a letter from the person whose mortgage has been released (the buyer) on his publications to the administration requesting the transfer of ownership, mentioning the name of the trademark, registration certificate number, date, and category. 4- Attach a notarized power of attorney or authorization for the person who has the capacity to apply, “a duly certified power of attorney or certified by an apostille certificate.” -If any-.
serviceConditions: Availability of required documents according to the type of applicant (individual - institution - company).

services.name: Request a true copy of the registration certificate
services.shortDescription: Satisfying the desire of the trademark owner to obtain a copy of the trademark registration certificate or any document submitted with the application.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of requesting an exact copy of the registration certificate is a service that enables the trademark owner or his representative to obtain an official, certified copy of the trademark registration certificate or any document submitted with the application to the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property. This copy is used as official and reliable evidence in legal, commercial and financial transactions, and is submitted electronically through the Authority’s platform, allowing ease and speed in obtaining the document while ensuring the credibility of the data. 

The process begins by submitting an electronic application through the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property portal. The application is accompanied by the owner’s personal or commercial data, the registration certificate number or trademark data, and payment of the prescribed fees. After reviewing the application and ensuring the accuracy of the data, the Authority issues an exact copy of the certificate in an electronic, printable format, bearing the electronic documentation and the Authority’s approved signature. It can be used in dealing with government or private entities, during licensing, or filing legal cases, and comes with an official guarantee of the legality and quality of the document.

The importance of the service of requesting a true copy of the registration certificate

Providing an official, certified and clear copy of the trademark certificate available electronically and at any time.
Enhancing reliability and legal and monthly acceptance in all official dealings and government and private correspondence.
Facilitating procedures that require proof of ownership of a mark or legal assets in multiple entities.
Enabling trademark owners to quickly and easily prove their rights before customers or in legal disputes.
Save time and effort by replacing the need for paper copies with official electronic copies.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing a request for a true copy of the registration certificate

It is necessary to ensure that accurate and consistent data are provided to those approved by the Authority to avoid rejection or postponement of the application.
You may encounter some technical difficulties in downloading or receiving the digital copy during times of pressure on the platform.
The need to renew the original certificate or validity of the mark registration to ensure the issuance of an exact copy.
Fees incurred for the service, which may vary depending on the type and level of documentation required.
Dealing with third parties that may request additional certifications or special follow-up transactions for the quality of copies.
requiredDocuments: Brand data that requires an image.
A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: Valid trademark.

services.name: Search for a trademark
services.shortDescription: A service that allows the user to search for a trademark in a category specified by the customer for the purpose of ensuring that the trademark has not been registered in the required category.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The trademark search service is an electronic service that enables the user to search for the presence of a trademark registered with the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property in a specific commercial category specified by the customer. This service helps to ensure that the mark that the user wishes to register has not been previously registered in the same category, or to check the status of an existing mark to inquire about its legal and administrative details. The search is carried out through the official trademark database provided by the Authority, which supports the integrity of the trademark registration and compliance with legal systems by avoiding similarity to registered trademarks. 

The process begins by entering the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property portal, choosing the trademark search service, then entering the trademark name, registration number, or commercial category classification, to display detailed results that include the status of the trademark, its availability, or the presence of previous objections to it. The service is available to individuals and companies for free, and is also used in the design stages of new brands or legal verification of competing trademarks.

The importance of a trademark search service

It ensures that a similar mark will not be registered repeatedly in the same commercial category, which reduces the possibility of rejection or legal disputes in the future.
It contributes to accelerating and improving the process of accurately registering new marks by providing advance and updated information.
It helps investors and business owners make sound decisions before investing time and money in developing brands.
Enhances the protection of individuals' and companies' intellectual property from imitation or unauthorized use.
It provides a comprehensive database that enables one to know the details of distinctive trademarks registered in the Saudi market.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing a trademark search service

It requires accuracy in entering the name of the brand or category to avoid inaccurate or ambiguous results.
Some users may experience technical difficulties or platform slowdowns during periods of high stress.
Users sometimes need interpretation of search results or legal advice to fully understand its implications.
Continuous updating of data and level of accuracy of the rule is required to avoid outdated or newly registered tags.
Rely on a strong Internet connection to prevent service interruptions while searching.
requiredDocuments: Data for the tag you want to search for.
A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: Valid trademark.

services.name: Modify the brand image
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the owner of a previously registered trademark to amend the trademark, provided that the amendment to the form is not essential. If the amendment is essential, a new application must be submitted.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The trademark image modification service is an official service provided by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property, and allows the trademark owner to make changes to the visual image or design of his trademark registered in the commercial registry. Possible modifications include improving the image quality, updating the design, or replacing the old image with a new image while preserving the rest of the tag data. This service aims to update the mark in line with the development of the company's identity or modern fashion while preserving the intellectual property rights and legal system of the mark. 

The amendment process is done by submitting an electronic application through the Authority’s platform, where the application is attached with a picture of the new mark, and the details of the required change can be specified, along with attaching the mark ownership documents and the original certificate. The service is subject to technical and legal review by the Authority to ensure that the new image conforms to the standards and does not conflict with other signs. After approval, the trademark registry is updated with the new image and the information related to it is officially amended within the authority’s database.

The trademark image modification service is a flexible and vital tool for preserving the visual identity of the trademark while continuing legal protection and revitalizing the commercial value of the trademark in accordance with modern international and local standards.

The importance of brand image modification service

Updating the mark to reflect the modern visual identity of the facility or product.
Preserving the owner's rights by updating the mark and not losing legal protection with image changes.
Enabling the company to adapt to market requirements and competing brands easily and flexibly.
Providing legal protection for intellectual property rights in a modern, reliable and officially documented form.
Enhancing the marketing attractiveness of the brand and enhancing customer awareness of the new brand.

Difficulties related to achieving or issuing a brand image amendment

It is necessary to present a clear and new image that conforms to the standards of the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property.
There may be a delay in examining the application if the image is not clear or there is a conflict with other signs.
Technical requirements in the electronic filing platform may hinder uploading images or editing at certain times.
Special legal requirements related to not changing the essence of the basic mark or causing similarity with other marks.
The importance of matching the updated data with the rest of the trademark’s records to avoid any systematic conflicts.
requiredDocuments: Image data to be modified.
Image of the brand.
A copy of the commercial register.

services.name: Palace products
services.shortDescription: Trademarks are classified into 45 categories according to the International Classification of Goods and Services (Nice Agreement), and each category has a list of products/services to meet the customer’s desire if he wants to delete some products/services after registering them.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The Product Limitation Service is a regulatory service provided by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property, which aims to restrict the scope of legal protection for a trademark to include only a specific category or type of products or services instead of all registered categories. This procedure is used when the mark owner wants to focus protection on specific products or reduce the legal obligations associated with protecting the mark more broadly, which reduces costs and helps direct the mark investment effectively. 

The limitation process begins by submitting an electronic application through the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property platform, which includes details of the products or services to which the owner wishes to limit protection, along with attaching supporting documents such as the original registration certificate and ownership documents. Applications are subject to legal examination, and the mark registry is amended to effectively reflect the limited scope. After acceptance, the amendment is published for a specified period of time to allow for objections, and if there are no objections, the Palace officially becomes effective and notice is issued.

The product shortening service enhances the flexibility of trademark management and makes protection more aligned with business strategies, providing owners with a good tool to focus on the most important categories and effectively address legal challenges.

The importance of product shortening service

Enabling trademark owners to determine and size the scope of protection in accordance with the actual business plan.
Reduce the costs and liabilities associated with protecting the mark over multiple unused classes.
Improve intellectual rights management and focus marketing and legal efforts on actual products or services.
Avoid legal disputes related to use in unwanted or custom categories.
Enhancing legal and business effectiveness in a competitive and changing market.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing the product shortening service

The need for clarity and accuracy in defining the products or services covered by minors to avoid rejection or objections.
The procedure may be delayed due to the period of publication and objection, which may extend for a certain period of time.
Legal complications in the event of disputes over the scope of minors or the rights of competitors.
It is necessary to coordinate with trademark records and update them continuously to keep pace with legal and commercial amendments.
Potential additional costs in the event of resorting to legal defense procedures or specialized consultations during objections.
requiredDocuments: A list of products to be removed from the trademark.
Image of the brand.
A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: Valid trademark.

services.name: Modifying the trademark data (changing the name of an agent)
services.shortDescription: A service that allows the trademark owner to modify the agent’s data

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The trademark data amendment service (changing the name of an agent) is an electronic service provided by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property, and enables the trademark owner to update the data of the authorized legal agent who represents the owner before the authority in all procedures related to the trademark. The amendment includes changing the name of the agent in the official register of the mark to ensure accurate and effective legal and administrative communication, which supports the flow of transactions and preserves the legal rights of the owner. 

The change process is done by submitting an electronic application on the Authority’s portal, attaching the new authorization for the agent, documents proving the legal relationship between the owner and the new agent, and accurate data for the agent (such as personal identification or commercial registration). The documents are subject to scrutiny by the Authority, and after the amendment is approved, the agent’s record is officially updated and the necessary notifications are sent to all relevant parties, so that the new agent becomes the legal representative of the mark in all procedures.

The service of amending trademark information (changing the name of an agent) provides a vital administrative and legal tool to ensure the smooth and complete conduct of business and regulatory procedures within the Saudi intellectual property system.

The importance of the trademark data modification service (changing agent name)

Ensuring the presence of an authorized legal representative of the owner to facilitate the completion of all legal and administrative procedures.
Protecting the owner’s rights by documenting the agent’s authorization officially and legally.
Facilitating communication and dealing with the Authority and government agencies through the officially authorized person.
Supporting the continuity of trademark registration procedures, renewals, or other amendments without delay or administrative disruption.
Providing a fast electronic service that reduces effort and time compared to paper procedures.

Difficulties related to completing or issuing an amendment to the trademark data (changing the name of an agent)

It is necessary to provide a legally valid official authorization to avoid rejection of the application.
The application may be delayed if the power of attorney documents are missing or the new agent’s data does not match.
Technical requirements within the Authority’s platform may sometimes hinder uploading documents or completing the procedure steps easily.
The need to closely follow up on the request to ensure that changes to the authority’s records are completed correctly.
The importance of keeping agent data up to date to avoid legal problems or stumbling into procedures later.
requiredDocuments: Image of the brand.
A copy of the commercial register.
Agent data: (ID, Absher mobile phone, email, and national address).
serviceConditions: Valid trademark.

services.name: Professional accreditation (engineer-architect-technical engineer).
services.shortDescription: The professional accreditation system enables the engineer to manage his personal file without the need to review the authority and implement many self-services such as requests for renewal or promotion or renewal and promotion, applying for professional tests, linking with passports for expatriates, and many other services through the electronic services portal.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The professional accreditation service is one of the basic services provided by the Saudi Council of Engineers, and aims to enable engineers, architects, and technical engineers to obtain official accreditation that proves their professionalism and competence in practicing the engineering profession within the Kingdom. This service is based on an integrated system for managing the engineer’s personal file electronically through the Authority’s portal, where a large number of self-procedures are provided, such as requesting initial accreditation, renewal, promotion, and linking membership with relevant authorities such as passports for expatriates, without the need to visit the Authority’s centers directly.

The Saudi Council of Engineers provides this system in accordance with the regulations regulating the practice of engineering professions issued by royal decree, which requires professional registration for all workers in the engineering sector, with specific exceptions for Saudi citizens and some special categories. Those approved prove their academic and practical experience by uploading the required certificates and records, and undergo approved professional tests to ensure the quality of specialization and skill.
After completing the verification and review process, the engineer will be issued a professional accreditation certificate and a membership card that can be received electronically or from the authority’s branches throughout the Kingdom, with the possibility of requesting it to be delivered by mail for a specific fee. This service is considered important for enhancing the professionalism and legal accreditation of engineers in the sector, opening broader job opportunities and regulating the practice of the profession in accordance with international standards.

The importance of professional accreditation service

It proves the engineer's professional skills and enhances his credibility before employers.
Facilitates employment in local and international engineering institutions and companies.
It helps in applying unified engineering standards and raising the quality of professional work.
It allows the Authority to monitor and follow up the performance of specialists and manage the file of each engineer.
Provides a clear database for professional cadres that facilitates organizational and development processes.

Difficulties and challenges related to the service

Challenges of proving practical and academic experiences accurately and documented.
Dealing with continuous updates to professional accreditation regulations and conditions that require continuous follow-up.
Difficulty ensuring the commitment of all engineers, especially expatriates, to registration and periodic renewal.
The constant need to improve and develop the electronic system to ensure ease of use and efficient performance.
requiredDocuments: ID photo.
A copy of the passport.
The academic certificate is certified by the Saudi Foreign Ministry.
An equivalence certificate from the Ministry of Education for those holding qualifications from outside the Kingdom.
serviceConditions: Obtaining the necessary academic qualification in one of the engineering/architectural/technical engineering specializations.
Attach the necessary documents to complete the registration process (ID/border number, passport, work identification letter, certificate from behind, academic record).

An equivalence certificate from the Ministry of Education for those holding qualifications from outside the Kingdom (for Saudis) or authentication of the certificate from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Cultural Attachés (for non-Saudis).
The engineer, architect, and technical engineer must each hold a bachelor's degree in their specializations.
Approval depends on the application according to its examination by the system.
The tuition fee from the system for the application is 500 riyals, paid once.
Government fees for membership, first year 250 riyals, second year 500 riyals, third year 750 riyals.
Signing the acknowledgment and authorization.
Payment of registration fees.​
