serviceConditions: The establishment must be existing.
The worker does not have a valid contract with Qiwi.
The worker’s work permit must be valid for more than (60) days.

Required data: (email address - mobile number linked to Absher - employee ID - bank IBAN - contract duration - salary determination - profession - academic qualification - number of working days and hours).

services.name: Contract management and documentation
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the facility owner to manage and document employee contracts

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The contract management and documentation service via the Qiwa platform is one of the basic digital services provided by the Saudi Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development, and aims to develop the human resources management sector, raise the level of transparency, and protect the rights of both parties to the employment contract within the Saudi market. This service allows establishments and companies to create, document, amend and terminate new employment contracts completely electronically without the need for paperwork or physical presence in work offices, which contributes to accelerating procedures and saving time and effort for both business owners and employees.

Using the service begins with the facility owner or human resources manager logging into the Qiwa platform, choosing the contract management and documentation service, then adding data for the facility, the worker, and contract details (contract type, salary, allowances, trial period, benefits, work start date, etc.). The contract is then sent electronically to the employee to review the terms and confirm or reject the contract or request a modification. Once approved by both parties, the contract becomes officially documented and linked to the employee’s record at the General Organization for Social Insurance.

The service supports contract documentation for Saudi and expatriate workers, and includes all types of contracts such as fixed-term, indefinite-term, partial contracts, as well as remote work contracts. The platform provides a mechanism for monitoring the status of each contract and the documentation percentages; Facilities can track undocumented or expired contracts or contracts under review, and issue periodic performance and compliance reports. The service also simplifies the procedures for terminating the contract or terminating the contractual relationship in accordance with the regulations approved by the Ministry of Human Resources, and allows the employee to follow up on his requests and rights related to the end of service and its rewards through the platform.

Among the advantages of the service are ensuring electronic archiving of all contracts, providing legal protection for the worker and the facility, ease of reviewing and amending contracts, the possibility of verifying the validity of data and achieving its consistency between government agencies, and supporting digital development and the transition to a paperless work environment. Qiwa platform emphasizes the importance of documenting contracts for all workers and that this is mandatory from the date the worker begins until the end of the relationship to guarantee the rights of all parties.

The importance of managing and documenting contracts.

Accelerating recruitment and documentation procedures and facilitating connection with social insurance.
Promoting transparency and protecting the legal rights of all parties.
Supporting ease of archiving, accessing and verifying contracts electronically.
Reducing errors and achieving compliance with the Saudi labor system.

Challenges

It is necessary to constantly complete and update worker and facility data to ensure correct documentation.
Manage contract and form changes in compliance with legislative updates.
Handling large numbers of contracts electronically during peak or training times.
requiredDocuments: ID photo.
A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: The establishment must be existing.
The worker does not have a valid contract with Qiwi.
The worker’s work permit must be valid for more than (60) days.

Required data: (email address - mobile number linked to Absher - employee ID - bank IBAN - contract duration - salary determination - profession - academic qualification - number of working days and hours).

services.name: Issuance of instant visas
services.shortDescription: This service is provided to establishments that wish to issue professional labor visas immediately without the need for paper documents or visiting one of the Ministry’s branches.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The instant visa issuance service is one of the digital services provided by the Qiwa platform of the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in Saudi Arabia. It aims to support the private sector in attracting professional workers quickly and safely according to unified and transparent electronic procedures. This service enables existing establishments that adhere to all nationalization requirements and Saudization rates to issue immediate work visas to non-Saudi workers without the need to wait long periods, submit additional documents, or visit labor offices.

The visa issuance process begins by logging into the establishment’s account on the Qiwa platform, then entering the services menu and choosing the “instant visa issuance” service. The platform provides eligibility conditions such as the presence of a valid commercial register, effective business licenses, and the absence of violations of the facility regarding the labor system or social insurance. The establishment must have a sufficient balance of visas according to the specific nationalization rates and scopes, and the government visa fees must be paid, which usually amount to 2,000 Saudi riyals for each visa.

After choosing the type of visa and entering all the required data (the worker’s nationality, religion, destination, number of visas required), the application details are added and reviewed electronically. Upon approval, an immediate visa document is issued that the facility can use to bring in the worker from outside the Kingdom through Saudi embassies. The service is effective and fast, as all procedures are completed electronically and approval is obtained immediately if the facility meets all conditions.

The service enables business owners to follow up on the details of all issued visas, manage employment data, and submit the necessary reports on compliance with Emiratisation rates. Among its advantages is that it increases the effectiveness and speed of recruitment in private sector establishments, supports the attraction of professional competencies in various specializations, reduces the administrative burden and helps improve the work experience for both parties.

The importance of the instant visa issuance service

Rapid recruitment of competencies and provision of required labor without delay.
Promote compliance with the visa regime and facilitate monitoring of employment status.
Protecting the rights of establishments and workers through clear official procedures.
Reducing administrative errors and increasing the transparency of operational processes.
Supporting the competitiveness of the Saudi private sector in attracting the best human resources.

Challenges

It is necessary to adhere to nationalization rates and have a sufficient balance of permits.
Accurately documenting workers’ data to reduce recruitment problems.
Dealing with changes and new requirements in government policies.
Ensure electronic data and procedures are constantly updated.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: Validity of work permits in unified number establishments.
Achieving compliance with documenting contracts.
Validity of the commercial register. Activities that do not require a commercial register are excluded from this condition.
The status of the facility must be current.
The scope of the green facility entity must be medium or higher (for the expansion stage only).
There are no existing notes from the wage protection system on the facility.
There is a sufficient balance for the transaction in the facility’s unified national number account at the Ministry of Interior.
The service is intended for establishments that achieve high localization rates and adhere to labor regulations.
Required data: (nationality - profession - destination - religion - passport copy)

services.name: Moving service agent
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the facility owner to transfer the service of a resident employee to it.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The worker service transfer service is an electronic service provided by the Qiwa platform of the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It aims to enable the transfer of the expatriate worker service from one facility to another in an official and smooth manner and in accordance with regulatory procedures. This service allows new employers to transfer the services of expatriate workers transferred from their previous employers, while ensuring workers’ rights and access to their data and files in an integrated manner.

The service begins with the employer or human resources manager logging into their account on the Qiwa platform, after which the “employee transfer” service is chosen and then the appropriate path for transferring the worker is determined, such as “transferring an employee from another employer” or “transferring an employee between facilities of the same employer.” The system requires entering the worker’s data and residence number, and specifying the target facility to transfer the service to. The transfer request is sent electronically to the worker via an SMS on his mobile phone for approval of the transfer, and then the approval reaches the old employer to complete the process.

The service is subject to conditions that include: The worker must not be in violation of the residency system, and the period of his stay must be less than a certain period if the service is transferred to a new facility. It is also required that there be a documented employment contract between the worker and the current employer, and that prior notice must be provided to the old employer 30 days before the contract expiration date in accordance with known transportation regulations. In the event of transferring job roles or between different employers, the platform provides comprehensive follow-up of the application status from the beginning until the issuance of the new residency.

The service provides a flexible means for companies to meet their workforce needs without the need for complex processes or legal delays. It also enhances the transparency of worker rights and employers’ rights by adhering to applicable legal regulations and systems. This system contributes to developing the Saudi labor market and increasing the effectiveness of human resources management in the private sector.

Importance of moving service worker service

Controlling and organizing the movements of expatriate workers in a way that preserves all rights.
Accelerating worker transfer procedures to quickly meet facility needs.
Improving the level of transparency and commitment to the system between workers and employers.
Providing accurate monitoring reports on transportation status and compliance with legislation.

Challenges

Ensure the worker’s approval in the electronic communication system.
Manage the time periods required for notifications and transfers.
Dealing with special cases or violations that may hinder transportation.
Follow up on system updates in accordance with new laws.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
National address.
ID photo.
serviceConditions: 1- For establishment establishments: All other entities under the unified number must be at least in the medium green range or very small green, with the exception of the entity under establishment.
2- When performing a transfer of services, the employee’s approval of the request is required, including the employment contract.
3- Your establishment belonging to the same group of establishments must not have any expired work licenses.
4- Your facility must comply with the wage protection system.
5- The transfer of employees does not exceed the permitted balance of recruitment (visas and transportation) for the level of the medium green ranges (for establishments in the expansion period)
6- Fees are calculated at 2000 riyals for the first time, 4000 riyals for the second time, and 6000 riyals for the third time or more.
7- The employee must currently work within the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
8- The employee does not have an existing transfer process (i.e. a transfer request that has not yet been completed).
9- The employee does not have a final exit visa.
10- The employee does not have a final exit work permit.

services.name: Issuing a work permit
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the facility owner to issue work permits for workers.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The work permit issuance service is an electronic service provided by the Qiwa platform, affiliated with the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in Saudi Arabia, and allows business owners to issue work permits to non-Saudi workers quickly and effectively through a unified and advanced system. This service is an essential step for registering the worker in the Ministry’s system. It enables the employer to obtain a work permit for the worker in accordance with applicable regulations and laws, which is a necessary condition before issuing residency.

The steps for issuing a work license include logging into the employer’s account on the Qiwa platform, entering the work permits section, and then choosing to issue a new license. The employer enters the required worker data, such as personal data, contract type, salary, and work period, and then the application is submitted electronically. After submitting the application, the data is audited and the license is linked to the worker’s record at the General Organization for Social Insurance. The platform allows you to follow up on the application status and provide notifications and alerts related to the license expiration date and renewal.

The service is compatible with the scopes and nationalization rates system, and it is not permitted to issue or renew licenses if the facility is in the red zone, and specific government fees apply (usually 100 Saudi riyals for each business license). The service includes support for several types of licenses, such as specific and non-specific work licenses, as well as partial contracts and remote work. The service provides legal protection for employers and workers alike, and reduces the need for paperwork and visits to labor offices.

The importance of the service of issuing a work permit.

Simplifying and accelerating the procedures for issuing work permits for foreign workers.
Ensuring compliance with labor and nationalization laws in the private sector.
Supporting labor monitoring and indexing and its system in the formal sector.
Increase the transparency of recruitment processes and reduce violations.
Strengthening the digital work environment in Saudi Arabia towards complete digital transformation.

Challenges

It is necessary to accurately update workers and employers’ data to ensure successful transactions.
Manage high volume of orders at peak times.
Keeping up with periodic amendments and updates to the Saudi labor system.
Follow up on employers’ awareness of the requirements and policies for issuing licenses.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
National address.
serviceConditions: The status of the facility must be current.
The scope of the establishment should not be in red.
The facility must be committed to the wage protection program.
The worker accepts the contract through the forces of individuals.
Required data: (email - Absher mobile number - national ID or resident ID if the facility owner is a resident).

services.name: Issuing a Saudization certificate
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the employer to issue a certificate specifying the facility’s Saudization rates.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The Saudization certificate issuance service is a basic electronic service provided by the Qiwa platform of the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It aims to issue an official certificate showing the extent of the establishment’s commitment to the percentage of labor nationalization according to the “Nitaqat” system. This certificate is one of the important documents used by establishments when applying for government tenders and obtaining financial extracts for projects. It also contributes to enhancing transparency and compliance with Saudi labor market legislation.

To obtain a Saudization certificate, the facility must be committed to a Saudization rate of no less than 50% during the twelve months preceding the date of submission of the application. The process of issuing the certificate takes place entirely electronically via the Qiwa platform, where the employer or the person responsible for human resources logs into the facility’s account and requests the issuance of the certificate, and the facility’s data and Emiratization rates are automatically audited and reviewed. If there is sufficient non-compliance, the certificate cannot be issued until the required percentage is achieved.

The Qiwa platform provides enterprises with the ability to constantly monitor the rate of Emiratisation through periodic reports, and facilitates the management of recruitment and contracting processes in accordance with approved laws. The platform also provides the ability to communicate with technical support to address any inquiries or review requests, and facilitates establishments’ access to all required official documents in a flexible and quick manner without the need for paperwork.

The importance of the Saudization certificate issuance service

Enhancing commitment to Emiratisation policies, which supports national employment and reduces unemployment.
Facilitating the completion of government procedures related to contracts and tenders.
Providing a unified and reliable mechanism to measure the facility’s commitment to localization.
Supporting raising the level of transparency between government agencies and the private sector.
Improving the business experience through automation, digitization and reducing bureaucracy.

Challenges

Vigilance and continuity of localization must be maintained to achieve the required level of commitment.
The necessity of accurately and accurately updating employee records in the system.
Effectively address non-compliance cases to avoid disruption of operations.
The need to quickly keep pace with amendments and changes in localization systems.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: The status of the facility must be current.
The facility must be in one of the ranges: Platinum, High Green, Medium Green, Low Green, or Green for Small Entities Category A.

services.name: Book an appointment for the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the employer to book an appointment to review his transaction.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The appointment booking service is an electronic service provided by the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It aims to organize and implement advance appointments to visit the various branches of the Ministry, whether to review services related to work, social development, or general civil services. This service helps reduce congestion and congestion at the Ministry's headquarters, and improves the beneficiaries' experience by facilitating access to the required service at the right time and with complete ease.

Beneficiaries can book an appointment through the Ministry’s electronic portal or through the Ministry’s applications available on various smart phone platforms. The service requires choosing the appropriate city and branch, specifying the date and time of the visit, then entering the beneficiary’s basic data such as the ID or residence number and mobile number, followed by a verification step via the verification code (CAPTCHA) to ensure security. The appointment details are then sent to the beneficiary via text message, and he has the ability to modify or cancel the appointment electronically.

The service includes all categories of beneficiaries, including employers, individuals, and visitors, and is provided free of charge. The service also facilitates beneficiaries' access to the Ministry's services with high efficiency thanks to advance scheduling and electronic coordination. In addition, the Ministry provides the ability to communicate with officials and book appointments with senior officials through the service to increase interaction and improve the quality of response.

The importance of the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development’s appointment booking service.

Improving the organization of visitor numbers to reduce waiting and crowding in branches.
Saving time and effort for beneficiaries through a seamless digital appointment system.
Enhancing the user experience with electronic government services.
Supporting the achievement of the objectives of the Ministry of Human Resources through effective communication and empowerment.
Facilitating access to various services to increase beneficiaries’ satisfaction.

Challenges

Ensure appointment schedules are updated and increased requests are accommodated efficiently.
Dealing with cancellations or late attendance for better organization.
Providing technical support for users who are not technically proficient.
Integration of the service with the rest of the ministry’s systems to ensure a homogeneous experience.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial registry.
Specify the date, time and work office.
National address.
serviceConditions: Specify the date, time and work office.

services.name: Changing a worker's profession
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the employer to change the worker's profession

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The worker’s profession change service is an electronic service available through the Qiwa platform of the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in Saudi Arabia. It aims to enable employers to submit requests to change the profession of their expatriate workers in accordance with the actual work tasks, or to suit the worker’s academic and professional competencies and qualifications. This service provides an official and documented update of occupation data, which enhances the compatibility of the professional biography with residency records and ensures the worker and employer’s compliance with the laws and regulations regulating the Saudi labor market.

The service steps begin with the sponsor, employer, or human resources manager logging into his account on the Qiwa platform, then choosing the “Change Profession” service from the Career Management menu. After that, the user selects the worker whose profession he wants to update, and selects the new profession from the list of professions allowed to be changed to according to the unified Saudi classification system. The new profession must be compatible with the worker’s academic or professional qualifications. Some professions may require a practicing certificate or professional licenses from official authorities such as the Saudi Council of Engineers or the Ministry of Agriculture, depending on the type of profession.

The application is sent electronically to the employee for approval, and the applications are studied by the competent authorities who ensure verification that all requirements are met. The fee for changing profession is usually 1,000 Saudi riyals, paid via the SADAD electronic platform. Upon approval, the system updates the employee’s official data and automatically updates the records of social insurance and related entities.

The service provides effective solutions to guide workers' careers in a way that enhances the use of skills, contributes to achieving specified Saudization rates, and better organizing the labor market. It also makes it easier for establishments to manage their employees’ data with ease and transparency, and enhances their competitive capabilities in the market.

The importance of changing a worker's profession

Ensuring that the registered profession is compatible with what the worker actually practices.
Facilitating data updating and meeting the requirements of systems and regulatory authorities.
Supporting the policy of localization of jobs and enhancing the quality of the labor market.
Reducing legal violations related to non-conforming professions.
Enabling organizations to manage human resources effectively.

Challenges

Ensure that all documents required to change profession are completed.
Dealing with cases that require special licenses or professional certificates.
Follow up on updating the system and defining professions according to sectoral changes.
The need to ensure worker approval and follow up on the request until completion.
requiredDocuments: Resident ID of the worker.
National address.
A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: The status of the facility must be current.
All licenses must be valid.
The new profession should not be limited to Saudis only.
Determine the profession to be changed for the worker.

services.name: Create a delegate account
services.shortDescription: This service enables the facility owner to authorize a user for the facility

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of creating an authorized account﻿ is an electronic service provided by the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It aims to enable establishment owners to appoint official delegates to deal on their behalf on the Ministry’s forces and services platform, which facilitates the management of electronic transactions for employees and workers. The delegate account provides multiple powers, including submitting requests, following up on cases, issuing documents, and implementing transactions related to human resources without the need for direct intervention from the facility owner.

The steps for creating the account begin with the employer or human resources manager logging in to the Qiwa platform, then choosing the user identification service or adding a new user, where the proposed delegate’s data is filled in, such as the name, national ID or residence number, mobile number, and the appropriate powers are assigned to him as needed. The delegate is subject to electronic approval from the facility owner (sponsor) to ensure security and privacy.

The delegate's account can handle all platform services, including issuing and renewing business licenses, documenting contracts, transferring services, booking appointments, and others, with the ability to upload and update data and facilitate electronic workflow within the facility. The Ministry also provides technical support and detailed instructions to ensure that this service is used efficiently.

The importance of creating a delegate account service.

Accelerating the completion of transactions and reducing reliance on personal visits.
Distributing administrative tasks among approved cadres in the facility.
Improve time management and reduce errors in order processing.
Ensuring the security and transparency of transactions.
Supporting digital transformation and continuous modernization of business systems.

Challenges

Ensure the accuracy and definition of commissioners’ powers to ensure appropriate handling.
It is necessary to update the commissioners’ data regularly.
Dealing with cases of account deactivation or expiration.
Provide ongoing support to users in understanding and using the Service.
requiredDocuments: National address.
A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: If the delegate is a resident, the resident ID must be valid.

services.name: Create an account
services.shortDescription: This service enables the opening of an account for the owner of the establishment and for the individual in Quwa

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The account creation service is an electronic service provided by the Qiwa platform of the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It aims to enable individuals and business owners to open their own account on the platform to use all of its advanced digital services related to the Saudi labor market. Creating an account allows seamless access to services such as issuing and renewing work licenses, registering contracts, transferring employee services, issuing nationalization certificates, and benefiting from various services that facilitate human resources management and employment in the Kingdom.

The steps for creating an account for an individual include visiting the Qiwa platform website or downloading the mobile application, choosing the “New Registration” option, entering the national ID number or residence number, the mobile number registered with Absher, receiving the verification code via text messages, then setting a strong password consisting of at least 10 characters that includes upper and lower case letters, numbers and symbols. After completing these steps, the account will be activated via the activation link sent to the email, where the user can log in and continue his services.

For business owners, the registration steps begin by entering the commercial registration number, establishment data, and mobile number, then following the same verification steps and setting the password. After activating the account, the employer can manage the facility’s account, add or authorize users, issue documents related to employment, monitor Emiratisation rates, and other operational services.

The service is characterized by ease of use, information security, and its connection to other e-government systems such as the Absher platform, which ensures data integrity and continuous updating. The Qiwa platform also provides technical support to help solve problems and guide users through creating their accounts.

The importance of creating an account on the Qiwa platform

Enabling full access to the services of the Qiwa digital platform for the labor market.
Facilitating transactions and reducing paper procedures and field reviews.
Improving the user experience through a unified and secure interface.
Supporting compliance with the localization system and Saudi labor controls.
Facilitating the process of communicating with government agencies and obtaining interactive services.

Challenges

It is necessary to have a registered mobile number and correct data with the Absher system.
Secure a strong password to maintain account security.
Dealing with cases where a verification code or email cannot be received.
Ensure continuous updating of personal and institutional information.
requiredDocuments: National address.
serviceConditions: Required data: (email - Absher mobile number - national ID or resident ID if the facility owner is a resident).

services.name: Visa cancellation
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the employer to cancel a visa that has been applied for.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The visa cancellation service is an electronic service provided by the Qiwa platform of the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It aims to enable establishment owners to cancel professional visas (whether immediate or permanent visas) issued to expatriate workers through the platform in a flexible and official manner and without the need to visit government offices, with the possibility of refunding fees if the cancellation is before use and automatically transferring the balance to the recruitment system or the establishment’s account.

The cancellation steps begin by logging into the facility’s account on the Qiwa platform, then entering the visa list, where all previously issued visas, their numbers, and all associated details (border number, nationality, profession, visa type) appear. The employer selects the visa to be cancelled, then clicks on the “View Details” option or the three dots next to the request and chooses “Cancel Visa.” The user receives a confirmation message for submitting the cancellation request, and the request is automatically sent to government agencies for review. The recruitment balance is usually updated automatically once the cancellation is accepted, and the visa fee refund process - if it has been paid and not used - takes place within 24 to 48 hours and is added to the account of the party from which the payment was made.

The service allows organizing recruitment processes and quickly modifying establishments’ labor needs, and helps control their financial resources, manage the balance, and accurately implement recruitment plans. The service also allows establishments to clearly manage records of active and canceled visas and prevents the accumulation of unused visas, and constitutes an important element in the digital transformation strategy for human resources in the private sector.

The importance of the visa cancellation service

Enabling visa cancellations electronically quickly in accordance with official recruitment policies.
Refund of balance and fees with the ability to seamlessly modify employment plans.
Facilitating control over active visas and reducing violations or the accumulation of unused visas.
Supporting digital transformation and reducing the need to review traditional government agencies.
Improving the user experience for business owners and delegates.

Challenges

It is necessary to ensure that the visa is canceled before using it or obtaining authorization for those wishing to enter the Kingdom.
Dealing with cancellation requests in times of increased visa numbers or seasonal needs.
Follow up on updating the application status and recover fees in the event of delayed bank transactions.
Educating users about the correct cancellation procedures according to the approved systems.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the visa to be cancelled, or the application number.
A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: Not using the visa and bringing in the worker.

services.name: Interruption from work
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the employer to submit a request for the worker to leave work.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The work interruption service is an electronic service provided by the Qiwa platform of the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It aims to enable establishment owners to officially report the absence of expatriate workers from work in accordance with the approved statutory procedures for the purpose of preserving the rights of employers and workers, and regulating the labor market in accordance with the rules of transparency and justice. The service allows you to quickly submit documented outage reports through the platform without the need to visit business offices.

The steps for submitting a report begin with the employer or the person responsible for human resources management logging in to the facility’s account on the Qiwa platform, then going to the “Contract Management” page and selecting the “Employees Away from Work” section. The facility can choose the interrupted employee or employees and submit a disconnection request, explaining the reason for the interruption according to the options provided on the platform. Immediate electronic notifications are sent to the other party (the worker) to give him the opportunity to respond or communicate if there is a legitimate reason for the absence.

The platform verifies that the conditions of service are met, such as: that the worker is an expatriate, that the current contract is expired or has expired, and that the worker’s residency is valid for a period of more than 60 days. If the conditions are met, the outage is recorded administratively, and the contract data is updated in the social insurance system and all relevant authorities. It is also possible to submit requests to terminate the contractual relationship and follow up on request statuses electronically.

A work break service that supports labor market stability by reducing unexcused absences, protecting employers from losses related to non-attendance, and ensuring the rights of regular workers. It also enhances communication mechanisms between the two parties to help manage emergency cases or sickness absence in a legal and organized manner.

The importance of service interruption from work.

Document interruptions accurately to preserve the rights of both parties.
Facilitating administrative procedures and developing clear mechanisms to address absenteeism.
Supporting job localization policies and ensuring workers’ commitment.
Enhancing the protection of employers and workers through an effective supervisory system.
Improving the work environment and increasing the transparency of the contractual relationship.

Challenges

Ensure compliance with the criteria for accepting reports and the terms of the system.
Effectively manage outage reports at peak times.
Handling disputes in the event that both parties do not agree.
Providing sufficient awareness for employers and workers about the service mechanism.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
Resident ID of the worker.
National address.
serviceConditions: Determine the reasons for the worker’s interruption.

services.name: Password recovery
services.shortDescription: This service enables the facility owner to modify and recover the password

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The password recovery service is an electronic service provided by the Qiwa platform of the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It allows users to reset the password for their accounts in the event that they forget or lose it. The service aims to restore safe and fast access to the platform's digital accounts, while providing multiple verification mechanisms to ensure data protection and prevent unauthorized access.

Steps to recover your password include entering the Qiwa platform login page, then clicking on the “Forgot your password” option. The user enters the national ID number or residence number associated with his account, then the system requests that a verification link be sent via SMS (OTP) to the registered mobile number. After receiving the code, the user enters it to confirm his identity, and is then allowed to set a new password that includes at least 10 characters, mixing upper and lower case letters, numbers, and symbols to ensure strong security.

The service is designed to be easy to use and responsive, with an emphasis on privacy integrity and security through advanced encryption techniques. The user can modify the contact information associated with the account to improve the recovery service in the future. The platform also provides guidance materials and technical support to facilitate the recovery process for users.

The importance of the password recovery service on the Qiwa platform.

Maintaining the security of accounts to prevent unauthorized access.
Enabling users to quickly recover their accounts without the need to contact support directly.
Facilitating the continuity of use of the electronic services of the Qiwa platform.
Building users’ confidence in dealing with digital government platforms.
Reducing the risks of hacking and electronic theft.

Challenges

Careful verification of the user's identity to ensure that the service is not exploited.
Dealing with technical errors or delayed arrival of verification codes.
The need to educate users on how to protect their data and account security.
Manage cases where the user forgets the security data needed for recovery.
requiredDocuments: ID photo.

services.name: Disclosure of training
services.shortDescription: Training management enables the employer to document the training courses provided to employees by allowing him to enter training data, select employees, send the request to employees for approval, evaluate the training, and attach the certificate.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The Training Disclosure Service﻿ is an official electronic service provided by the Qiwa platform of the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It aims to raise the quality and efficiency of training programs in private sector establishments by requiring establishments with 50 or more employees to annually disclose the training data they provide to their employees via the digital platform. The disclosure process includes accurate information about the number of training hours completed, types of training activities, and the number of trainees from various categories such as employees, students, graduates, and job seekers.

The disclosure steps begin by logging into the facility’s account on the Qiwa platform, and then moving to the “Training Management” or “Training Data Disclosure” service, where the facility can fill out the form designated for disclosure, which includes training program data, the budget spent, and general training plans. Establishments are allowed to amend and add disclosure during a specific period of time that usually extends from the beginning of September to the end of January of each year, with the possibility of reviewing the data and saving the amendments before final submission. After completing the disclosure, a detailed report is issued showing the facility’s commitment to workforce skills development programs.

The importance of the service is to enhance transparency and accountability with regard to human resources training, and to support the continuous professional development journey of employees in line with the requirements of the Saudi labor market and Vision 2030, as it helps improve productivity and enhance the competitiveness of establishments. It also contributes to enabling the Ministry of Human Resources to monitor the extent of implementation of approved training plans and improve the quality of training programs applied in the private sector.

The importance of the training disclosure service

Ensuring transparent disclosure of training programs and human resources plans.
Improving opportunities to develop employee skills and achieve professional development goals.
Supporting periodic evaluation of the performance and impact of training programs in organizations.
Contributing to enhancing the competitiveness and development of the Saudi labor market.
Providing documented data that helps in making strategic decisions related to training.

Challenges

The establishments’ commitment to entering training data completely and accurately.
Monitoring the disclosure application on time and not delaying it.
Follow up on updating and developing disclosure forms to meet changing needs.
Activating the involvement of all concerned parties to ensure effective implementation of the decision.
requiredDocuments: A list of the names of employees who are supposed to attend the training programs.
National address.
A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: General information: (name and classification of the training - training provider - location of the training - choosing the start and end dates of the training - total number of hours - providing information if a training certificate is available).

services.name: Modify facility file data
services.shortDescription: A service that enables the facility owner to modify the facility data

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The facility file data modification service is an electronic service provided by the Qiwa platform of the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It allows establishments to update and modify the information registered with them in an accurate and smooth manner. The amendments include commercial registry data, facility activity, mobile number, email, address, and number of employees, ensuring that the files match the actual situation of the facility, improving the quality of services provided, and facilitating human resources management and operation.

The amendment process is carried out by logging into the facility’s account on the Qiwa platform, and then going to the facility management section, which displays all the registered details. The user selects the data to be modified, then enters the new information and attaches supporting documents such as a new commercial registration certificate or any necessary official documents. After completing the amendments, the application is submitted for review by the competent authorities to officially complete the update with automatic notification to the facility of the result of the application.

The service increases the transparency of establishment data, and ensures that localization requirements and the scope system are accurately completed, as data updates are linked to assessing the establishment’s level of compliance with government policies in the labor market. It also provides the ability to follow the history of amendments and detailed reports that enhance the facility’s ability to make strategic decisions and manage operations efficiently.

The importance of the facility file data modification service.

Ensuring the updating and credibility of facility information in the government system.
Facilitating procedures for obtaining other services such as work and employment permits.
Supporting the achievement of compliance with localization rates and improving the facility’s scope classification.
Enhancing the facility’s ability to conduct strategic and technical planning based on correct data.
Reducing errors and irregularities resulting from outdated or inaccurate data.

Challenges

Users are keen to provide correct and updated official documents.
Ensuring that applications are quickly reviewed and approved by the competent authorities.
Dealing with different types of data and the need to adapt updates according to system requirements.
Educating institutions about the importance of updating their data periodically to avoid systemic problems.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: Specify modification data.

services.name: Uninstall an established file
services.shortDescription: This service enables the facility owner to cancel the facility

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The facility file cancellation service is an electronic service provided by the Qiwa platform of the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It aims to enable facility owners to close and delete the facility file permanently after meeting all regulatory conditions, which leads to stopping all services related to the facility such as issuing visas, renewing work permits, and managing labor, in addition to ending activity in the General Organization for Social Insurance. This service is an important step to end the establishment’s official relationship with the Ministry’s systems in a legal and effective manner without the need to visit the labor offices directly.

Conditions for canceling the establishment file include:

There should be no employees working or absent from work within the facility.
There are no issued and unused work visas.
There are no service transfer requests in progress.
There are no valid employment contracts or accumulated debts owed by the facility.
Settle any violations or issues against the facility.
Cancellation of licenses and commercial records.

The cancellation process is done by logging into the employer’s or delegate’s account on the Qiwa platform, going to the “Facility Information” menu, then choosing “Close the Facility” or “Cancel an Facility File.” The previous conditions are confirmed to be met, and after final approval, the facility’s file is officially canceled and users are notified of the status of the application. You can follow up on the cancellation status electronically and ensure that the procedures are completed with the competent authorities.

The service provides the opportunity to end commercial activity in an orderly manner and with full control over the impact of cancellation on all services linked to the Qiwa and Social Insurance Institution platforms, which saves time and ensures compliance with legal procedures.

The importance of the establishment file cancellation service.

Achieving a complete legal closure of the facility and stopping its services comprehensively.
Settle all financial and legal dues before cancellation.
Preventing the continued use of the Ministry’s services for closed facilities.
Officially documenting and terminating the relationship with the supervisory and regulatory authorities.
Reducing legal and financial risks related to inactive facilities.

Challenges

Ensure closing conditions are fully met before submitting.
Follow up on the completion of settlement of violations and financial debts.
Address issues that may delay the cancellation process.
Educating establishment owners about the need to update their data and follow up on requests.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: The establishment must be closed only by the unified number manager or whoever has the authority.
Ensure that there are no employees working in the facility.
Ensure that there are no unused visas, pending service transfer requests, valid employment contracts, and valid employment contracts in Ajeer.
Ensure that there are no business license debts, violations, lawsuits, or any observations on the facility.
Ensure that there is no active commercial record on the facility or an unpaid combined invoice.

services.name: Changing the facility's activity
services.shortDescription: Changing the facility's activity

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The establishment activity change service is an electronic service available through the Qiwa platform of the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, which enables establishment owners to amend the registered commercial activity of their establishment electronically and officially. This service aims to update facility data to become compatible with its current actual activity, which is necessary to ensure facilities’ compliance with regulatory and economic laws and to enhance the accuracy of data in the records of the ministry and relevant state institutions.

The process of changing the activity is carried out by logging into the employer’s account on the Qiwa platform, then going to the “Facility Management” section, then choosing “Registration Information”, where you can click on the “Change Activity” option linked to the Unified Industrial Activity Classification (ISIC4). After reading the instructions, the user selects the new activity from the list of available activities according to the commercial registry, then follows the steps to send the change request for review and approval from the relevant official authorities.

The service requires providing correct data and supporting documents such as an updated copy of the commercial registry and new activity licenses upon request. The amendment process undergoes examination by government agencies to ensure that the activity complies with the legal requirements and localization rates for the new activity. Some cases may require the submission of additional documents or the implementation of supplementary procedures depending on the type of activity.

The activity change service contributes to helping establishments remain compliant with regulations and raise the level of economic competitiveness. It also links establishment data to market transformations and new investment opportunities. The service is characterized by ease of use and provides technical support to beneficiaries, which enhances digital transformation in the private sector.

The importance of changing the facility’s activity

Accurate and documented updating of facility activity to improve legal compliance.
Facilitating obtaining other related services such as issuing licenses and permits.
Supporting the Ministry’s efforts to regulate the labor market and improve domain management.
Increase data transparency and enhance strategic decision making.
Enabling establishments to keep pace with economic changes smoothly.

Challenges

The accuracy of the data provided is necessary to avoid rejection of applications or delays.
Continuous review by the competent authorities to ensure that activities comply with regulations.
Handling cases that require special licenses or additional conditions.
The importance of educating establishment owners about the mechanisms and requirements of change through digital platforms.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
Select the activity you want to modify.
serviceConditions: New activity information.

services.name: Managing facility sites
services.shortDescription: The location management service allows you to add the locations of your facility and its branches and assign employees to the branches.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The establishment website management service is an integrated electronic service provided by the Qiwa platform of the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It aims to enable establishment owners to add, modify, and manage the locations and branches of their establishments in an accurate and flexible manner. The service includes the ability to register the facility's main location as well as branch locations, and assign employees to each location separately to ensure efficient and effective work organization.

Site management is carried out by logging into the facility’s account on the Qiwa platform, then moving to the “Facility Site Management” section, where new sites can be added by entering site data such as the site name, description, detailed address, and the site’s municipal license, if any. There are options for adding in two ways: entering site data manually or linking to the site’s municipal license. It is also possible to modify the status of sites, whether by activating or deactivating them, and designating a site as a main branch affecting various localization reports.

The service is distinguished by its connection to localization systems and classification of the scope of establishments, as updating sites and distributing employees are among the basic requirements to fully benefit from the services of the Qiwa platform, such as issuing and renewing work permits, transferring services, and managing contracts. Detailed reports are provided that reflect the status of each site and the number of employees assigned to it, making it easier for facilities to monitor the implementation of work policies and the organization of human resources.

The importance of facility site management service

Organizing and distributing workers accurately according to locations and branches.
Facilitating the management of operational processes and controlling attendance according to work sites.
Supporting the localization and domains system through updated and accurate data.
Improve compliance with labor regulations and enhance productivity.
Enable management to track performance and perform required analyses.

Challenges

Duties of updating websites on an ongoing basis to ensure continuity of services.
The necessity of accurate data for all registered sites and branches.
Dealing with regulatory changes and implementing them across different sites.
The need for continuous technical support for users to manage the sites.
requiredDocuments: The national address of the establishment’s location.
A copy of the commercial register.

services.name: Issuing a service investment license
services.shortDescription: The service license includes the following activities: contracting, senior management consulting, information technology, tourism, training, health, insurance and reinsurance, education, advertising, sea freight and air freight services for goods, organizing exhibitions, catering and nutrition services, financial services, aviation and handling services,,, etc..)

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of issuing a service investment license is one of the basic services provided through the Saudi Ministry of Investment, and aims to enable foreign or Gulf investors to obtain an official license that allows them to practice service activities within the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia in a regular and legal manner. This license is considered the main entry point for an investor wishing to establish a company or branch that provides various services, such as: management consulting, logistics services, information technology, training, marketing, financial services, and other authorized service activities.


The application is submitted electronically, and the investor must fulfill all regulatory requirements, such as:


Fill out the service investment license application form.
Submitting the investor’s ID or passport (for individuals) or the commercial registry of the parent company (for foreign companies).
Attach the approved financial budget statements of the parent company (in the case of foreign companies).
Proof of qualifications and previous experiences in the field of service activity.
Pay the prescribed statutory fees.


The importance of issuing a service investment license


Opening the way for foreign and Gulf investment in the vital services sector in Saudi Arabia.
Enabling foreign companies to carry out their service activities legally through approved branches.
Promoting economic diversification by attracting investments in renewable service areas that support Saudi Vision 2030.
Creating new job opportunities by attracting international expertise in the fields of modern services such as technical and financial consulting.
Raising the efficiency of the local market by introducing innovative and diverse services that support various sectors.
Facilitating entering into strategic partnerships with local companies or government institutions.
Strengthening legal confidence before banks and government agencies by granting the investor an official license confirming his commitment to the regulations.


Difficulties related to obtaining or issuing a service investment license:


Multiple requirements and documents, such as approving financial statements or authenticating documents from embassies and chambers of commerce.
The need to translate and authenticate foreign documents to become acceptable to the Ministry of Investment.
Special conditions for some service activities, such as requiring professional certificates or additional licenses from competent ministries (health, finance, education...).
The length of the review period in the event of deficiencies or ambiguities in the documents.
The possibility of rejecting the application if the activity is not included among the activities permitted for foreign investment.
Subsequent operational challenges, such as requiring a minimum capital in some activities, or the necessity of employing specific Saudi cadres according to nationalization rates.
requiredDocuments: The commercial register of the establishment is certified by the Saudi embassy.
The financial statements for the last fiscal year of the foreign company requesting the license are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
National address.
The establishment’s articles of incorporation are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
serviceConditions: The total percentage of the Saudi partner or partners and the capital corresponds to the minimum specified by the type of activity.
If one of the partners was previously licensed by the Ministry of Investment, this must be clarified when filling out the partners’ data while submitting the electronic application.

The data (Saudi National ID / Privileged Residency / Iqama) must be entered, if one of the partners holds any of them, or the commercial registry data of the participating Saudi companies while submitting the electronic application.
Payment of fees within 15 days.
Required data: (email address - Absher mobile number - service activities - identities of partners and managers - city in which the license is to be issued).

services.name: Issuing a scientific and technical office license
services.shortDescription: This service is provided to foreign companies that have a Saudi agent or authorized distributor in the Kingdom, and wish to open an office that provides scientific and technical services to agents, distributors and consumers of the company’s products.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of issuing a scientific and technical office license is a specialized regulatory and investment service provided by the Saudi Ministry of Investment in cooperation with the relevant sectoral authorities. It aims to enable foreign or Gulf companies to open scientific or technical offices within the Kingdom to support their products or technologies, and to practice the authorized activities in this field in a regular manner. This license is considered an important gateway to representing international companies in Saudi Arabia, especially in sectors such as: medicines and medical supplies, industrial or technical devices and equipment, engineering solutions, advanced technology, and scientific and technical services.


The application is submitted electronically, and the parent company (foreign or Gulf) must complete the following requirements:

A copy of the commercial register of the parent company in its country, certified and translated into Arabic.
The parent company's financial statements for the last three years (in most cases).
An official authorization letter for the company’s representative in the Kingdom certified by chambers of commerce and embassies.
A detailed description of the scientific or technical activity that the office will provide.
Proof of experience and professional qualification in the targeted field.
Payment of statutory fees.

The importance of the service of issuing a scientific and technical office license:

Enabling foreign and Gulf companies to have a regular presence within the Saudi market through official offices.
Supporting the transfer of technical and scientific knowledge and expertise to the Saudi market.
Enhancing the quality of products and services through the presence of official offices that provide technical support and after-sales services.
Raising the level of local competitiveness and innovation as a result of introducing international expertise and standards.
Enhancing trust between foreign companies and local customers through the presence of an official and accredited representative office.
Facilitating cooperation with government agencies and the private sector in major scientific and technical projects.
Contributing to achieving Saudi Vision 2030 to diversify the economy and attract quality investments.

Difficulties related to obtaining or issuing a scientific and technical office license:

There are multiple competent authorities supervising scientific or technical activities, which requires additional approvals before a license is issued.
The need to fully translate and authenticate foreign documents, which may cause delays.
Strict financial or administrative requirements such as submitting current, certified financial statements.
The application may be rejected if the activities are not included within the scope of permitted foreign investment.
The length of the review cycle in the case of precise technical activities subject to special standards such as medical supplies or advanced devices.
Difficulties in proving technical and scientific competence if the parent company does not provide sufficient documents or a CV of its experiences.
Subsequent operational obstacles such as localization requirements, hiring local talent, or obtaining additional municipal and operational licenses for the office.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the establishment’s commercial register certified by the Saudi Embassy.
A copy of the approved agency of the Saudi agent or distributor.
The financial statements for the establishment's last fiscal year are approved and certified by the Saudi Embassy.
The establishment’s articles of incorporation are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
National address.
serviceConditions: The office may study the markets in relation to the type of company activity and prepare reports on this study for the head office. The office must submit to the Ministry of Investment an annual summary of activity.
The office is prohibited from executing any contracts or carrying out commercial or investment activity in the Kingdom, directly or indirectly, by charging any fees for training Saudi technicians.
Payment of fees within 15 days.
Required data: Email: (Absher mobile number - scientific and technical activities - identities of partners and managers - city in which the license is to be issued).

services.name: Issuing a real estate investment license
services.shortDescription: A license for activities that include investments in the field of real estate development, provided that the cost of the project is not less than 30 million Saudi riyals for land and construction outside the cities of Makkah Al-Mukarramah and Medina.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of issuing a real estate investment license is one of the investment services provided by the Saudi Ministry of Investment, and aims to enable foreign and Gulf investors to enter the real estate sector in the Kingdom and practice real estate activities in a regular and official manner.


The real estate investment license allows the foreign or Gulf investor to practice real estate activities such as: developing residential or commercial projects, property management, investing in real estate complexes, operating real estate facilities, or real estate activities authorized according to Saudi laws. In some cases, activities are restricted by special controls, such as buying and selling real estate to foreigners, as it is subject to a license and specific exceptions.


To submit the application, the investor enters the Ministry of Investment portal and fills out the “Request to Issue a Real Estate Investment License” form, attaching the required documents, such as:


The commercial register of the parent company (certified and translated).
Approved financial budgets for the last three years.
A feasibility study or detailed description of the targeted real estate project.
An operational and investment plan showing the type of real estate activity.
Official letters of authorization of the representative in the Kingdom.
Payment of statutory fees.


The importance of the service of issuing a real estate investment license:


Opening the Saudi real estate market to foreign and Gulf investments in an organized and legal manner.
Promoting urban development projects by attracting development companies and international expertise.
Contributing to achieving the goals of Saudi Vision 2030 by raising the efficiency of the real estate sector and developing quality investments.
Diversifying the national economy by attracting investment in one of the most important sectors supporting growth.
Enhancing competitiveness and quality in residential, commercial and service projects through the entry of international and Gulf companies.
Creating new job opportunities through companies and huge real estate projects.
Activating local and international partnerships with real estate developers, contractors and service offices.


Difficulties related to obtaining or issuing a real estate investment license:


Strict conditions related to foreign ownership of real estate, especially in some holy regions or cities (such as Mecca and Medina).
The need to translate and authenticate foreign documents through chambers of commerce and embassies, which increases the processing time.
Additional approvals are necessary in the event of practicing certain real estate activities such as property management or residential development.
Possibility of application rejection if the investor does not present a clear plan or strong financial record.
The review period is long, especially for large projects that require approvals from the housing authority or municipalities.
Special conditions for the minimum capital required to enter the real estate market.
Subsequent obligations, such as employment rates (Nationalization) and submitting periodic reports to regulatory authorities.
requiredDocuments: The commercial register of the establishment is certified by the Saudi embassy.
The financial statements for the last fiscal year of the foreign company requesting the license are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
The establishment’s articles of incorporation are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
National address.
serviceConditions: The total percentage of the Saudi partner or partners and the capital corresponds to the minimum specified by the type of activity.
If one of the partners was previously licensed by the Ministry of Investment, this must be clarified when filling out the partners’ data while submitting the electronic application.
Payment of fees within 15 days.

The data (Saudi National ID / Privileged Residency / Iqama) must be entered, if one of the partners holds any of them, or the commercial registry data of the participating Saudi companies while submitting the electronic application.

If the licensed activity is related to real estate investment projects, the limit of the value of one project to be established is not less than 30,000,000 Saudi riyals and the building is outside the scope of the Two Holy Mosques, and there is no minimum capital for real estate investment projects.
The real estate financing capital must not be less than 200,00,000 Saudi riyals and the Saudi percentage must not be less than 40%.
The real estate development capital outside the scope of the Two Holy Mosques shall not be less than 30,000,000 million Saudi riyals.
Required data: (email address - Absher mobile number - real estate activities - identities of partners and managers - city in which the license is requested).

services.name: Issuing a temporary investment license to implement one government/semi-government contract
services.shortDescription: This service is provided to the company when awarding governmental or semi-governmental projects to foreign companies that do not hold a foreign investment license in the activity and scope of the contract, as they can apply for a temporary license to implement the government/semi-governmental contract that expires at the end of the contract.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of issuing a temporary investment license to implement a government contract is a special service provided by the Saudi Ministry of Investment, and aims to enable foreign or Gulf companies to enter the Saudi market temporarily to implement a government contract with a specific duration and scope. This type of license is considered a flexible tool that makes it easier for government agencies to benefit from the expertise of international companies in major projects, while ensuring that these companies comply with Saudi regulations during the contract implementation period.


To submit the application, the foreign company must log in through the Ministry of Investment platform and submit the following documents:


A copy of the government contract concluded with the Saudi entity.
A copy of the parent company’s commercial register, notarized and translated.
The parent company's latest financial statements to prove its financial viability.
An authorization for the company’s representative in Saudi Arabia certified by the chambers of commerce and the embassy.
An operational plan showing the mechanism for implementing the contract within the Kingdom.
Pay the prescribed statutory fees.


The importance of the service of issuing a temporary investment license to implement a government contract:


Facilitating the implementation of major government projects by attracting international companies with advanced expertise.
Providing regulatory flexibility for foreign companies to operate temporarily without the need to establish a permanent company.
Contributing to transferring expertise and technologies to the Saudi market through implemented projects.
Accelerating the pace of national development by involving highly qualified companies.
Strengthening strategic partnerships between Saudi Arabia and international companies.
Providing legal protection for both parties (the government agency and the foreign company) by documenting the license as an official reference.


Difficulties related to completing or issuing a temporary investment license to implement a government contract:
The requirement of a concluded and approved government contract, which is the essential condition and cannot be replaced.
Multiple documents required to be authenticated, such as the foreign commercial register and financial statements, which may prolong the period.
The need to authenticate all foreign official documents from the relevant authorities (embassies - chambers of commerce).
The license duration is exclusively linked to the contract duration, which may restrict the company to expand into other businesses outside the scope of the project.
The application may be rejected if the applicant company does not have sufficient financial or technical qualifications.
Operational obstacles, such as the necessity of adhering to nationalization rates and registering workers in Saudi labor systems during the contract period.
Strict restrictions on permitted activities, as the license is limited to contract-related activity only.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the establishment’s commercial register certified by the Saudi Embassy.
A copy of the government entity’s letter or the project implementation contract.
National address.
The financial statements for the last fiscal year of the foreign company requesting the license are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
The establishment’s articles of incorporation are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
Photocopies of IDs for Saudi and resident partners and managers.
serviceConditions: A copy of the government entity’s letter or the project implementation contract signed by the concerned authority must include the duration of the contract.
Payment of fees within 15 days.

Required data: Email - Absher mobile number - Phone number from the company’s country - Government or semi-government contract activities - Identities of partners and managers - City in which the license is requested).

services.name: Issuing a commercial license with a Saudi partner
services.shortDescription: The service is provided to foreign establishments that wish to engage in wholesale and retail trade activity in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia with a Saudi partner.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of issuing a commercial license with a Saudi partner is one of the types of investment licenses provided by the Saudi Ministry of Investment, and targets foreign investors wishing to establish a commercial entity within the Kingdom in partnership with a Saudi citizen. This license allows the establishment of companies with various activities (commercial, service, industrial...) while benefiting from the presence of a local partner who helps facilitate market requirements and increase the chances of success.

The presence of a Saudi partner in this type of license gives the foreign investor greater flexibility and special privileges, such as facilitating some government approvals, increasing access to local tenders and contracts, and ease of opening channels of dealing with the government and private sectors.
To apply for a license, the investor enters the Ministry of Investment platform and performs the following steps:

Fill out the commercial license issuance form.
Submit a copy of the parent company’s register or passport (for individuals).
Attach a copy of the national ID or commercial register of the Saudi partner.
Signing a partnership agreement stating the participation rates and capital provided by each party.
Submitting financial statements (for foreign companies) to prove financial solvency.
Pay the prescribed fees for issuing the license.

The importance of the service of issuing a commercial license with a Saudi partner

Enhancing joint investment opportunities and attracting foreign expertise while ensuring local participation.
Facilitating regulatory procedures due to the presence of a Saudi partner familiar with local laws.
Increased reliability among banks, customers and government agencies with the presence of a supportive local partner.
Supporting Saudi Vision 2030, which encourages foreign-local partnerships to diversify the economy.
The possibility of benefiting from special investment opportunities available through the local partner.
Expanding the business base and entering tenders and contracts that may require the presence of a Saudi partner.

Difficulties related to obtaining or issuing a commercial license with a Saudi partner:

The need to agree on a fair and clear partnership formula to avoid future disputes.
The need to document official agreements between the two parties in a legal and certified manner.
Multiple documents and regulatory requirements, such as translation and certification of foreign financial statements.
Possibility of delayed processing if there are deficiencies or objections to the chosen activity.
Operational risks arising from the partnership, such as differences in administrative or investment visions between the foreign and local partner.
The activity is required to be compatible with Saudi laws, and some activities may only be granted with a certain percentage of Saudi participation.
Technical or administrative obstacles on the investment platform while entering or uploading the required documents.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the establishment’s commercial register certified by the Saudi Embassy.
The financial statements for the establishment's last fiscal year are approved and certified by the Saudi Embassy.
Photocopies of IDs for Saudi and resident partners and managers.
The establishment’s articles of incorporation are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
National address.
serviceConditions: The presence of a Saudi partner with an investment license, provided that his share in the company is not less than 25%, according to the minimum specified by the type of activity. (Prime residency holders are exempt from this condition)
If one of the partners was previously licensed by the Ministry of Investment, this must be clarified when filling out the partners’ data while submitting the electronic application.

The data (Saudi National ID / Privileged Residency / Iqama) must be entered, if one of the partners holds any of them, or the commercial registry data of the participating Saudi companies while submitting the electronic application.
Payment of fees within 15 days.
The company's capital must not be less than 27,000,000 Saudi riyals.
Required data: (email - Absher mobile number - phone number from the company’s country - commercial activities - identities of partners and managers - city in which the license is requested)

services.name: Issuing a license for public land transportation by buses within cities
