services.shortDescription: This service is provided to international companies that wish to provide public land transportation services by buses within cities in the Kingdom.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of issuing a license for land public transportation by buses within cities is a regulatory service provided by the General Authority for Transport in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and aims to enable Saudi companies or licensed foreign investors to operate public transportation activities by buses within cities within specific controls and standards that guarantee the quality of service and the safety of passengers. This license is a basic condition for practicing the activity and allows operating entities to enter one of the most important service sectors within the Kingdom supported by Saudi Vision 2030, which seeks to develop the transportation sector and raise its efficiency and quality.


Through the license, the company is committed to operating public transportation buses within regulated and approved route networks, and providing transportation services to passengers taking into account safety requirements, operational discipline, using smart tracking systems, providing approved maintenance centers, and adhering to precise operating schedules. This aims to enhance reliance on mass transportation and reduce reliance on private cars within cities.


The importance of the service of issuing a license for public land transportation by buses within cities:


Organizing and operating the public transportation sector within cities in an official and effective manner.
Developing the infrastructure for transportation services and supporting the achievement of Vision 2030 goals.
Providing safe and reliable alternatives for daily transportation for citizens and residents.
Reducing traffic congestion and carbon emissions by reducing reliance on private vehicles.
Creating new job opportunities for Saudis in the fields of drivers, technicians and administrators.
Ensuring a higher quality of service provided through modern buses equipped with technical systems.


Difficulties related to obtaining or issuing a license for public land transportation by buses within cities:


Significant increase in capital requirements due to the need to purchase or equip a fleet of buses.
Strict technical standards imposed on buses and operating facilities such as maintenance centers and parking lots.
The need to provide an accurate and approved operational plan before obtaining a license.
Challenges of complying with nationalization requirements by employing Saudi drivers and technicians in large numbers.
Field review and inspection procedures that may delay the issuance of a license until safety standards are met.
The possibility of withdrawing or suspending the license when there are violations or deficiencies in the level of service.
High operational costs of managing public transport service within cities.
requiredDocuments: The presence of a decision from the company’s board of directors stating their desire to invest in the Kingdom, stating the names of the partners, the capital, the percentage of each partner, the activity, the location, the appointment of the general manager, and specifying his powers, certified by the Saudi embassy.
A copy of the establishment’s commercial register certified by the Saudi Embassy.
Providing proof of compliance with contract implementation periods in the past five years.
The financial statements for the last fiscal year of the foreign company requesting the license are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
The establishment’s articles of incorporation are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
Photocopies of IDs for Saudi and resident partners and managers.
National address.
serviceConditions: The total percentage of the Saudi partner or partners and the capital corresponds to the minimum specified by the type of activity.

The average revenues of the foreign partner company for the three years preceding the granting of the license must be equal to or more than 3 billion Saudi riyals, and the size of its assets must not be less than 1.5 billion Saudi riyals.
The foreign partner company must be licensed in at least two countries, and the number of its employees must not be less than 10,000 workers.
The minimum capital required to supply, operate and maintain buses according to what is followed by the Ministry of Investment for service sector companies is 500,000 Saudi riyals.
In the event that there is a Saudi partner, a license from the Ministry of Transport or the Public Transport Authority is required to practice bus transportation activity.
Implementing the nationalization rates established in transportation services in force in the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development, and any amendments thereto.
The register of establishments requesting a license is free of decisions to cancel their licenses inside or outside the Kingdom for the ten years preceding the request to grant the license.

The register of establishments requesting a license must be free of any notices or penalties issued - inside or outside the Kingdom - related to traffic safety for the ten years preceding the request to grant a license.
Approval to use public transportation - in the event of disasters, crises or wars - according to what is issued by the relevant authorities in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
The technical specifications of the product and its production method must conform to Saudi, Gulf, or international standards.
Submitting annual plans to improve security levels to the Public Transport Authority for review and review.
Proof of the financial ability to invest in proportion to the project capital and the share of each partner, along with the business plan submitted for the project according to the type of activity.
Payment of fees within 15 days.
The capital must not be less than 500,000 Saudi riyals, and the Saudi percentage must not be less than 30%.
Required data: Email - Absher mobile number - Phone number from the company’s country - Public transportation activities - The city in which the license is requested).

services.name: Issuing a license for the activity of recruitment agents and renting domestic labor services
services.shortDescription: The service is provided to foreign establishments that wish to engage in the activities of domestic labor recruitment services and temporary employment agencies for domestic services in the Kingdom

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of issuing a license for the activity of recruitment agents and leasing domestic workers is a regulatory service provided by the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in cooperation with the competent government agencies in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It aims to enable companies and institutions wishing to practice the activity of recruiting and leasing domestic workers to obtain the official licenses necessary to practice these activities in a legal and organized manner, while ensuring their compliance with the applicable rules and regulations.

The issuance service includes obtaining a license that allows the business to represent recruitment agencies, and provide services for transporting and arranging qualified and authorized domestic workers, while adhering to all safety conditions, workers’ rights, and local market controls. The license is subject to strict controls in terms of:

Meet legal and regulatory requirements.
Recognizing companies and institutions with reputation and financial capacity.
Commitment to labor rights in accordance with the Saudi labor system and international agreements.
Providing the necessary guarantees and insurances to workers and clients.


The importance of the service of issuing a license for the activity of recruitment agents and renting domestic labor services:

Regulating the recruitment and domestic labor sector in a way that protects the rights of workers and employers alike.
Raising the level of transparency and credibility in the domestic labor market.
Ensuring that agents adhere to Saudi regulations and laws, which limits illegal or unregulated practices.
Providing clear and guaranteed market opportunities for companies and establishments that provide these services.
Contributing to the stability of the home labor market by regulating the service provider and preventing leaks and manipulation.
Supporting workers’ rights and protecting customers’ rights through reliable oversight mechanisms.

Difficulties related to obtaining or issuing a license for the activity of recruitment agents and renting domestic labor services:

Complex regulatory requirements and licensing requirements that include multiple legal and legal aspects.
It is necessary to meet large financial requirements, such as providing financial guarantees and insurance for workers.
The lengthy period of study and review of the application due to the large number of documents and verification of competence.
Strict requirements for compliance with workers’ rights regulations, which requires expertise and competencies in management.
The possibility of rejecting the application if the required conditions are not met or if there are previous violations.
The need for continuous follow-up to update and renew the license according to changing regulations.
Possible technical problems in the electronic platform when uploading the application or documents.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register of the participating establishment certified by the Saudi Embassy.
The financial statements for the last 3 financial years of the foreign company requesting the license are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
Photocopies of IDs for Saudi and resident partners and managers.
The establishment’s articles of incorporation are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
National address.
serviceConditions: Commitment to submit a performance evaluation certificate from the authority supervising the export activity in the country of sending, showing the applicant’s historical record and legal observations, if any.

The obligation to provide a certificate issued by the regulatory authority in the investor’s country that no final judgment has been issued against any of the natural partners convicting him of a crime against honor or trust, or any of the violations related to the provisions regulating the protection of children or combating trafficking in persons, unless he is considered.

Commitment to pledge that none of the partners has previously obtained a license to export labor or was a partner in a company that practices this activity and whose license has been canceled by a judicial decision or ruling unless at least five years have passed.
Commitment to acknowledge knowledge of and adherence to the regulations and instructions in the Kingdom and the regulations and instructions regulating recruitment activity in the Kingdom.
If one of the partners is previously licensed by the Ministry of Investment, this must be clarified when filling out the partners’ data during the application submission.
Payment of fees within 15 days.
The commercial registration of the facility requesting a license must be at least 3 years old to practice recruitment activity.

The data (Saudi National ID / Privileged Residency / Iqama) must be entered, if one of the partners holds any of them, or the commercial registry data of the participating Saudi companies while submitting the electronic application.
Required data: (email - Absher mobile number - phone number from the company’s country - recruitment activities - identities of partners and managers - city in which the license is requested).

services.name: Issuing a license for printing and publishing activities
services.shortDescription: The service is provided to establishments that wish to engage in specific activities in the field of publications, publishing, libraries, and distribution.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of issuing a license for printing and publishing activities is a regulatory and investment service provided by the Saudi Ministry of Information with the aim of enabling individuals and companies to establish and practice printing and publishing activities officially and legally within the Kingdom. These services include multiple fields such as: issuing newspapers and magazines, book and publication printing presses, electronic publishing services, production of advertising and marketing materials, and various media publishing activities.


Obtaining the license begins by submitting an electronic application through the Ministry’s portal, where the applicant must fulfill a number of requirements such as:


Submit a copy of the commercial register or company incorporation document.
Submit a detailed file on the activity to be practiced, including the scope of work and targeted areas.
Proof of the technical and professional competence of the applicant or the company through experience, training and qualification certificates.
Adherence to publishing and printing regulations in accordance with the regulations issued by the Ministry of Information, which include respect for intellectual property rights and ethical standards in publishing.
Provide data on the printing press device or the work methods used in publishing.
Pay the prescribed fees.

The importance of the service of issuing a license for printing and publishing activities:

Regulating the publishing and printing sector in the Kingdom to ensure compliance with professional and legal standards.
Strengthening the protection of intellectual property rights and preventing violations in the field of printing and publishing.
Supporting the development of Saudi media and cultural content by granting regular licenses to actors.
Providing a stable environment for investors and publishers to carry out their activities with confidence and transparency.
Enhancing the competitiveness of the local market by diversifying print and media production.
Facilitating communication between publishers and publications with government and national agencies.
Difficulties related to obtaining or issuing a license for printing and publishing activities:

Careful adherence to the Ministry of Information’s regulations and instructions, which may have detailed and stringent requirements.
The need to demonstrate technical competence and compliance with ethical and professional standards, which requires specialized experience and qualifications.
Multiple documents required that may require further attestation and translation.
The possibility of delaying the review of applications due to the large number of applications or the need for additional reviews.
Certain financial requirements, such as the presence of a suitable headquarters containing the required printing equipment.
The risk of rejecting the application if the activity or documents do not comply with the specified conditions.
Continuous updating of regulations requires constant follow-up from the authorities required to obtain licenses.
requiredDocuments: Photocopies of IDs for partners and managers.
The financial statements for the last 3 financial years of the foreign company requesting the license are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
A copy of the commercial records of at least 3 international branches of the establishment in the same field certified by the Saudi embassy.
The establishment contract is certified by the Saudi Embassy.
National address.
Required data: (email - Absher mobile number - phone number in the company’s country - specifying publications and publishing activities - identities of Saudi and resident partners and managers).
serviceConditions: If one of the partners was previously licensed by the Ministry of Investment, this must be clarified when filling out the partners’ data while submitting the electronic application.
Commitment to implementing all obligations regulating the activity, including the standards and percentages of Emiratisation approved by the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development.
The foreign company must be licensed in at least 3 countries in the same field for which the license is to be licensed.
The company must have no less than 3 years of experience in the same field for which the license is to be licensed.
Payment of fees within 15 days.

services.name: Issuing a professional license for a Saudi partner
services.shortDescription: This service is provided to foreign companies that wish to practice professional activities in the Kingdom with a Saudi partner

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of issuing a professional license with a Saudi partner is an investment and regulatory service provided by the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development in cooperation with the Ministry of Investment, and aims to enable individuals or foreign companies to establish a professional activity within the Kingdom in partnership with a Saudi citizen. Professional licensing includes specialized professional fields such as engineering, medicine, law, accounting, consulting, education, and other professions that require special permits and approvals.

The license gives foreign investors the ability to practice their legal professional activity within a clear regulatory framework, and makes it easier for them to benefit from the expertise of the Saudi partner who possesses knowledge of the local environment and enhances the chances of success and expansion within the Saudi market.

To obtain a professional license with a Saudi partner, applicants must meet specific requirements, including:

Submit an electronic application through the Ministry of Human Resources or Ministry of Investment platform.
Providing commercial records, incorporation documents for foreign companies, or the investor’s commercial registry.
Attach the identity of the Saudi partner and the approved professional registration certificate (if any).
Signing a legally documented partnership agreement that explains the rights and obligations of each party.
Submitting the professional qualifications required to obtain a license (experience certificates, Kingdom accreditation, professional licenses).
Pay the required regulatory fees.

The importance of the service of issuing a professional license with a Saudi partner:

Facilitating the entry of foreigners to practice professional professions in Saudi Arabia while ensuring the presence of an approved local partner.
Enhancing cooperation between foreign and local investors, which creates job opportunities and develops local skills.
Raising the quality of professional services through the exchange of experiences and technologies.
Supporting Saudi Vision 2030 by encouraging economic diversification and investment in professional sectors.
Increasing the confidence of customers and government agencies in the presence of accredited local partners who support the activity.
Improving opportunities to obtain government contracts and tenders that may require Saudi participation.


Difficulties related to obtaining or issuing a professional license to a Saudi partner:

The necessity of documenting legal agreements between partners to avoid future disputes.
The need to provide accredited professional certificates and qualifications that match the requirements of the relevant regulatory authorities.
Multiple technical procedures and requests, which may prolong the period of obtaining the license.
Possible delay in approvals due to complexity of procedures or lack of documents.
Special requirements for some professions may require additional licenses or professional examinations.
Technical challenges in uploading documents via electronic platforms.
requiredDocuments: The financial statements for the last fiscal year of the foreign company are approved and certified by the Saudi Embassy.
A copy of the commercial register of the participating foreign facility certified by the Saudi Embassy.
Photocopies of IDs for Saudi and resident partners and managers.
A copy of the professional license.
The establishment’s articles of incorporation are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
National address.
serviceConditions: The presence of a Saudi partner who is professionally licensed with an investment license, provided that his share in the company is not less than 25% according to the minimum limits specified by the type of activity.
The percentage of shares of non-professionals, whether Saudi or Gulf, holders of premium residency, or non-professional companies, should not exceed 30%.
The non-Saudi professional company participating or contributing to the company must have a distinguished reputation in the field of the liberal profession.

The non-Saudi professional company participating or contributing to the professional company must have one or more representatives who hold a license to practice the liberal profession in which the professional company is active from the competent authority in the non-Saudi professional company’s domicile or in his domicile, in every liberal profession in which the professional company participates or contributes.
The non-Saudi professional company must provide expertise and technical knowledge to the professional company and train Saudis in it.
Obtaining the approvals of the competent authority in the Kingdom in cases where this is necessary.
If one of the partners was previously licensed by the Ministry of Investment, this must be clarified when filling out the partners’ data while submitting the electronic application.
Determine the status of partners, whether professionals or non-professionals.
Payment of fees within 15 days.

The data (Saudi National ID / Privileged Residency / Iqama) must be entered, if one of the partners holds any of them, or the commercial registry data of the participating Saudi companies while submitting the electronic application.

Required data: (email address - Absher mobile number - phone number from the company’s country - identification of professional activities - identities of partners and managers - city in which the license is to be issued).

services.name: Issuing a license for regional headquarters
services.shortDescription: This service is provided to a foreign multinational company wishing to take the Kingdom as its regional headquarters by establishing an entity in accordance with the regulations of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia for the purposes of support, management and strategic direction of its branches and subsidiaries operating in the Middle East and North Africa region.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The regional headquarters license issuance service is an investment and regulatory service provided by the Saudi Ministry of Investment, and aims to enable foreign companies to establish regional offices in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia that represent them and manage their commercial operations within the region. Licensing the regional headquarters is an important step to enhance the presence of international companies in the Saudi market, as it allows the regional headquarters to manage and track the company’s business in a specific geographical area without engaging in direct commercial activity, such as sales or distribution, while complying with Saudi regulations and controls.


This service is granted to foreign companies that wish to:


Coordinating the work of its branches or agencies in the Gulf Cooperation Council countries or the Middle East.
Managing financial, administrative and marketing operations on behalf of the parent company.
Strengthening cooperation with local partners and facilitating expansion into local markets.
The regional headquarters is allowed to carry out management and support activities within legally defined limits.


The importance of the regional headquarters licensing service:


Enabling foreign companies to coordinate their business and expand their presence in the region easily and flexibly.
Supporting the transfer of expertise and knowledge between regional headquarters and the parent company to enhance operational effectiveness.
Enhancing trust with local partners and government agencies through the presence of an official, accredited regional office.
Facilitating the central management of regional operations to achieve better results against established regulatory requirements.
Reducing costs and efforts compared to establishing independent branches in each country in the region.
Keeping pace with Saudi Vision 2030 in attracting global investments and providing an advanced work environment.
Difficulties related to obtaining or issuing a regional headquarters license:


Strict requirements for notarization and translation of documents, which may extend processing times.
The need for the approval of the parent company’s Board of Directors and the provision of official records proving the decision to establish the regional office.
Clearly defining the scope of the office’s work and its geographical boundaries, which may require careful legal and technical review.
Complex administrative procedures that include multiple reviews between the Ministry of Investment and other concerned parties.
The possibility of rejecting the application if it is found that the financial or legal criteria are not met.
Challenges in coordination with government agencies to obtain all necessary approvals in a timely manner.
Technical problems in submitting the application electronically or uploading the required attachments.
requiredDocuments: Submit at least two commercial registrations issued in two different countries that do not include the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, or a register of the applying company certified by the Saudi embassy in the two countries concerned.
The financial statements for the last fiscal year of the foreign company requesting a license are certified by the Saudi embassy in the country concerned.
The facility’s articles of incorporation are certified by a Saudi embassy.
National address.
Photocopies of IDs for Saudi and resident partners and managers.
serviceConditions: The parent company must be licensed in two different countries, with the exception of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the country in which the main headquarters is located, either through subsidiaries or branches.
The regional headquarters must be established as an entity independent of other multinational company entities in the Kingdom, whether as a company or as a foreign company branch.
The regional headquarters does not directly engage in activities that generate commercial revenues other than regional headquarters licensing activities.
The regional headquarters must begin implementing the mandatory activities within 6 months from the date of granting the license.

The regional headquarters must begin implementing at least 3 optional activities within one year from the date of granting the license, provided that the applicant selects the optional activities during the submission of the license application.

Regional headquarters employees involved in mandatory activities must have relevant skills and knowledge acquired at the headquarters or another regional headquarters of the parent company, with at least 3 of them being at the CEO level and the vice president level.
Commitment to employ at least 15 full-time employees to implement the activities of the regional headquarters within one year from the date of granting the license, including senior executives.

The Ministry of Investment has the right to cancel the regional headquarters license in any of the following cases: (failure to undertake mandatory or optional regional headquarters licensing activities or employ the minimum number of employees during the specified time period / stop operating one of the mandatory activities or stop operating at least three optional activities / stop fulfilling any of the conditions for licensing the regional headquarters by the regional headquarters or the parent company / failure to comply with the restrictions and conditions for the investment license issued by the Ministry of Investment).
Payment of fees within 15 days.

Required data: (email address - Absher mobile number - phone number from the company’s country - regional activities - identities of partners and managers - city in which the license is to be issued).

services.name: Amending shares, exit or entry of other partners, or merging companies.
services.shortDescription: This service is provided to companies that wish to amend shares between partners or the entry or exit of other partners according to a documented agreement between them, or establishments licensed by the Ministry of Investment that wish to merge into another existing company of its kind or of another type, or to combine two or more companies to establish a new company.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of amending shares, exiting or entering other partners, or merging companies is one of the most important investment and regulatory services provided by the Ministry of Commerce and the Ministry of Investment in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, as it enables companies of all types to adjust the ownership structure or partners in line with changes in the administrative or investment reality of the company. This service includes a number of vital regulatory procedures:

Modifying the proportions or value of shares between current partners for any regulatory or investment reason.
Exit of a partner (selling his share or relinquishing it in whole or in part), with the process officially documented and the remaining shares transferred to the rest of the partners or new parties.
The entry of new partners, whether individuals or companies, with the determination of their shares in the capital after the approval of the competent authorities and documentation of the new entries.
Merging two or more companies into one entity, with the transfer of assets and legal and administrative obligations to the new or merged entity, in accordance with the approved merger regulations.

The importance of the service of amending shares, exit or entry of other partners, or merging companies

Providing flexibility in restructuring companies and distributing ownership according to operational and investment variables.
Facilitating investment or exit in legally regulated ways, in a way that supports the Saudi business environment.
Enabling the attraction of new investors or financial institutions by facilitating entry procedures as official partners.
Protecting all partners with their rights and documenting all operations officially with the competent authorities.
Support growth plans or strategic transformation through mergers or reconfigurations.
Enhancing transparency and corporate governance in accordance with the highest international standards.

Difficulties related to implementing the service

Multiple documents and regulatory requirements, such as official ratifications of partners’ minutes and agreements.
The partners’ entry or exit procedures need to be carefully documented by a notary public or chambers of commerce.
The possibility of delaying procedures if any conflict occurs between partners or incomplete supporting documents.
Financial and legal complications when evaluating shares or rights of partners in the event of a merger or exit.
Arranging financial obligations and debts when disposing of shares or merging companies, which requires careful review.
Regulatory dependencies such as updating data of other related government agencies (Zakat, insurance, municipal licenses...).
Technical difficulties in the electronic platform when uploading or amending contracts and documents.
requiredDocuments: Documents of the new partner(s) in the event that partners enter a new company or a new investor.

In the event of merging companies: a letter/decision from the partners for each company to merge, specifying the number of shares or shares belonging to each company in the capital of the merging company or the company emerging from the merger, certified by the Chamber of Commerce.
National address.
Photocopies of IDs for Saudi and resident partners and managers.
A copy of the investment license.
serviceConditions: The total percentage of Saudi partner(s) and capital agrees according to the minimum limits specified by the type of activity in the department.

The facility is committed to the procedures for amending the partners’ decision and registering the commercial registry within 30 days from the date of amending the investment license. In the event of a merger of companies, this will be done within 60 days. If this is not done, the Ministry of Investment has the right to return the license data to the previous status.
If one of the partners is previously licensed by the Ministry of Investment, this must be clarified when filling out the partners’ data during the application submission.

In the event of a merger of companies, each company party to the merger must issue a merger decision to amend the contract of incorporation of that company or its articles of association. The merger must be done for foreign legal entities only, and it is necessary to address the investment license status of the merged company immediately after approving the merger by submitting a cancellation request. In the event that the type of license for the merging and merging company is different, the license of the merged company is maintained as a branch of the merging company.
Required data: (email - Absher mobile number - identities of partners and managers).

services.name: Amending ownership by replacing heirs
services.shortDescription: This service is provided to establishments that have a license from the Ministry of Investment, and one of the partners has died and wishes to adjust the shares for the heirs.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of amending ownership by replacing heirs is an integrated regulatory procedure provided by the Saudi Ministry of Commerce in cooperation with the Ministry of Investment and other parties. It is used upon the death of one of the partners or owners in a commercial establishment (institution or company), whereby the regular heirs must take formal steps to transfer ownership and distribute shares/shares according to the heirs’ inventory instrument, in accordance with Sharia and the commercial system. The service is necessary to ensure the continuation of business activity in a legal manner and to document the rights of heirs in commercial records.

The service mechanism depends on the heirs or their representatives submitting a request to amend the commercial register electronically through the Ministry of Commerce platform, attached to the instrument listing the legal heirs, the decisions of the partners or the minutes of the associations, and documenting all the regulatory data for the distribution of shares or the transfer of ownership in whole or in part. The facility is obligated to complete these procedures within a specific period, usually (three months from the death announcement). Otherwise, the Ministry is legally authorized to cancel the commercial register or comment on it until the regulatory situation is corrected.

The importance of the property modification service by replacing heirs

Protecting the rights of the heirs and ensuring that they receive their regular share of the company or institution’s profits in accordance with the legal inventory deed and Sharia.
Maintaining the continuity of business activity without legal disruptions due to the death of one of the partners or owners.
Enabling the heirs to make decisions regarding the future of the enterprise, whether continuing, selling, or transferring to another type of business entity.
Full compliance with Saudi regulations and avoiding cancellation or suspension of the commercial register and loss of commercial assets.
Updating the data of all government and private entities related to ownership, partnership, and financial obligations.

Difficulties related to implementing the service

It is necessary to have a legal and complete heir inventory instrument, and have it documented electronically before proceeding with the transfer of ownership.
The need for the heirs to agree among themselves on the distribution of shares, and to document the agreement and obtain the issuance of official decisions from the competent authorities or the General Assembly.
Formal notarization procedures often require review by a notary public or personal status court and necessary authentication of documents.
The possibility of temporarily halting some government or banking services until the ownership change in all relevant entities is completed.
Conflict of heirs, the presence of minors, or family disputes may hinder or complicate the rapid transfer of ownership.
The need to settle debts or settle financial disputes before distributing shares or disposing of business assets.
The process is linked to zakat, taxes, and social insurance obligations that must be settled before or during the ownership transfer process.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the passport and residence permit for foreigners.
A letter issued by the court or an accredited law firm stating the legal quorum of heirs and how to distribute it, specifying the percentages of the heirs’ shares in the establishment.
National address.
Photocopies of IDs for Saudi and resident partners and managers.
A copy of the investment license.
serviceConditions: The facility is committed to the procedures for amending the partners’ decision and registering the commercial registry within 30 days from the date of amending the investment license. If this is not done, the Ministry of Investment has the right to return the license data to the previous status.
Required data: (email - Absher mobile number - identities of partners and managers).

services.name: Opening branches or a marketing center
services.shortDescription: This service is provided to all establishments that have a license from the Ministry of Investment, and wish to open branches with the same activity, and to industrial establishments that wish to open centers to market their products.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of opening branches or a marketing center is a regulatory and investment service that allows Saudi or foreign companies registered in the Kingdom to expand their geographical presence by opening new branches or establishing official marketing centers in different regions of Saudi Arabia. This service enables companies to facilitate the access of products or services to customers and enhance their share in the local market. It is also considered an essential step to enhance competitiveness and provide after-sales services, technical support, or distribution on a broader scale.

As for foreign companies, it is also required to submit an officially licensed decision from the parent company to open the branch or marketing center in the Kingdom, including the data for the new branch in the company’s main registry, and appointing an authorized manager with a clarification of his legal powers. It is important to fulfill all legal conditions for the type of business activity, reserve the trade name for the new branch, and submit notarized documents such as the commercial registry, incorporation contracts, and approved resolutions.

The importance of opening branches or a marketing center

Expanding the company's geographical presence and facilitating the access of products or services to a larger segment of customers.
Increasing operational efficiency and customer service by providing direct and effective communication points and after-sales services.
Enhancing opportunities for growth and commercial expansion while keeping pace with local market requirements and demand changes.
Supporting recruitment and employment plans by creating jobs within new branches or marketing centers.
Enabling the development of local relationships with partners and suppliers through effective presence in targeted cities and regions.

Difficulties related to opening branches or a marketing center

The number of entities and licenses required for each new branch, such as the municipality, labor, zakat, and insurance, increases procedures and regulatory supervision.
The need to document all contracts and decisions with official bodies, while following up on legal and accounting obligations.
Restricting some sectoral activities with prior approvals and conditions on the location, area, or engineering design of the headquarters.
Relatively high establishment and documentation costs in addition to ongoing operating and management costs.
The possibility of facing technical or administrative obstacles when registering branch data in government systems or when appointing a new manager.
The commercial registry data must be constantly updated for each branch or marketing center upon expiration of the contract or change of activity.
Follow up on compliance with tax and Saudi regulations for all new branches and marketing centers.
requiredDocuments: Decision of the company's board of directors.
National address.
Photocopies of IDs for Saudi and resident partners and managers.
A copy of the investment license.
serviceConditions: Updating the facility’s government data in the Ministry of Investment system.

The facility is committed to the procedures for amending the partners’ decision and registering the commercial registry within 30 days from the date of amending the investment license. If this is not done, the Ministry of Investment has the right to return the license data to the previous status.
To open a marketing center, the facility must have begun the actual production of its products.
Required data: (email address - Absher mobile number - name of the branch or marketing center to be added - identities of partners and managers).

services.name: Closing branches or marketing centers
services.shortDescription: This service is provided to establishments that have branches or marketing centers licensed by the Ministry of Investment and wish to close them.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of closing branches or marketing centers is a regulatory and legal step that allows Saudi or foreign companies registered in the Kingdom to officially terminate the work of one of their branches or marketing centers, so that the branch or center becomes closed in the systems of the Ministry of Commerce and the rest of the approved authorities. This process requires specific procedures in accordance with the Saudi corporate system to ensure the protection of the rights of partners and creditors and to organize the legal liquidation of assets and obligations of the branch or center.

The procedures begin with the issuance of an official decision by the partners or the company’s board of directors to close the branch or marketing center, and the decision must be documented according to legal requirements. After that, an electronic application is submitted through the Ministry of Commerce platform, explaining the reasons for the closure and attaching the necessary documents.

You must ensure that all licenses associated with the branch with government agencies (municipality, Zakat Authority, Social Insurance, etc.) are cancelled, and the branch’s bank accounts are closed. After completing the steps and liquidations, the Ministry of Commerce issues the final delisting decision for the branch or center in the electronic system. Thus, the branch loses its legal and commercial status and the company can focus on managing its main business or its other branches.

The importance of closing branches or marketing centers:

Organizing the termination of suspended activities and ensuring that unmanaged receivables or financial obligations do not continue.
Protect the rights of creditors, customers and employees in the closed branch through clear formal liquidation procedures.
Enabling the company to reallocate resources and focus on active and profitable branches or activities.
Updating company data in all government agencies to avoid regulatory problems later.

Difficulties and risks related to closing branches or marketing centers:

Multiple procedures between different government agencies such as the Ministry of Commerce, the Municipality, Zakat, Insurance, and others.
The need to carefully control the liquidation of assets and financial and legal settlements with workers and creditors.
The possibility of facing disputes with customers, employees or suppliers in the process of liquidation or closure.
Longer time consumption if the branch has large commitments or existing long-term contracts.
Additional procedures for the official publication of the liquidation are sometimes required, especially in large companies or economically influential branches.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the establishment’s commercial register.
National address.
Photocopies of IDs for Saudi and resident partners and managers.
A copy of the investment license.
serviceConditions: Updating the facility’s government data in the Ministry of Investment system.
The facility is obligated to cancel the branch’s commercial register before applying for the service.

The facility is committed to the procedures for amending the partners’ decision and registering the commercial registry within 30 days from the date of amending the investment license. If this is not done, the Ministry of Investment has the right to return the license data to the previous status.

services.name: Capital reduction
services.shortDescription: This service is provided to establishments licensed by the Ministry of Investment that wish to reduce the capital or total financing for industrial licensing.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription: We submit a request to establishments licensed by the Ministry of Investment that wish to reduce the capital or total funding for industrial licensing
requiredDocuments: Determine the capital to be added.
National address
Attach a copy of the investment license.
National ID or resident ID if the license holder is a resident.
serviceConditions: The total ratio of the Saudi partner/partners and the capital agrees according to the minimum limits specified by the type of activity in the department

The facility is committed to the procedures for amending the partners’ decision and registering the commercial registry within 60 days from the date of amending the investment license. If this is not done, the Ministry of Investment has the right to return the license data to the previous status.
After submitting the application to the competent government department within 2 working days for approval, unless there are comments

services.name: Increase capital
services.shortDescription: This service is provided to establishments licensed by the Ministry of Investment that wish to increase capital or total financing for industrial licensing.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription: We submit an application for establishments licensed by the Ministry of Investment that wish to increase capital, or total financing for industrial licensing.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the investment license.
Required data: (Absher mobile number, email, national address, national ID or resident ID if the license holder is a resident).
serviceConditions: The facility is committed to the procedures for amending the partners’ decision and registering the commercial registry within 30 days from the date of amending the investment license. If this is not done, the Ministry of Investment has the right to return the license data to the previous status.
Determine the capital to be added.

services.name: Updating the establishment’s contact information
services.shortDescription: This service aims to provide the Ministry of Investment with the information of the Director General and representatives of the establishment, and to designate a delegate with the Ministry to receive notices, notifications, decisions, and the like. As well as submitting requests for the facility to the Ministry.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription: This service aims to provide the Ministry of Investment with the information of the Director General and representatives of the establishment, and to designate a delegate with the Ministry to receive notices, notifications, decisions, and the like. As well as submitting requests for the facility to the Ministry.
requiredDocuments: Attach a copy of the national ID or residence permit of the General Manager.
Specify an approved national address and email address for the facility.
A copy or national ID number of a representative of the establishment at the Ministry of Investment.
serviceConditions: 1- This service excludes establishments licensed for 6 months or less.
2- In the event of any change in the establishment’s contact information, the information on the website must be updated in accordance with this service
3- After submitting the application to the competent government department within 1 working day for approval.

services.name: Updating government data
services.shortDescription: It is a service that aims to reach the “integrated electronic service” by updating the government data of the investment facility licensed by the Ministry of Investment to achieve a reliable and updated government information base by directly linking to the “Yesser” system without the need to request attachment of government documents from the facility.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
It is a service that aims to reach the “integrated electronic service” by updating government data for the investment facility licensed by the Ministry of Investment.
To achieve a reliable and updated government information base by directly linking to the “Yesser” system without the need to request attachment of government documents from the facility.
requiredDocuments: National address
Attach a copy of the investment license.
Identities of Saudi and resident partners and managers.
Attach a copy of the commercial register.
serviceConditions: 1- Updating only once is mandatory for all licensed establishments that did not provide the Ministry of Investment with this data previously and two years or more have passed since their license was issued.
2- After submitting the application to the competent government department within 3 working days for approval

services.name: Amending the investment license
services.shortDescription: A service through which establishments submit a request to amend an investment license.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription: We submit a request to establishments to amend an investment license.
requiredDocuments: Determine the type of modification required.
A copy of the investment license, articles of incorporation, and commercial register.
Required data: (email, national address, identity of Saudi and resident partners and managers).
serviceConditions: After submitting the application to the relevant government department within 2 working days for approval.

Fees: 3000 riyals (if the amendment is related to partners or activities)
1500 riyals (if the amendment is in other data).

services.name: Renewal of investment license
services.shortDescription: This service is provided to all licenses issued by the Ministry of Investment and those wishing to renew their investment license.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The investment license renewal service is an electronic service provided by the Saudi Ministry of Investment, which enables foreign investors or companies with foreign ownership in the Kingdom to extend the validity of licenses to practice their investment activities after the expiry of their statutory period. The service is in the interest of formal legal compliance and business continuity, while ensuring that you benefit from the benefits of the Saudi investment environment without interruption.

Steps to renew the investment license:

Submit a renewal application electronically through the Ministry of Investment portal (misa.gov.sa) by selecting the “Renewal of the Investment Authority License” service.
Fill out the renewal form, update the previously installed activity data, and add any new amendments (if any).
Attach the required documents, usually including:
- An updated copy of the commercial register.
- A copy of the valid or expired investment license.
- Audited financial statements for the most recent fiscal year.
Any supporting documents required depending on the activity.
Pay the renewal fees electronically (the annual fees usually amount to about 62 thousand Saudi riyals, but may vary depending on the activity and the number of renewal years).
Follow up on the status of the application and wait for the Ministry’s approval, which sends an acceptance notice or completion request via email or the portal.

The most important conditions and requirements:

Submit a renewal application at least 60 days before the expiration of the license period.
Ensuring the accuracy of financial and legal data and their compliance with investment conditions approved by the Ministry.
Updating company data, ownership structure, and any changes in management or activity within licensing information.
Pay all government fees and financial obligations, while maintaining payment records and official documents.
Some activities/sectors require additional documents depending on the type of activity or specific license (such as licenses for the environment, health sector, or media...).

The importance of the investment license renewal service:

Ensuring the continuity of business activity legally without interruption or violations.
Continuously benefiting from the advantages of foreign investment and the eligibility to deal with Saudi authorities and banks.
Protecting the rights of investors and partners by renewing the license officially approved by the Saudi authorities.
Full compliance with Saudi regulations and avoiding penalties, delisting or suspension.

Difficulties and challenges in renewing the investment license:

The multiplicity of documents and requirements for some activities, and the possible need to translate and authenticate foreign documents.
High annual fees for some investment activities compared to local licenses.
Delay in issuing approval in the event of a lack of documents or there are fundamental amendments to the activity or partners.
It is necessary to update all company data registered in government systems and synchronize it with renewal.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the commercial register.
Photo of the national address.
A copy of the investment license.
A valid zakat and income certificate.
A copy of the national ID or resident ID
Required data: (ID number - Absher mobile number - email).
serviceConditions: The fees must be paid within 15 days.
Investment establishments requesting renewal must commit to updating and approving their data with the Ministry of Investment annually.
Before applying for the service, you must ensure that the zakat and income certificate is valid.
It is necessary to attach a valid zakat certificate if the data does not appear due to a technical problem during the application submission.

services.name: Issuing a 100% foreign commercial investment license
services.shortDescription: The service is provided to 100% foreign establishments that wish to engage in wholesale and retail trade or e-commerce activity in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
Introduction to the service of issuing a 100% foreign commercial investment license 


It is a service provided by the Saudi Ministry of Investment that enables foreign investors to establish companies with 100% foreign ownership within the commercial sectors permitted in the Kingdom. This license allows the practice of wholesale, retail and e-commerce activities legally and officially in the Saudi market without the need for a local partner. 


Steps for issuing a 100% foreign commercial investment license


Submit a license application through the Ministry of Investment platform, along with submitting the required legal documents such as the parent company’s commercial register, certified and translated, the latest financial statements, and the articles of incorporation.
Verify that the investment activity meets the conditions and ensure that it is not included among the activities prohibited for foreign investment.
Pay the fees for issuing the license, which vary according to the type of activity and nature of work.
Issuing the license after official approval, after which the procedures for establishing the company and registering it in the Ministry of Commerce can be completed, and files for the entity can be opened in government agencies such as zakat, taxes, and social insurance.
Obtaining work visas for foreign managers and employees as needed and in accordance with applicable regulations.


The importance of a 100% foreign commercial investment license


Enabling foreign investors to enter the Saudi market with full ownership benefits, without the need for a Saudi partner.
Supporting the diversification of the Saudi economy and attracting foreign direct investment in the trade and services sector.
Providing legal protection and compliance with Saudi regulations, which enhances investors’ confidence in the market.
Facilitating recruitment and recruitment processes through easy visas for foreign managers and employees.
Opening business expansion opportunities within an organized environment compatible with the Kingdom’s Vision 2030.


Basic conditions and procedures


The minimum capital required to practice wholesale and retail activity is 30 million Saudi riyals.
All documents and certificates must be authenticated and certified according to the system by the Saudi Embassy in the countries of origin.
There are no final judicial rulings against the license applicant in cases related to investment or commercial activity.
Commitment to the permissible activities proposed by the Ministry of Investment and the permissible business guide.


Potential challenges and difficulties


Requirements for authentication and translation of parent company documents, which may increase the time required to issue a license.
Higher fees and initial investments than some local investments.
Careful assessment of financial solvency and investment plans to ensure that the application matches the standards.
Similar procedures apply to entities with full foreign ownership that require coordination with several government agencies.
requiredDocuments: The financial statements for the last fiscal year of the three regional companies are approved and certified by the Saudi Embassy.
A copy of the commercial records of the establishment participating in at least 3 regional or global markets certified by the Saudi Embassy.
The incorporation contracts for the three regional companies were certified by a Saudi embassy.
National address.
serviceConditions: Payment of fees within 15 days.
. This service is limited to foreign companies that have a presence in at least 3 regional or global markets.
The minimum capital required to practice wholesale and retail trade activity for 100% foreign companies is 30 million Saudi riyals.
If one of the partners was previously licensed by the Ministry of Investment, this must be clarified when filling out the partners’ data while submitting the electronic application.

The company must adhere to several standards during the first five years, as follows: 1- Commitment to the employment rates of Saudis as determined by the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development. 2- Develop and implement a program to appoint them to leadership positions in the first five years and ensure their continuation. 3- Commitment to training 30% of Saudi employees annually.

The company must commit to one of the following options: Option 1 - Commitment to invest an amount of no less than 300 million Saudi riyals, including 30 million riyals - the company’s cash capital over a period of 5 years starting from the date of obtaining the investment license. 2- Commitment to invest an amount of not less than 200 million Saudi riyals, including 30 million riyals - the company’s cash capital over a period of 5 years starting from the date of obtaining the investment license.

If the company is unable to complete one of the previous two options, it is committed to manufacturing 30% or more of the company’s locally distributed products in the Kingdom. In research and development programs, 5% or more of total sales to establish research and development programs in the Kingdom. In logistics and distribution services, establishing a center to provide these services and provide after-sales services.
The company's capital must not be less than 30,000,000 Saudi riyals.
Required data: Email - Absher mobile number - Phone number from the company’s country - Commercial activities - Identities of partners and residents - City in which the license is requested)

services.name: Issuing other licenses in land transport
services.shortDescription: The service is provided to foreign establishments that wish to practice land transportation activities in the Kingdom at a rate of 100%, in the following activities: - “public - private” fare - “school - tourist - teachers - materials” transportation - transporting pilgrims and visitors from inside and outside the Kingdom - transporting passengers by buses outside the Kingdom - directing taxis - renting cars - trucks - buses - car rental brokers - shipping brokers

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The other land transport licensing service is a group of regulatory services provided by the General Transport Authority in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and aims to regulate various types of land transport activities other than public bus transport within cities. These licenses include areas such as:


Private transportation and school buses,
Express and freight transport (heavy transport and commercial transport)
Refrigeration trucks.
Transporting passengers via taxis and e-taxis.
Commercial transportation drivers license.
Licensing of transport vehicles and their safety and operating requirements.


Investors or companies wishing to start a specific land transport activity can apply for the appropriate license through the General Authority for Transport platform, as the licensing process requires:


Providing commercial and financial registration documents to the investor or company.
Fill out the license application form for the desired activity.
Availability of safety standards and technical inspection for vehicles used in the activity.
Proof of compliance with the laws regulating road safety and transportation procedures in Saudi Arabia.
Pay the prescribed fees for each type of license.


The importance of the other licensing service in land transport:


Organizing various land transportation activities in a way that ensures safety and operational efficiency.
Supporting the diversification of transport services and ensuring the safety of passengers and goods within a strict regulatory framework.
Facilitating the entry of investors into vital transportation sectors such as private transportation, school transportation, and taxi services.
Enhancing transport companies’ compliance with local and international laws to ensure quality services.
Supporting nationalization programs and providing job opportunities for Saudis in the land transportation sector.
Providing standardized technical requirements and operating conditions that facilitate performance monitoring and service improvement.
Challenges and difficulties in issuing other licenses in land transport:


High requirements for safety and technical inspection of vehicles including specific equipment for each type of transport.
Accurate procedures to verify drivers, ensure their qualifications, and obtain commercial transportation licenses.
Strict adherence to traffic regulations and national and international transport laws.
Coordination with a number of government agencies such as Traffic, the Ministry of Municipal Affairs, and Zakat and Taxes.
Accurate financial and administrative evaluation to ensure the solvency of companies and their ability to manage operations.
Potential technical complications in tracking and updating license data electronically via the platform.
High documentation requirements and strict legal procedures, especially in licenses for sensitive types of transportation such as school or refrigeration.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the establishment’s commercial register certified by the Saudi Embassy.
The financial statements for the last three financial years of the foreign company requesting a license are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
Submitting a business plan to open the company’s branches and locations mentioned in the economic feasibility study.
The establishment’s articles of incorporation are certified by the Saudi Embassy.
National address.
Photocopies of IDs for Saudi and resident partners and managers.
serviceConditions: He must be present in three international markets for the same activity he wishes to practice.

He must have been practicing the same activity for more than 10 years in his country of origin, and the establishment must be one of the establishments that provides a distinguished quality service in the field of land transportation after the Public Transport Authority has evaluated it.

The total percentage of the Saudi partner or partners and the capital must agree that the capital in practicing the activity that the company wishes to practice should not be less than 10 million riyals, according to the minimum specified for the type of activity.
Commitment to implementing all obligations regulating the activity, including the standards and percentages of Emiratisation approved by the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development.
The facility that relies entirely on providing the service through technical channels is excluded from applying the above requirements
If the rental activity is “cars - trucks - buses - car rental brokers - freight brokers” without a driver, the establishment is excluded from applying the above requirements.

Commitment to having a website that includes all the details related to the services provided by the company, in addition to allocating and establishing a call center and channels to receive complaints from workers, employers, or customers.

The data (Saudi National ID / Privileged Residency / Iqama) must be entered, if one of the partners holds any of them, or the commercial registry data of the participating Saudi companies while submitting the electronic application.
Payment of fees within 15 days
The capital must not be less than 10,000,000 Saudi riyals.
Required data: Email - Absher mobile number - Phone number from the company’s country - Land transport activities - Identities of partners and residents - City in which the license is to be issued)

services.name: Issuing a temporary certificate for submitting to bids for government projects
services.shortDescription: A service provided to establishments that wish to be qualified by the Ministry of Investment to apply for government project competitions. Apply for a temporary certificate that enables them to submit to bids for government projects in accordance with Cabinet Resolution No. 4.

serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The service of issuing a temporary certificate for submitting to bids for government projects is an electronic service provided by government agencies in Saudi Arabia. It allows contractors and companies wishing to participate in government bids and tenders to obtain a temporary certificate proving their initial eligibility and granting them the authority to apply and participate in the competition procedures for projects. This certification is an essential step to ensure the credibility of companies and the transparency of competitions, as well as regulating entry and regulatory requirements for projects funded by the state or public sector entities.

The application is submitted entirely electronically via the central bidding platform or the electronic portal of the relevant government agency, where the applicant attaches a set of documents proving technical and financial merit, such as:

Valid commercial registration.
Professional or investment permits and licensing.
Recent financial statements.
Certificates of previous experience in the field of related projects.
Any additional documents required by the entity supervising the bid.


The importance of the service of issuing a temporary certificate for submitting to bids for government projects:

Enabling companies to participate in important government competitions without waiting for lengthy verification procedures.
Accelerating the application procedures and initial selection of contractors or suppliers according to professional standards.
Raising the level of transparency and competition in the government projects market while ensuring the seriousness of applicants.
Ensuring the achievement of systemic credibility and compliance with the conditions of the implementing agencies.
Facilitate tracking of bid requests and official participation through a unified central hub.
Supporting the achievement of the goals of Saudi Vision 2030 in enhancing transparency and improving the efficiency of the construction and contracting sector.

Challenges related to issuing a temporary certificate:

Multiple technical and financial requirements required of companies, which may take time to verify.
It is necessary to periodically update documents and records to ensure the continued validity of the certificate.
The possibility of rejecting the application or suspending the certificate in the event of a deficiency or non-compliance in the documents submitted.
Potential administrative complications in procedures, especially between different government agencies or when bidding conditions differ.
Great pressure on the certification system in seasons that witness the launch of many major tenders.
Strict controls require companies to adhere to the quality and safety standards of government projects.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the establishment’s commercial register in its home country certified by the Saudi embassy.
. Submit financial statements for the last three years, prepared by an internationally accredited chartered accounting firm, and the audit report should be free of notes.

Providing completion certificates for three projects that the company had previously implemented as a main contractor during the past seven years, the value of each of which exceeds a minimum value of the projects required for contractors classified as first class approved by the Ministry of Municipal and Rural Affairs in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
Submit a copy of a valid quality management certificate - ISO 9001.
Submit a copy of the ISO 14001 Environmental Management Certificate or valid alternative certificates.
Submit a copy of a valid OSHAS 18001 occupational safety certificate.
Submitting an acknowledgment confirming the reliability of the information provided that the signatory is authorized to make decisions, indicating the means of communication.
The national address of the facility
The establishment contract is certified by the Saudi Embassy.
serviceConditions: The duration of the certificate is only one year.
This certificate does not authorize the establishment to issue a commercial register.

If the government contract is awarded to the facility, it does not have the right to implement the contract until it obtains a service license or a temporary license to implement a single government or semi-government contract.
The company’s total revenues for the financial statements according to the three years preceding the certificate request must be more than 500,000,000 Saudi riyals.
The company must have achieved profits in three financial statements from the last five financial years prior to requesting the temporary certificate.
All required documents are certified by the Saudi embassy in the country in which the certificate applicant is located.
Required data: (email - Absher mobile number - phone number in the company’s country - government project bids - identities of Saudi and resident partners and managers)

categoryName: Ministry of Environment, Water and Agriculture

services.name: License to drill a new well
services.shortDescription: A service that enables beneficiaries (individuals - business sectors - government agencies) to submit a request for a license to drill an alternative well (tubular or manual).

serviceDetails.ministryInfo: Ministry of Environment Water and Agriculture
serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The new well drilling license service is one of the basic services provided by the Ministry of Environment, Water and Agriculture in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia through the electronic services system “Nama Portal”. The service targets all categories, whether individuals, companies, government agencies, or agricultural investors, and allows them to obtain an official permit to dig a new or replacement well, whether the well is manual or tubular. This service is considered necessary to regulate groundwater extraction, achieve sustainability of water resources, protect property rights, and ensure drilling only in permitted places.

The request to obtain a drilling license is submitted electronically through the Ministry’s platform, where the applicant must fulfill a set of regulatory conditions and requirements, such as:

Fill out a license application form that includes data on the geographical location of the well, the purpose of drilling, and specifying the use (agricultural, industrial, drinking, gas station, municipal, surveillance...).
Attach a valid title deed or notarized lease contract with written approval from the leasing party to dig the well.
Attach an approved engineering sketch showing the location of the well and the total area, in addition to drilling coordinates.
If the applicant is an agent or wishes to dig on behalf of heirs, a legal power of attorney and a deed of heirs must be attached.
The presence of an agricultural record developed when digging for agricultural purposes.
In the event of digging an alternative well/deepening/cleaning well, a previous license or a disclosure or correction certificate for the old site is required.
A letter specifying the daily water requirement and purpose of use must be attached.

After submitting the application and studying it technically by a specialized committee at the Ministry, approval is issued if all requirements are met and is sent to the beneficiary to begin excavation work through qualified contractors. Implementation is followed up and compliance with technical conditions and specifications is ensured during visits by oversight teams. Obtaining the license requires paying the prescribed fees, which vary according to the type of application, and an information sign must be placed at the site showing the license number and the depth of the well.

The importance of the new well drilling license service
Regulating groundwater extraction operations according to water resources sustainability controls.
Protecting property and ensuring that drilling is not carried out in prohibited areas or overlapping with service networks.
Supporting the development of agricultural and industrial projects and providing safe and sustainable water sources.
Ensuring the beneficiary’s compliance with technical conditions, and facilitating government oversight of drilling.

Challenges and difficulties

It is necessary to complete all documents and clarify the location accurately to avoid rejection of the application.
Facing drilling restrictions in some areas and reserves.
Coordination with oversight and follow-up authorities and technical inspection procedures.
Updating licenses or correcting the well’s status in the event of a change in use or deepening/cleaning the existing well.
requiredDocuments: Property title deed, lease contract, or ministerial decision.

A building permit from the municipality and a photo showing that the land is fenced in any way for the purposes of washing and watering crops in residential lands located in the Arabian Shield and in the excluded area on the sedimentary shelf and in the cities of (Riyadh, Buraidah, Dammam, Al-Khobar, Dhahran, Jubail).<br>
A valid commercial register containing the activity of producing purified water for the purposes of adding drinkable things.

Permission from the Ministry of Municipalities and Housing to withdraw groundwater and drain it using the flood drainage network, allowing the license applicant to drain the water through the wastewater network or through tanks.<br>
Industrial license to establish a factory for the purpose of drilling a new well for bottled drinking water factories, ice factories, food factories, and juice factories.

A valid project establishment license from the competent authority for the purpose of drilling a new well for mining projects, crushers, block and brick factories, cement and ready-mixed concrete, and other factories.
serviceConditions: Do not drill the well until after obtaining the new well drilling license and the contractor’s approval.
Approving the conditions and controls for issuing licenses for groundwater sources (wells) and classifying their violations.
Approval from the Ministry of Transport and Logistics to drill a well for the purpose of drilling a new well for road construction projects.

services.name: License to drill a new well
services.shortDescription: A service that enables beneficiaries (individuals - business sectors - government agencies) to submit a request for a license to drill an alternative well (tubular or manual).

serviceDetails.ministryInfo: Ministry of Environment Water and Agriculture
serviceDetails.serviceDescription:
The new well drilling license service is one of the basic services provided by the Ministry of Environment, Water and Agriculture in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia through the electronic services system “Nama Portal”. The service targets all categories, whether individuals, companies, government agencies, or agricultural investors, and allows them to obtain an official permit to dig a new or replacement well, whether the well is manual or tubular. This service is considered necessary to regulate groundwater extraction, achieve sustainability of water resources, protect property rights, and ensure drilling only in permitted places.

The request to obtain a drilling license is submitted electronically through the Ministry’s platform, where the applicant must fulfill a set of regulatory conditions and requirements, such as:

Fill out a license application form that includes data on the geographical location of the well, the purpose of drilling, and specifying the use (agricultural, industrial, drinking, gas station, municipal, surveillance...).
Attach a valid title deed or notarized lease contract with written approval from the leasing party to dig the well.
Attach an approved engineering sketch showing the location of the well and the total area, in addition to drilling coordinates.
If the applicant is an agent or wishes to dig on behalf of heirs, a legal power of attorney and a deed of heirs must be attached.
The presence of an agricultural record developed when digging for agricultural purposes.
In the event of digging an alternative well/deepening/cleaning well, a previous license or a disclosure or correction certificate for the old site is required.
A letter specifying the daily water requirement and purpose of use must be attached.

After submitting the application and studying it technically by a specialized committee at the Ministry, approval is issued if all requirements are met and is sent to the beneficiary to begin excavation work through qualified contractors.
